Revision ID: 0002
Revises: 0001
Create Date: 2026-03-31

What this migration adds on top of the baseline DDL (0001):

CHECK CONSTRAINTS  (via batch_alter_table — SQLite requires full table recreation)
  users               ck_users_role
  commission_rules    ck_commission_rate_bounds, ck_commission_cycle
  settlement_runs     ck_settlement_status, ck_settlement_period
  settlement_disputes ck_dispute_status, ck_dispute_amount
  inventory_lots      ck_lot_costing_method, ck_lot_qty_nonneg
  inventory_transactions  ck_inv_txn_type, ck_inv_txn_adjustment_reason
  cost_layers         ck_cost_layer_qty, ck_cost_layer_cost
  avg_cost_snapshots  ck_avg_cost_nonneg, ck_avg_cost_qty
  cycle_count_lines   ck_cycle_count_variance_reason
  messages            ck_message_type, ck_message_single_target
  message_receipts    ck_receipt_status
  content_items       ck_content_type, ck_content_status
  content_versions    ck_cv_status, ck_cv_version_positive
  attachments         ck_attachment_mime, ck_attachment_size, ck_attachment_single_owner
  capture_templates   ck_template_status
  template_versions   ck_tv_status, ck_tv_version_positive
  template_migrations ck_tmig_from_positive, ck_tmig_forward_only
  admin_tickets       ck_ticket_type, ck_ticket_status
  audit_log           ck_audit_action_type

UNIQUE CONSTRAINTS
  avg_cost_snapshots  uix_avg_cost_sku_wh   (sku_id, warehouse_id)
  message_receipts    uix_receipt_msg_recipient  (message_id, recipient_id)

NEW INDEXES
  sessions            ix_sessions_user_id           (user_id)
  products            ix_products_price_usd         (price_usd)
  product_attributes  ix_product_attributes_product_id (product_id)
  product_tags        ix_product_tags_product_id    (product_id)
  cost_layers         ix_cost_layers_fifo           (sku_id, warehouse_id, received_at)
  cycle_counts        ix_cycle_counts_warehouse     (warehouse_id)
  cycle_count_lines   ix_ccl_cycle_count            (cycle_count_id)
  content_versions    ix_cv_content_id              (content_id)
  template_versions   ix_tv_template_id             (template_id)
  template_migrations ix_tmig_template_id           (template_id)
  settlement_runs     ix_settlement_community       (community_id)
  settlement_disputes ix_dispute_settlement         (settlement_id)
  commission_rules    ix_commission_community       (community_id)
  messages            ix_messages_expires_at        (expires_at)

EXPRESSION INDEX (NULL-safe lot uniqueness)
  inventory_lots      uix_lot_location
      ON inventory_lots(sku_id, warehouse_id, COALESCE(bin_id,''), COALESCE(lot_number,''))

SQLITE TRIGGERS
  trg_lot_costing_immutable     — prevents costing_method change after first transaction
  products_fts_ai / _au / _ad   — keeps products_fts FTS5 table in sync with products rows

DOWNGRADE
  Drops all triggers, indexes, and expression index.
  Uses batch_alter_table to recreate affected tables without the check/unique constraints.
"""
from alembic import op
import sqlalchemy as sa

revision = "0002"
down_revision = "0001"
branch_labels = None
depends_on = None

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# helpers
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

_BATCH_NAMING = {"ix": "ix_%(column_0_label)s", "uq": "uq_%(table_name)s_%(column_0_name)s"}


def _batch(table: str):
    """Return a context manager for batch_alter_table (SQLite constraint recreation)."""
    return op.batch_alter_table(table, schema=None)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# upgrade
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def upgrade():
    # ---- new plain indexes (always safe; no data movement needed) ----------

    op.create_index("ix_sessions_user_id", "sessions", ["user_id"])
    op.create_index("ix_products_price_usd", "products", ["price_usd"])
    op.create_index("ix_product_attributes_product_id", "product_attributes", ["product_id"])
    op.create_index("ix_product_tags_product_id", "product_tags", ["product_id"])
    op.create_index("ix_cost_layers_fifo", "cost_layers", ["sku_id", "warehouse_id", "received_at"])
    op.create_index("ix_cycle_counts_warehouse", "cycle_counts", ["warehouse_id"])
    op.create_index("ix_ccl_cycle_count", "cycle_count_lines", ["cycle_count_id"])
    op.create_index("ix_cv_content_id", "content_versions", ["content_id"])
    op.create_index("ix_tv_template_id", "template_versions", ["template_id"])
    op.create_index("ix_tmig_template_id", "template_migrations", ["template_id"])
    op.create_index("ix_settlement_community", "settlement_runs", ["community_id"])
    op.create_index("ix_dispute_settlement", "settlement_disputes", ["settlement_id"])
    op.create_index("ix_commission_community", "commission_rules", ["community_id"])
    op.create_index("ix_messages_expires_at", "messages", ["expires_at"])

    # ---- expression index: NULL-safe lot uniqueness -----------------------
    # COALESCE turns NULL bin_id / lot_number into '' so that two rows with the
    # same sku+warehouse+no-bin+no-lot-number are correctly treated as duplicates.
    op.execute(
        "CREATE UNIQUE INDEX uix_lot_location "
        "ON inventory_lots(sku_id, warehouse_id, COALESCE(bin_id,''), COALESCE(lot_number,''))"
    )

    # ---- CHECK constraints via batch_alter_table --------------------------
    # SQLite does not support ADD CONSTRAINT via ALTER TABLE; Alembic's
    # batch_alter_table handles this by recreating the table transparently.

    with _batch("users") as b:
        b.create_check_constraint(
            "ck_users_role",
            "role IN ('Administrator','Operations Manager','Moderator',"
            "'Group Leader','Staff','Member')",
        )

    with _batch("commission_rules") as b:
        b.create_check_constraint(
            "ck_commission_rate_bounds",
            "floor >= 0 AND ceiling <= 15 AND floor <= rate AND rate <= ceiling",
        )
        b.create_check_constraint(
            "ck_commission_cycle",
            "settlement_cycle IN ('weekly','biweekly')",
        )

    with _batch("settlement_runs") as b:
        b.create_check_constraint(
            "ck_settlement_status",
            "status IN ('pending','processing','completed','disputed','cancelled')",
        )
        b.create_check_constraint(
            "ck_settlement_period",
            "period_end >= period_start",
        )

    with _batch("settlement_disputes") as b:
        b.create_check_constraint(
            "ck_dispute_status",
            "status IN ('open','resolved','rejected')",
        )
        b.create_check_constraint("ck_dispute_amount", "disputed_amount >= 0")

    with _batch("inventory_lots") as b:
        b.create_check_constraint(
            "ck_lot_costing_method",
            "costing_method IN ('fifo','moving_average')",
        )
        b.create_check_constraint("ck_lot_qty_nonneg", "on_hand_qty >= 0")

    with _batch("inventory_transactions") as b:
        b.create_check_constraint(
            "ck_inv_txn_type",
            "type IN ('receipt','issue','transfer','adjustment')",
        )
        b.create_check_constraint(
            "ck_inv_txn_adjustment_reason",
            "type != 'adjustment' OR (reason IS NOT NULL AND reason != '')",
        )

    with _batch("cost_layers") as b:
        b.create_check_constraint("ck_cost_layer_qty", "quantity_remaining >= 0")
        b.create_check_constraint("ck_cost_layer_cost", "unit_cost_usd >= 0")

    with _batch("avg_cost_snapshots") as b:
        b.create_unique_constraint("uix_avg_cost_sku_wh", ["sku_id", "warehouse_id"])
        b.create_check_constraint("ck_avg_cost_nonneg", "avg_cost_usd >= 0")
        b.create_check_constraint("ck_avg_cost_qty", "on_hand_qty >= 0")

    with _batch("cycle_count_lines") as b:
        b.create_check_constraint(
            "ck_cycle_count_variance_reason",
            "variance = 0 OR (variance_reason IS NOT NULL AND variance_reason != '')",
        )

    with _batch("messages") as b:
        b.create_check_constraint(
            "ck_message_type",
            "type IN ('text','image_meta','file_meta','emoji','system')",
        )
        b.create_check_constraint(
            "ck_message_single_target",
            "NOT (recipient_id IS NOT NULL AND group_id IS NOT NULL)",
        )

    with _batch("message_receipts") as b:
        b.create_unique_constraint(
            "uix_receipt_msg_recipient", ["message_id", "recipient_id"]
        )
        b.create_check_constraint(
            "ck_receipt_status",
            "status IN ('sent','delivered','read')",
        )

    with _batch("content_items") as b:
        b.create_check_constraint(
            "ck_content_type",
            "type IN ('article','book','chapter')",
        )
        b.create_check_constraint(
            "ck_content_status",
            "status IN ('draft','published')",
        )

    with _batch("content_versions") as b:
        b.create_check_constraint(
            "ck_cv_status",
            "status IN ('draft','published')",
        )
        b.create_check_constraint("ck_cv_version_positive", "version >= 1")

    with _batch("attachments") as b:
        b.create_check_constraint(
            "ck_attachment_mime",
            "mime_type IN ('image/png','image/jpeg','application/pdf','text/plain','text/markdown')",
        )
        b.create_check_constraint(
            "ck_attachment_size",
            "size_bytes > 0 AND size_bytes <= 26214400",
        )
        b.create_check_constraint(
            "ck_attachment_single_owner",
            "(content_id IS NOT NULL) != (template_id IS NOT NULL)",
        )

    with _batch("capture_templates") as b:
        b.create_check_constraint(
            "ck_template_status",
            "status IN ('draft','published')",
        )

    with _batch("template_versions") as b:
        b.create_check_constraint(
            "ck_tv_status",
            "status IN ('draft','published')",
        )
        b.create_check_constraint("ck_tv_version_positive", "version >= 1")

    with _batch("template_migrations") as b:
        b.create_check_constraint("ck_tmig_from_positive", "from_version >= 1")
        b.create_check_constraint("ck_tmig_forward_only", "to_version > from_version")

    with _batch("admin_tickets") as b:
        b.create_check_constraint(
            "ck_ticket_type",
            "type IN ('moderation','report','other')",
        )
        b.create_check_constraint(
            "ck_ticket_status",
            "status IN ('open','in_progress','closed')",
        )

    with _batch("audit_log") as b:
        b.create_check_constraint(
            "ck_audit_action_type",
            "action_type IN ('settlement','moderation','inventory','auth','content','template')",
        )

    # ---- SQLite triggers ---------------------------------------------------

    # Costing method immutability (design.md §3.5, questions.md Q8):
    # Fires BEFORE UPDATE OF costing_method and aborts if any transaction already
    # references this lot.
    op.execute("""
        CREATE TRIGGER trg_lot_costing_immutable
        BEFORE UPDATE OF costing_method ON inventory_lots
        WHEN EXISTS (
            SELECT 1 FROM inventory_transactions WHERE lot_id = OLD.lot_id LIMIT 1
        )
        BEGIN
            SELECT RAISE(ABORT,
                'costing_method_locked: cannot change after transactions exist');
        END
    """)

    # FTS5 content-table sync triggers (design.md §3.4):
    # The products_fts virtual table uses content=products, so SQLite does NOT
    # automatically keep it in sync — these triggers are required.
    op.execute("""
        CREATE TRIGGER products_fts_ai
        AFTER INSERT ON products BEGIN
            INSERT INTO products_fts(rowid, name, brand, description)
            VALUES (new.rowid, new.name, new.brand, new.description);
        END
    """)

    op.execute("""
        CREATE TRIGGER products_fts_au
        AFTER UPDATE ON products BEGIN
            INSERT INTO products_fts(products_fts, rowid, name, brand, description)
            VALUES ('delete', old.rowid, old.name, old.brand, old.description);
            INSERT INTO products_fts(rowid, name, brand, description)
            VALUES (new.rowid, new.name, new.brand, new.description);
        END
    """)

    op.execute("""
        CREATE TRIGGER products_fts_ad
        AFTER DELETE ON products BEGIN
            INSERT INTO products_fts(products_fts, rowid, name, brand, description)
            VALUES ('delete', old.rowid, old.name, old.brand, old.description);
        END
    """)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# downgrade — reverses everything added above
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def downgrade():
    # Drop triggers first (no dependencies)
    op.execute("DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS trg_lot_costing_immutable")
    op.execute("DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS products_fts_ai")

    op.execute("DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS products_fts_ad")

    # Drop expression index
    op.execute("DROP INDEX IF EXISTS uix_lot_location")

    for ix in (
        "ix_sessions_user_id",
        "ix_products_price_usd",
        "ix_product_attributes_product_id",
        "ix_product_tags_product_id",
        "ix_cost_layers_fifo",

This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:

**Task 1** (completed): Scaffold a Flask + SQLAlchemy backend project for a "Neighborhood Commerce & Content Operations Management System". Requirements:
- Production-style structure: app factory, blueprints/routes, models, services, middleware, jobs, migrations, tests
- Include Dockerfile, docker-compose, requirements.txt, .env.example, startup scripts
- APIs versioned under `/api/v1`
- Do not implement deep business logic — focus on clean architecture and wiring
- Based on four docs: `docs/prompt.md`, `docs/questions.md`, `docs/api-spec.md`, `docs/design.md`

**Task 2** (in progress, nearly complete): Implement database schema and migrations properly. Must include:
- SQLAlchemy models + Alembic migrations for all 26 core entities listed
- Constraints: one active group leader per community, settlement idempotency key unique, soft delete fields, template/version uniqueness, immutability patterns from design/questions docs
- CHECK constraints, UNIQUE constraints, indexes, SQLite triggers

2. Key Technical Concepts:
- **Flask** app factory pattern (`create_app`) with blueprints registered under `/api/v1`
- **SQLAlchemy** ORM with custom TypeDecorators: `GUID` (stores UUID as TEXT), `EncryptedText` (Fernet-encrypted at rest)
- **Alembic** / Flask-Migrate for schema versioning; `batch_alter_table` required for CHECK constraints on SQLite (full table recreation)
- **SQLite** with WAL mode (`PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL`), foreign keys enabled, FTS5 virtual table for product search
- **Fernet** symmetric encryption (cryptography library) for `password_hash` and payout fields; key stored at `data/keys/secret.key`
- **bcrypt** (cost factor ≥ 12) for password hashing; hash stored Fernet-encrypted
- **APScheduler** `BackgroundScheduler` in-process for 5 background jobs
- **Flask-SocketIO** with eventlet for WebSocket/STOMP messaging
- **marshmallow** for input validation schemas
- **bleach** for Markdown/HTML sanitization in content service
- **Partial unique index** on `group_leader_bindings(community_id) WHERE active=1` — one active leader per community
- **COALESCE expression index** `uix_lot_location` for NULL-safe lot uniqueness in `inventory_lots`
- **Append-only audit_log** enforced by SQLite `BEFORE UPDATE/DELETE` triggers
- **FTS5 content-table sync triggers** (`products_fts_ai/au/ad`) — FTS5 with `content=products` does NOT auto-sync without triggers
- **Costing method immutability trigger** `trg_lot_costing_immutable` — prevents `costing_method` change after first transaction exists
- **Soft delete** pattern (`deleted_at` nullable datetime) on Users, Communities, ServiceAreas, Products, ContentItems, CaptureTemplates, Attachments
- **Correlation ID** (`X-Correlation-ID` header) propagated through Flask `g` context to all log entries
- Role hierarchy: Administrator > Operations Manager > Moderator > Group Leader > Staff > Member
- Row-level scoping for Group Leaders (own community only) enforced in service layer
- Session tokens: random 32-byte hex stored as SHA-256 hash (no JWT — offline deployment)
- Idempotency key unique constraint on `settlement_runs` prevents duplicate settlements
- Commission rate bounds: `0 ≤ floor ≤ rate ≤ ceiling ≤ 15` enforced at both DB (CHECK) and service layer
- `AdminTicket.created_by` is nullable — system-generated tickets (safety-stock alerts) have no user actor
- `migration 0001` → `migration 0002` chain via `down_revision`

3. Files and Code Sections:

**`repo/requirements.txt`**
- Defines all Python dependencies: Flask==3.0.3, Flask-SQLAlchemy==3.1.1, Flask-Migrate==4.0.7, Flask-SocketIO==5.3.6, bcrypt==4.1.3, cryptography==42.0.8, marshmallow==3.21.3, APScheduler==3.10.4, eventlet==0.36.1, bleach==6.1.0, pytest==8.2.2

**`repo/app/__init__.py`** — App factory
```python
def create_app(config_name: str | None = None) -> Flask:
    app = Flask(__name__)
    app.config.from_object(config_map.get(config_name, Config))
    init_fernet(app.config["FERNET_KEY_PATH"])
    db.init_app(app); migrate.init_app(app, db); socketio.init_app(app)
    # WAL mode + foreign keys via SQLAlchemy event listener
    register_middleware(app)
    _register_blueprints(app)
    register_error_handlers(app)
    if app.config.get("JOBS_ENABLED"):
        _start_scheduler(app)
    return app
```

```sql
CREATE TRIGGER trg_lot_costing_immutable
BEFORE UPDATE OF costing_method ON inventory_lots
WHEN EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM inventory_transactions WHERE lot_id = OLD.lot_id LIMIT 1)
BEGIN SELECT RAISE(ABORT, 'costing_method_locked: cannot change after transactions exist'); END

CREATE TRIGGER products_fts_ai AFTER INSERT ON products BEGIN
  INSERT INTO products_fts(rowid, name, brand, description) VALUES(new.rowid, new.name, new.brand, new.description);
END

CREATE TRIGGER products_fts_au AFTER UPDATE ON products BEGIN
  INSERT INTO products_fts(products_fts, rowid, name, brand, description) VALUES('delete', old.rowid, ...);
  INSERT INTO products_fts(rowid, ...) VALUES(new.rowid, ...);
END

CREATE TRIGGER products_fts_ad AFTER DELETE ON products BEGIN
  INSERT INTO products_fts(products_fts, rowid, ...) VALUES('delete', old.rowid, ...);
END
```
- Full downgrade: drops all triggers, indexes, then uses `batch_alter_table` to drop each constraint

4. Errors and Fixes:
- **`InventoryTransaction.notes` field reference error**: `inventory_service.py` used `notes=data.get("notes")` in a constructor call, but the model has no `notes` column (only `reference` and `reason`). Fixed by changing to `reference=data.get("notes")`.
- **`AdminTicket.created_by` nullable mismatch**: Model had `nullable=False` but migration had `nullable=True`, and background jobs create system tickets with no user. Fixed: changed model to `nullable=True` to align with migration and support system-generated tickets.
- **Missing FTS5 sync triggers**: The `products_fts` virtual table uses `content=products` which does NOT auto-sync — without `AFTER INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE` triggers on `products`, the FTS5 table would always be empty. Added three triggers in migration 0002.
- **Missing `products_fts` drop in 0001 downgrade**: The downgrade in `0001_initial_schema.py` drops `products` but never drops `products_fts` virtual table first, which would fail. This was identified but the fix was in progress when the summary was requested.

- **SQLite CHECK constraint addition**: SQLite does not support `ALTER TABLE ADD CONSTRAINT`. Solved using Alembic's `batch_alter_table` which recreates the table transparently (copy data → new table with constraint → swap → drop old).
- **Single attachment owner**: The `ck_attachment_single_owner` constraint `(content_id IS NOT NULL) != (template_id IS NOT NULL)` uses SQLite's boolean arithmetic (expressions evaluate to 0/1) to enforce XOR — exactly one of content_id or template_id must be set.

Perfect! I have successfully read all the requested files. Let me now provide you with a comprehensive summary organized by category:

---

## FULL FILE CONTENTS SUMMARY

### 1. **C:\TASK-105\docs\api-spec.md**

Complete API specification for the Neighborhood Commerce & Content Operations Management System. Key sections relevant to auth and users:

**Authentication & Users Endpoints:**
- `POST /auth/register` - Create account (username, password min 12 chars, role). Errors: 400 `password_too_short`, 409 `username_taken`
- `POST /auth/login` - Return Bearer token. Errors: 401 `invalid_credentials`, 423 `account_locked` (after 5 failures, 15-min lockout with `retry_after` timestamp)
- `POST /auth/logout` - Invalidate token. Returns 204
- `GET /users` - List users (Admin, Operations Manager only). Supports `role` filter, pagination
- `GET /users/{user_id}` - Get single user (Admin, Operations Manager, or self)
- `PATCH /users/{user_id}` - Update role (Admin only) or username (self)
- `PATCH /users/{user_id}/password` - Change password (requires current_password if self). Invalidates all tokens. 204 response
- `DELETE /users/{user_id}` - Soft-delete (Admin only)

**RBAC Rules:**
- Administrator: Full access
- Operations Manager: Full access except user role changes and audit deletion
- Moderator: Content moderation, ticket management; read-only commerce
- Group Leader: Own community performance; read catalog/inventory
- Staff: Inventory operations; read catalog
- Member: Search, content read, messaging

**Row-Level Scoping:**
- Group Leaders filtered to their communities
- Members cannot access others' search history/messages
- Staff inventory access unscoped by community

---

### 2. **C:\TASK-105\docs\questions.md**

Business logic clarifications covering 20 questions. Key auth/security-related:

**Q20: Authentication Lockout Reset**
- 5 consecutive failures → locked for 15 minutes
- Counter resets on successful login or timeout expiry
- Solution: Track failed attempts with timestamp; reset after success or expiry

**Q15: Role-Based Data Scope**
- Group leaders scoped to their communities
- Row-level filters in ORM queries based on role + ownership

**Q16: Soft Delete vs Hard Delete**
- System requires auditability → soft delete
- Add `deleted_at` field, exclude in queries by default

---

### 3. **C:\TASK-105\docs\design.md**

Complete system design document. Key sections:

**Auth Design (Section 3.1):**
```
Users table:
  - user_id UUID PK
  - username TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL
  - password_hash TEXT (Fernet-encrypted bcrypt)
  - role TEXT (enum: 6 roles)
  - failed_attempts INTEGER DEFAULT 0
  - locked_until DATETIME NULL
  - created_at DATETIME NOT NULL
  - deleted_at DATETIME NULL

Sessions table:
  - token_hash TEXT PK (SHA-256 hash)
  - user_id UUID FK
  - expires_at DATETIME NOT NULL
  - created_at DATETIME NOT NULL
```

**Auth Rules:**
- Password: min 12 chars, bcrypt (cost ≥12), Fernet-encrypted storage
- Lockout: 5 failures → `locked_until = now + 15 min`. Resets on success or expiry
- Tokens: 32-byte random, stored as SHA-256 hash. No JWT (offline system)

**Session Management:**
- Token stored as hash (not plaintext)
- Check: `session.expires_at > now` and `user.deleted_at is None`

**Encryption (Section 5.2):**
- Fernet symmetric encryption for `password_hash` and payout fields
- TypeDecorator `EncryptedText` handles transparent en/decryption
- Key from `data/keys/secret.key` (volume-mounted in Docker, never in image)

**Middleware Stack (Section 4.3):**
1. CorrelationIDMiddleware
2. AuthMiddleware (validate Bearer token, load user+role)
3. RBACMiddleware (check role against permission map)
4. RowScopeFilter (inside service methods)
5. AuditMiddleware (log mutations)
6. StructuredLogMiddleware (JSON logs, no message bodies)

**Audit Log (Section 3.8):**
- Append-only, no UPDATE/DELETE (SQLite trigger enforced)
- Covers: settlements, moderation, inventory adjustments, auth lockouts, content publish/rollback
- Fields: `action_type`, `actor_id`, `target_type`, `target_id`, `before_state`, `after_state`, `correlation_id`

**Background Jobs (Section 6):**
- Run via APScheduler in-process
- Message redelivery: Every 1 min with exponential backoff, 7-day purge

---

### 4. **C:\TASK-105\repo\app\middleware\auth.py**

Complete auth middleware:

```python
def _hash_token(raw: str) -> str:
    return hashlib.sha256(raw.encode()).hexdigest()

def load_user_from_token() -> User | None:
    # Extract "Bearer <token>" from Authorization header
    # Hash token, lookup in Sessions table
    # Check expiry and user.deleted_at
    # Return User or None

@require_auth  # Decorator
def decorated(*args, **kwargs):
    user = load_user_from_token()
    if user is None:
        raise UnauthorizedError("unauthorized", "Authentication required")
    g.current_user = user
    return f(*args, **kwargs)
```

**Key behavior:**
- Validates Bearer token format
- Hashes token and looks up in `Sessions` table
- Checks token expiration against current UTC time
- Checks user is not deleted
- Raises `UnauthorizedError` if any check fails
- Sets `g.current_user` on success

---

### 5. **C:\TASK-105\repo\app\middleware\rbac.py**

RBAC decorator middleware:

```python
ROLE_HIERARCHY = {
    "Administrator": 6,
    "Operations Manager": 5,
    "Moderator": 4,
    "Group Leader": 3,
    "Staff": 2,
    "Member": 1,
}

@require_roles(*roles)  # Usage: @require_roles("Administrator", "Operations Manager")
def decorator(f):
    def decorated(*args, **kwargs):
        user = g.get("current_user")
        if user is None or user.role not in roles:
            raise ForbiddenError("forbidden", "Insufficient permissions")
        return f(*args, **kwargs)

@require_min_role(min_role: str)  # Usage: @require_min_role("Administrator")
# Allows any role with hierarchy level >= min_role
```

**Key behavior:**
- Checks `g.current_user` existence and role
- Raises `ForbiddenError` if not authorized
- Supports exact role matching or hierarchical minimum

---

### 6. **C:\TASK-105\repo\app\middleware\logging.py**

Structured JSON logging middleware:

```python
REDACTED_KEYS = frozenset({"password", "password_hash", "current_password", "new_password"})

def _redact(obj, depth=0):
    # Recursively redacts sensitive keys and keys starting with "payout_"
    # Depth limit: 5
```

**Logging behavior:**
- Captures request start/end times
- Emits JSON with: `timestamp`, `level` (WARN if >100ms), `correlation_id`, `span_id`, `user_id`, `method`, `path`, `status_code`, `duration_ms`
- Redacts password fields automatically
- Log file: `data/logs/app.jsonl`

### 7. **C:\TASK-105\repo\app\models\user.py**

SQLAlchemy ORM models:

```python
class User(db.Model):
    __tablename__ = "users"
    
    user_id = GUID (UUID PK)
    username = String(64) UNIQUE NOT NULL
    password_hash = EncryptedText NOT NULL  # Fernet-encrypted bcrypt
    role = String(32) NOT NULL DEFAULT "Member"
    failed_attempts = Integer DEFAULT 0
    locked_until = DateTime NULL
    created_at = DateTime NOT NULL (default: now UTC)
    deleted_at = DateTime NULL
    
    sessions = relationship("Session", cascade="all, delete-orphan")
    
    def is_locked() -> bool:
        return datetime.now(UTC) < locked_until (if set)
    
    def to_dict() -> dict:
        Returns: user_id, username, role, created_at, deleted_at

class Session(db.Model):
    __tablename__ = "sessions"
    
    token_hash = String(64) PK  # SHA-256 hash
    user_id = GUID FK → Users (indexed)
    expires_at = DateTime NOT NULL
    created_at = DateTime NOT NULL
    
    user = relationship("User", back_populates="sessions")
```

**Constraints:**
- `role` must be in: Administrator, Operations Manager, Moderator, Group Leader, Staff, Member
- `username` must be unique
- `deleted_at` soft-delete field

### 10. **C:\TASK-105\repo\app\services\user_service.py**

1	"""
2	Auth decorator — validates Bearer token and loads current_user into g.
3	Usage: @require_auth on any route handler.
4	"""
5	import hashlib
6	from functools import wraps
7	from datetime import datetime, timezone
8	from flask import request, g
9	from app.extensions import db
10	from app.models.user import User
11	from app.models.user import Session
12	from app.errors import UnauthorizedError
13	
14	
15	def _hash_token(raw: str) -> str:
16	    return hashlib.sha256(raw.encode()).hexdigest()
17	
18	
19	def load_user_from_token() -> User | None:
20	    auth = request.headers.get("Authorization", "")
21	    if not auth.startswith("Bearer "):
22	        return None
23	    raw_token = auth[7:]
24	    token_hash = _hash_token(raw_token)
25	    session = db.session.get(Session, token_hash)
26	    if session is None:
27	        return None
28	    if session.expires_at.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc) < datetime.now(timezone.utc):
29	        return None
30	    user = db.session.get(User, session.user_id)
31	    if user is None or user.deleted_at is not None:
32	        return None
33	    return user
34	
35	
36	def require_auth(f):
37	    @wraps(f)
38	    def decorated(*args, **kwargs):
39	        user = load_user_from_token()
40	        if user is None:
41	            raise UnauthorizedError("unauthorized", "Authentication required")
42	        g.current_user = user
43	        return f(*args, **kwargs)
44	    return decorated
45

1	"""
2	RBAC decorator — checks the current_user's role against an allowed-roles list.
3	Must be applied after @require_auth.
4	
5	Usage:
6	    @require_auth
7	    @require_roles("Administrator", "Operations Manager")
8	    def my_view(): ...
9	"""
10	from functools import wraps
11	from flask import g
12	from app.errors import ForbiddenError
13	
14	ROLE_HIERARCHY = {
15	    "Administrator": 6,
16	    "Operations Manager": 5,
17	    "Moderator": 4,
18	    "Group Leader": 3,
19	    "Staff": 2,
20	    "Member": 1,
21	}
22	
23	
24	def require_roles(*roles: str):
25	    def decorator(f):
26	        @wraps(f)
27	        def decorated(*args, **kwargs):
28	            user = g.get("current_user")
29	            if user is None or user.role not in roles:
30	                raise ForbiddenError("forbidden", "Insufficient permissions")
31	            return f(*args, **kwargs)
32	        return decorated
33	    return decorator
34	
35	
36	def require_min_role(min_role: str):
37	    """Allow any role with hierarchy level >= min_role."""
38	    min_level = ROLE_HIERARCHY.get(min_role, 0)
39	
40	    def decorator(f):
41	        @wraps(f)
42	        def decorated(*args, **kwargs):
43	            user = g.get("current_user")
44	            if user is None:
45	                raise ForbiddenError("forbidden", "Insufficient permissions")
46	            if ROLE_HIERARCHY.get(user.role, 0) < min_level:
47	                raise ForbiddenError("forbidden", "Insufficient permissions")
48	            return f(*args, **kwargs)
49	        return decorated
50	    return decorator
51

1	"""
2	Structured JSON request/response logger.
3	Strips sensitive keys: body, password, password_hash, payout_*.
4	Message body (Messages.body) is also excluded by convention in service layer.
5	"""
6	import json
7	import time
8	import uuid
9	import logging
10	from flask import Flask, g, request
11	
12	REDACTED_KEYS = frozenset({"password", "password_hash", "current_password", "new_password"})
13	
14	
15	def _redact(obj, depth: int = 0):
16	    if depth > 5:
17	        return obj
18	    if isinstance(obj, dict):
19	        return {
20	            k: "[REDACTED]" if k in REDACTED_KEYS or k.startswith("payout_") else _redact(v, depth + 1)
21	            for k, v in obj.items()
22	        }
23	    if isinstance(obj, list):
24	        return [_redact(i, depth + 1) for i in obj]
25	    return obj
26	
27	
28	def init_logging_middleware(app: Flask) -> None:
29	    log_file = app.config.get("LOG_FILE", "data/logs/app.jsonl")
30	    log_level = app.config.get("LOG_LEVEL", "INFO")
31	
32	    handler = logging.FileHandler(log_file)
33	    handler.setLevel(log_level)
34	    app.logger.addHandler(handler)
35	    app.logger.setLevel(log_level)
36	
37	    @app.before_request
38	    def start_timer():
39	        g.start_time = time.time()
40	        g.span_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
41	
42	    @app.after_request
43	    def log_request(response):
44	        duration_ms = round((time.time() - getattr(g, "start_time", time.time())) * 1000, 2)
45	        user = getattr(g, "current_user", None)
46	
47	        entry = {
48	            "timestamp": __import__("datetime").datetime.utcnow().isoformat() + "Z",
49	            "level": "WARN" if duration_ms > 100 else "INFO",
50	            "correlation_id": getattr(g, "correlation_id", ""),
51	            "span_id": getattr(g, "span_id", ""),
52	            "user_id": str(user.user_id) if user else None,
53	            "method": request.method,
54	            "path": request.path,
55	            "status_code": response.status_code,
56	            "duration_ms": duration_ms,
57	        }
58	        app.logger.info(json.dumps(entry))
59	        return response
60

1	"""User management service (update, delete, password change)."""
2	import bcrypt
3	from datetime import datetime, timezone
4	
5	from app.extensions import db
6	from app.models.user import User, ROLES
7	from app.errors import NotFoundError, ForbiddenError, AppError
8	from app.services.auth_service import AuthService
9	
10	
11	class UserService:
12	
13	    @staticmethod
14	    def _get_or_404(user_id: str) -> User:
15	        user = db.session.get(User, user_id)
16	        if user is None or user.deleted_at is not None:
17	            raise NotFoundError("user")
18	        return user
19	
20	    @staticmethod
21	    def list_users(role=None, page=1, page_size=20, include_deleted=False) -> dict:
22	        q = User.query
23	        if not include_deleted:
24	            q = q.filter(User.deleted_at.is_(None))
25	        if role:
26	            q = q.filter(User.role == role)
27	        total = q.count()
28	        items = q.offset((page - 1) * page_size).limit(page_size).all()
29	        return {"total": total, "page": page, "page_size": page_size, "items": [u.to_dict() for u in items]}
30	
31	    @staticmethod
32	    def get_user(user_id: str, requester: User) -> User:
33	        user = UserService._get_or_404(user_id)
34	        if requester.role not in ("Administrator", "Operations Manager") and str(requester.user_id) != user_id:
35	            raise ForbiddenError("forbidden", "Cannot view this user")
36	        return user
37	
38	    @staticmethod
39	    def update_user(user_id: str, data: dict, requester: User) -> User:
40	        user = UserService._get_or_404(user_id)
41	        if "role" in data:
42	            if requester.role != "Administrator":
43	                raise ForbiddenError("forbidden", "Only Administrators can change roles")
44	            if data["role"] not in ROLES:
45	                raise AppError("invalid_role", "Invalid role", field="role")
46	            user.role = data["role"]
47	        if "username" in data:
48	            if str(requester.user_id) != user_id and requester.role != "Administrator":
49	                raise ForbiddenError("forbidden", "Can only change your own username")
50	            user.username = data["username"]
51	        db.session.commit()
52	        return user
53	
54	    @staticmethod
55	    def change_password(user_id: str, data: dict, requester: User) -> None:
56	        user = UserService._get_or_404(user_id)
57	        if str(requester.user_id) != user_id and requester.role != "Administrator":
58	            raise ForbiddenError("forbidden", "Cannot change another user's password")
59	        new_pw = data.get("new_password", "")
60	        if len(new_pw) < 12:
61	            raise AppError("password_too_short", "Password must be at least 12 characters", field="new_password", status_code=400)
62	        if str(requester.user_id) == user_id:
63	            current_pw = data.get("current_password", "")
64	            if not bcrypt.checkpw(current_pw.encode(), user.password_hash.encode()):
65	                raise AppError("invalid_current_password", "Current password is incorrect", field="current_password", status_code=400)
66	        user.password_hash = bcrypt.hashpw(new_pw.encode(), bcrypt.gensalt(rounds=12)).decode()
67	        AuthService.invalidate_all_sessions(user.user_id)
68	        db.session.commit()
69	
70	    @staticmethod
71	    def delete_user(user_id: str) -> None:
72	        user = UserService._get_or_404(user_id)
73	        user.deleted_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
74	        db.session.commit()
75

32	def test_login_invalid_credentials(client):
33	    resp = client.post("/api/v1/auth/login", json={"username": "nobody", "password": "WrongPass1234!"})
34	    assert resp.status_code == 401
35	
36	
37	def test_login_lockout(client):
38	    client.post("/api/v1/auth/register", json={"username": "lockme", "password": "ValidPass1234!"})
39	    for _ in range(5):
40	        client.post("/api/v1/auth/login", json={"username": "lockme", "password": "WrongPass!"})
41	    resp = client.post("/api/v1/auth/login", json={"username": "lockme", "password": "ValidPass1234!"})
42	    assert resp.status_code == 423
43	    assert resp.json["error"] == "account_locked"
44	
45	
46	def test_logout(client):
47	    client.post("/api/v1/auth/register", json={"username": "logout_u", "password": "ValidPass1234!"})
48	    login_resp = client.post("/api/v1/auth/login", json={"username": "logout_u", "password": "ValidPass1234!"})
49	    token = login_resp.json["token"]
50	    resp = client.post("/api/v1/auth/logout", headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"})
51	    assert resp.status_code == 204

1	"""
2	Authentication service.
3	Handles registration, login (with lockout), logout, and token management.
4	"""
5	import hashlib
6	import os
7	import secrets
8	from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta
9	
10	import bcrypt
11	
12	from app.extensions import db
13	from app.models.user import User, Session, ROLES
14	from app.errors import ConflictError, UnauthorizedError, LockedError, AppError
15	
16	_LOCKOUT_ATTEMPTS = 5
17	_LOCKOUT_MINUTES = 15
18	_TOKEN_EXPIRY_HOURS = 24
19	
20	
21	class AuthService:
22	
23	    @staticmethod
24	    def register(username: str, password: str, role: str = "Member") -> User:
25	        if len(password) < 12:
26	            raise AppError("password_too_short", "Password must be at least 12 characters", field="password", status_code=400)
27	        if role not in ROLES:
28	            raise AppError("invalid_role", f"Role must be one of: {', '.join(ROLES)}", field="role", status_code=400)
29	        if User.query.filter_by(username=username).first():
30	            raise ConflictError("username_taken", "Username already in use", field="username")
31	
32	        hashed = bcrypt.hashpw(password.encode(), bcrypt.gensalt(rounds=12)).decode()
33	        user = User(username=username, password_hash=hashed, role=role)
34	        db.session.add(user)
35	        db.session.commit()
36	        return user
37	
38	    @staticmethod
39	    def login(username: str, password: str) -> dict:
40	        user = User.query.filter_by(username=username, deleted_at=None).first()
41	        if user is None:
42	            raise UnauthorizedError("invalid_credentials", "Invalid username or password")
43	
44	        if user.is_locked():
45	            retry_after = user.locked_until.isoformat()
46	            raise LockedError(retry_after)
47	
48	        pw_valid = bcrypt.checkpw(password.encode(), user.password_hash.encode())
49	        if not pw_valid:
50	            user.failed_attempts += 1
51	            if user.failed_attempts >= _LOCKOUT_ATTEMPTS:
52	                user.locked_until = datetime.now(timezone.utc) + timedelta(minutes=_LOCKOUT_MINUTES)
53	            db.session.commit()
54	            raise UnauthorizedError("invalid_credentials", "Invalid username or password")
55	
56	        # Successful login — reset counter
57	        user.failed_attempts = 0
58	        user.locked_until = None
59	
60	        raw_token = secrets.token_hex(32)
61	        token_hash = hashlib.sha256(raw_token.encode()).hexdigest()
62	        expires_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc) + timedelta(hours=_TOKEN_EXPIRY_HOURS)
63	
64	        session = Session(token_hash=token_hash, user_id=user.user_id, expires_at=expires_at)
65	        db.session.add(session)
66	        db.session.commit()
67	
68	        return {
69	            "token": raw_token,
70	            "expires_at": expires_at.isoformat(),
71	            "user_id": str(user.user_id),
72	            "role": user.role,
73	        }
74	
75	    @staticmethod
76	    def logout(raw_token: str) -> None:
77	        token_hash = hashlib.sha256(raw_token.encode()).hexdigest()
78	        session = db.session.get(Session, token_hash)
79	        if session:
80	            db.session.delete(session)
81	            db.session.commit()
82	
83	    @staticmethod
84	    def invalidate_all_sessions(user_id) -> None:
85	        Session.query.filter_by(user_id=user_id).delete()
86	        db.session.commit()
87

1	from datetime import datetime, timezone
2	import sqlalchemy as sa
3	from app.extensions import db
4	from .base import GUID, EncryptedText, new_uuid
5	
6	ROLES = (
7	    "Administrator",
8	    "Operations Manager",
9	    "Moderator",
10	    "Group Leader",
11	    "Staff",
12	    "Member",
13	)
14	
15	_ROLE_LIST = ", ".join(f"'{r}'" for r in ROLES)
16	
17	
18	class User(db.Model):
19	    __tablename__ = "users"
20	    __table_args__ = (
21	        sa.CheckConstraint(
22	            f"role IN ({_ROLE_LIST})",
23	            name="ck_users_role",
24	        ),
25	    )
26	
27	    user_id = db.Column(GUID, primary_key=True, default=new_uuid)
28	    username = db.Column(db.String(64), unique=True, nullable=False)
29	    # bcrypt hash stored Fernet-encrypted at rest
30	    password_hash = db.Column(EncryptedText, nullable=False)
31	    role = db.Column(db.String(32), nullable=False, default="Member")
32	    failed_attempts = db.Column(db.Integer, nullable=False, default=0)
33	    locked_until = db.Column(db.DateTime, nullable=True)
34	    created_at = db.Column(
35	        db.DateTime, nullable=False, default=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc)
36	    )
37	    deleted_at = db.Column(db.DateTime, nullable=True)
38	
39	    sessions = db.relationship(
40	        "Session", back_populates="user", lazy="dynamic", cascade="all, delete-orphan"
41	    )
42	
43	    def is_locked(self) -> bool:
44	        if self.locked_until is None:
45	            return False
46	        return datetime.now(timezone.utc) < self.locked_until.replace(
47	            tzinfo=timezone.utc
48	        )
49	
50	    def to_dict(self) -> dict:
51	        return {
52	            "user_id": str(self.user_id),
53	            "username": self.username,
54	            "role": self.role,
55	            "created_at": self.created_at.isoformat(),
56	            "deleted_at": self.deleted_at.isoformat() if self.deleted_at else None,
57	        }
58	
59	
60	class Session(db.Model):
61	    __tablename__ = "sessions"
62	
63	    token_hash = db.Column(db.String(64), primary_key=True)
64	    user_id = db.Column(GUID, db.ForeignKey("users.user_id"), nullable=False, index=True)
65	    expires_at = db.Column(db.DateTime, nullable=False)
66	    created_at = db.Column(
67	        db.DateTime, nullable=False, default=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc)
68	    )
69	
70	    user = db.relationship("User", back_populates="sessions")
71

1	"""
2	Shared base utilities for models.
3	"""
4	import uuid
5	from sqlalchemy import TypeDecorator, String
6	from app.extensions import db
7	from app import crypto
8	
9	
10	class GUID(TypeDecorator):
11	    """Stores UUID as TEXT in SQLite."""
12	    impl = String(36)
13	    cache_ok = True
14	
15	    def process_bind_param(self, value, dialect):
16	        if value is None:
17	            return None
18	        return str(value)
19	
20	    def process_result_value(self, value, dialect):
21	        if value is None:
22	            return None
23	        return uuid.UUID(value)
24	
25	
26	class EncryptedText(TypeDecorator):
27	    """Transparently Fernet-encrypts text columns at rest."""
28	    impl = String
29	    cache_ok = True
30	
31	    def process_bind_param(self, value, dialect):
32	        if value is None:
33	            return None
34	        return crypto.encrypt(value)
35	
36	    def process_result_value(self, value, dialect):
37	        if value is None:
38	            return None
39	        return crypto.decrypt(value)
40	
41	
42	def new_uuid() -> str:
43	    return str(uuid.uuid4())
44

1	import os
2	from flask import Flask
3	from sqlalchemy import event
4	from sqlalchemy.engine import Engine
5	import sqlite3
6	
7	from .config import config_map, Config
8	from .extensions import db, migrate, socketio, scheduler
9	from .errors import register_error_handlers
10	from .middleware import register_middleware
11	from .crypto import init_fernet
12	
13	
14	def create_app(config_name: str | None = None) -> Flask:
15	    if config_name is None:
16	        config_name = os.environ.get("FLASK_ENV", "development")
17	
18	    app = Flask(__name__, instance_relative_config=False)
19	    app.config.from_object(config_map.get(config_name, Config))
20	
21	    # --- Fernet encryption key ---
22	    init_fernet(app.config["FERNET_KEY_PATH"])
23	
24	    # --- Extensions ---
25	    db.init_app(app)
26	    migrate.init_app(app, db)
27	    socketio.init_app(app)
28	
29	    # Enable WAL mode for SQLite
30	    @event.listens_for(Engine, "connect")
31	    def set_sqlite_pragma(dbapi_conn, connection_record):
32	        if isinstance(dbapi_conn, sqlite3.Connection):
33	            cursor = dbapi_conn.cursor()
34	            cursor.execute("PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL")
35	            cursor.execute("PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON")
36	            cursor.close()
37	
38	    # --- Middleware ---
39	    register_middleware(app)
40	
41	    # --- Blueprints ---
42	    _register_blueprints(app)
43	
44	    # --- Error handlers ---
45	    register_error_handlers(app)
46	
47	    # --- Background jobs ---
48	    if app.config.get("JOBS_ENABLED"):
49	        with app.app_context():
50	            _start_scheduler(app)
51	
52	    return app
53	
54	
55	def _register_blueprints(app: Flask) -> None:
56	    from .routes.health import health_bp
57	    from .routes.auth import auth_bp
58	    from .routes.users import users_bp
59	    from .routes.communities import communities_bp
60	    from .routes.commission import commission_bp
61	    from .routes.catalog import catalog_bp
62	    from .routes.search import search_bp
63	    from .routes.inventory import inventory_bp
64	    from .routes.messaging import messaging_bp
65	    from .routes.content import content_bp
66	    from .routes.templates import templates_bp
67	    from .routes.admin import admin_bp
68	
69	    # Health probes (no version prefix — standard convention)
70	    app.register_blueprint(health_bp)
71	
72	    v1 = "/api/v1"
73	    app.register_blueprint(auth_bp, url_prefix=v1)
74	    app.register_blueprint(users_bp, url_prefix=v1)
75	    app.register_blueprint(communities_bp, url_prefix=v1)
76	    app.register_blueprint(commission_bp, url_prefix=v1)
77	    app.register_blueprint(catalog_bp, url_prefix=v1)
78	    app.register_blueprint(search_bp, url_prefix=v1)
79	    app.register_blueprint(inventory_bp, url_prefix=v1)
80	    app.register_blueprint(messaging_bp, url_prefix=v1)
81	    app.register_blueprint(content_bp, url_prefix=v1)
82	    app.register_blueprint(templates_bp, url_prefix=v1)
83	    app.register_blueprint(admin_bp, url_prefix=v1)
84	
85	
86	def _start_scheduler(app: Flask) -> None:
87	    from .jobs import register_jobs
88	    register_jobs(scheduler, app)
89	    if not scheduler.running:
90	        scheduler.start()
91

1	from flask import Flask, jsonify
2	
3	
4	class AppError(Exception):
5	    """Base application error — raised in service layer, caught here."""
6	    status_code = 400
7	
8	    def __init__(self, error: str, message: str, field: str | None = None, status_code: int | None = None):
9	        self.error = error
10	        self.message = message
11	        self.field = field
12	        if status_code is not None:
13	            self.status_code = status_code
14	        super().__init__(message)
15	
16	    def to_dict(self) -> dict:
17	        d = {"error": self.error, "message": self.message}
18	        if self.field:
19	            d["field"] = self.field
20	        return d
21	
22	
23	class NotFoundError(AppError):
24	    status_code = 404
25	
26	    def __init__(self, resource: str = "resource"):
27	        super().__init__("not_found", f"{resource} not found", status_code=404)
28	
29	
30	class ConflictError(AppError):
31	    status_code = 409
32	
33	
34	class UnauthorizedError(AppError):
35	    status_code = 401
36	
37	
38	class ForbiddenError(AppError):
39	    status_code = 403
40	
41	
42	class UnprocessableError(AppError):
43	    status_code = 422
44	
45	
46	class LockedError(AppError):
47	    status_code = 423
48	
49	    def __init__(self, retry_after: str):
50	        super().__init__("account_locked", "Account temporarily locked", status_code=423)
51	        self.retry_after = retry_after
52	
53	    def to_dict(self) -> dict:
54	        d = super().to_dict()
55	        d["retry_after"] = self.retry_after
56	        return d
57	
58	
59	def register_error_handlers(app: Flask) -> None:
60	    @app.errorhandler(AppError)
61	    def handle_app_error(err: AppError):
62	        return jsonify(err.to_dict()), err.status_code
63	
64	    @app.errorhandler(404)
65	    def handle_404(_err):
66	        return jsonify({"error": "not_found", "message": "Endpoint not found"}), 404
67	
68	    @app.errorhandler(405)
69	    def handle_405(_err):
70	        return jsonify({"error": "method_not_allowed", "message": "Method not allowed"}), 405
71	
72	    @app.errorhandler(500)
73	    def handle_500(_err):
74	        return jsonify({"error": "internal_error", "message": "An unexpected error occurred"}), 500
75

1	"""
2	Injects / propagates X-Correlation-ID on every request.
3	The value is stored in Flask's g context so service/job code can access it.
4	"""
5	import uuid
6	from flask import Flask, g, request, Response
7	
8	
9	def init_correlation_middleware(app: Flask) -> None:
10	    @app.before_request
11	    def set_correlation_id():
12	        g.correlation_id = request.headers.get("X-Correlation-ID") or str(uuid.uuid4())
13	
14	    @app.after_request
15	    def add_correlation_header(response: Response) -> Response:
16	        response.headers["X-Correlation-ID"] = getattr(g, "correlation_id", "")
17	        return response
18

12	    APP_VERSION = os.environ.get("APP_VERSION", "0.1.0")
13	
14	    LOG_LEVEL = os.environ.get("LOG_LEVEL", "INFO")
15	    LOG_FILE = os.environ.get("LOG_FILE", "data/logs/app.jsonl")
16	
17	    ATTACHMENT_DIR = os.environ.get("ATTACHMENT_DIR", "data/attachments")
18	    ATTACHMENT_MAX_BYTES = int(os.environ.get("ATTACHMENT_MAX_BYTES", 26_214_400))
19	    ATTACHMENT_ALLOWED_MIME = {"image/png", "image/jpeg", "application/pdf", "text/plain", "text/markdown"}

28	class DevelopmentConfig(Config):

30	    SQLALCHEMY_ECHO = False

32

1	"""
2	Fernet symmetric encryption helpers.
3	Used by the EncryptedText SQLAlchemy TypeDecorator for at-rest encryption of
4	sensitive fields (password_hash, payout identifiers).
5	"""
6	import os
7	from cryptography.fernet import Fernet
8	
9	_fernet: Fernet | None = None
10	
11	
12	def init_fernet(key_path: str) -> None:
13	    global _fernet
14	    os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(key_path) or ".", exist_ok=True)
15	    if not os.path.exists(key_path):
16	        with open(key_path, "wb") as f:
17	            f.write(Fernet.generate_key())
18	    with open(key_path, "rb") as f:
19	        _fernet = Fernet(f.read().strip())
20	
21	
22	def encrypt(plaintext: str) -> str:
23	    if _fernet is None:
24	        raise RuntimeError("Fernet not initialised — call init_fernet() first")
25	    return _fernet.encrypt(plaintext.encode()).decode()
26	
27	
28	def decrypt(ciphertext: str) -> str:
29	    if _fernet is None:
30	        raise RuntimeError("Fernet not initialised — call init_fernet() first")
31	    return _fernet.decrypt(ciphertext.encode()).decode()

{"file_path": "C:\TASK-105\repo\app\services\auth_service.py", "content": """"
Authentication service.
Handles registration, login (with lockout), logout, and token management.
"""
import hashlib
import json
import secrets
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta

import bcrypt
from flask import g

from app.extensions import db
from app.models.user import User, Session, ROLES
from app.models.audit import AuditLog
from app.errors import ConflictError, UnauthorizedError, LockedError, AppError

_LOCKOUT_ATTEMPTS = 5
_LOCKOUT_MINUTES = 15
_TOKEN_EXPIRY_HOURS = 24


def _correlation_id() -> str:
    return getattr(g, "correlation_id", "")


class AuthService:

    @staticmethod
    def register(username: str, password: str, role: str = "Member") -> User:
        if len(password) < 12:
            raise AppError("password_too_short", "Password must be at least 12 characters", field="password", status_code=400)
        if role not in ROLES:
            raise AppError("invalid_role", f"Role must be one of: {', '.join(ROLES)}", field="role", status_code=400)
        if User.query.filter_by(username=username).first():
            raise ConflictError("username_taken", "Username already in use", field="username")

        hashed = bcrypt.hashpw(password.encode(), bcrypt.gensalt(rounds=12)).decode()
        user = User(username=username, password_hash=hashed, role=role)
        db.session.add(user)
        db.session.flush()  # assigns user_id before audit log

        audit = AuditLog(
            action_type="auth",
            actor_id=None,
            target_type="User",
            target_id=str(user.user_id),
            after_state=json.dumps({"event": "user_registered", "username": username, "role": role}),
            correlation_id=_correlation_id(),
        )
        db.session.add(audit)
        db.session.commit()
        return user

    @staticmethod
    def login(username: str, password: str) -> dict:
        user = User.query.filter_by(username=username, deleted_at=None).first()
        if user is None:
            raise UnauthorizedError("invalid_credentials", "Invalid username or password")

        if user.is_locked():
            retry_after = user.locked_until.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc).isoformat()
            raise LockedError(retry_after)

        # EncryptedText TypeDecorator transparently decrypts; user.password_hash is the bcrypt hash
        pw_valid = bcrypt.checkpw(password.encode(), user.password_hash.encode())
        if not pw_valid:
            user.failed_attempts += 1
            if user.failed_attempts >= _LOCKOUT_ATTEMPTS:
                user.locked_until = datetime.now(timezone.utc) + timedelta(minutes=_LOCKOUT_MINUTES)
                audit = AuditLog(
                    action_type="auth",
                    actor_id=None,
                    target_type="User",
                    target_id=str(user.user_id),
                    after_state=json.dumps({
                        "event": "account_locked",
                        "locked_until": user.locked_until.isoformat(),
                    }),
                    correlation_id=_correlation_id(),
                )
                db.session.add(audit)
            db.session.commit()
            raise UnauthorizedError("invalid_credentials", "Invalid username or password")

        # Successful login — reset lockout state
        user.failed_attempts = 0
        user.locked_until = None

        raw_token = secrets.token_hex(32)
        token_hash = hashlib.sha256(raw_token.encode()).hexdigest()
        expires_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc) + timedelta(hours=_TOKEN_EXPIRY_HOURS)

        session = Session(token_hash=token_hash, user_id=user.user_id, expires_at=expires_at)
        db.session.add(session)
        db.session.commit()

        return {
            "token": raw_token,
            "expires_at": expires_at.isoformat(),
            "user_id": str(user.user_id),
            "role": user.role,
        }

    @staticmethod
    def logout(raw_token: str) -> None:
        token_hash = hashlib.sha256(raw_token.encode()).hexdigest()
        session = db.session.get(Session, token_hash)
        if session:
            db.session.delete(session)
            db.session.commit()

    @staticmethod
    def invalidate_all_sessions(user_id) -> None:
        Session.query.filter_by(user_id=user_id).delete()
        # Caller commits
"}

Structured JSON request/response logger.

Structured JSON request/response logger.
Strips sensitive keys from any logged data: passwords, payout fields, message body.
Message.body is excluded at this layer so it never appears in log output even if
a future code path accidentally includes it in a loggable structure.
"""
import json
import os
import time
import uuid
import logging
from flask import Flask, g, request

# Keys redacted from any dict that passes through _redact().
# "body" covers Messages.body (chat content — must not be stored in logs).
REDACTED_KEYS = frozenset({
    "password",
    "password_hash",
    "current_password",
    "new_password",
    "body",          # Messages.body — chat content must not appear in logs
})"}

{"file_path": "C:\TASK-105\repo\tests\test_auth.py", "content": """"Auth endpoint tests."""


def test_register_success(client):
    resp = client.post("/api/v1/auth/register", json={
        "username": "newuser", "password": "ValidPass1234!", "role": "Member",
    })
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    assert resp.json["username"] == "newuser"


def test_register_duplicate_username(client):
    client.post("/api/v1/auth/register", json={"username": "dup", "password": "ValidPass1234!"})
    resp = client.post("/api/v1/auth/register", json={"username": "dup", "password": "ValidPass1234!"})
    assert resp.status_code == 409
    assert resp.json["error"] == "username_taken"


def test_register_short_password(client):
    resp = client.post("/api/v1/auth/register", json={"username": "shortpw", "password": "short"})
    assert resp.status_code == 400
    assert resp.json["error"] == "password_too_short"


def test_register_invalid_role(client):
    resp = client.post("/api/v1/auth/register", json={
        "username": "badrole", "password": "ValidPass1234!", "role": "SuperUser",
    })
    assert resp.status_code == 400


def test_login_success(client):
    client.post("/api/v1/auth/register", json={"username": "loginuser", "password": "ValidPass1234!"})
    resp = client.post("/api/v1/auth/login", json={"username": "loginuser", "password": "ValidPass1234!"})
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    data = resp.json
    assert "token" in data
    assert "expires_at" in data
    assert "user_id" in data
    assert data["role"] == "Member"


def test_login_invalid_credentials(client):
    resp = client.post("/api/v1/auth/login", json={"username": "nobody", "password": "WrongPass1234!"})
    assert resp.status_code == 401
    assert resp.json["error"] == "invalid_credentials"


def test_login_lockout(client):
    client.post("/api/v1/auth/register", json={"username": "lockme", "password": "ValidPass1234!"})
    for _ in range(5):
        client.post("/api/v1/auth/login", json={"username": "lockme", "password": "WrongPass1234!"})
    # 6th attempt with correct password still locked
    resp = client.post("/api/v1/auth/login", json={"username": "lockme", "password": "ValidPass1234!"})
    assert resp.status_code == 423
    assert resp.json["error"] == "account_locked"
    assert "retry_after" in resp.json


def test_login_lockout_exactly_5_attempts(client):
    """4 failed attempts must NOT lock the account."""
    client.post("/api/v1/auth/register", json={"username": "notlocked", "password": "ValidPass1234!"})
    for _ in range(4):
        client.post("/api/v1/auth/login", json={"username": "notlocked", "password": "WrongPass1234!"})
    # 5th attempt with correct password should succeed
    resp = client.post("/api/v1/auth/login", json={"username": "notlocked", "password": "ValidPass1234!"})
    assert resp.status_code == 200


def test_logout(client):
    client.post("/api/v1/auth/register", json={"username": "logout_u", "password": "ValidPass1234!"})
    login_resp = client.post("/api/v1/auth/login", json={"username": "logout_u", "password": "ValidPass1234!"})
    token = login_resp.json["token"]
    resp = client.post("/api/v1/auth/logout", headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"})
    assert resp.status_code == 204


def test_token_invalid_after_logout(client):
    """Token must be rejected by protected endpoints after logout."""
    reg = client.post("/api/v1/auth/register", json={"username": "postlogout", "password": "ValidPass1234!"})
    user_id = reg.json["user_id"]
    token = client.post("/api/v1/auth/login", json={
        "username": "postlogout", "password": "ValidPass1234!",
    }).json["token"]

    client.post("/api/v1/auth/logout", headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"})

    # Token must now be rejected
    resp = client.get(f"/api/v1/users/{user_id}", headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"})
    assert resp.status_code == 401


def test_require_auth_missing_header(client):
    resp = client.post("/api/v1/auth/logout")
    assert resp.status_code == 401


def test_require_auth_malformed_header(client):
    resp = client.post("/api/v1/auth/logout", headers={"Authorization": "Token abc123"})
    assert resp.status_code == 401
"}

        client.post("/api/v1/auth/login", json={"username": "lockme", "password": "WrongPass1234!"})
    # 6th attempt with correct password still locked
    resp = client.post("/api/v1/auth/login", json={"username": "lockme", "password": "ValidPass1234!"})
    assert resp.status_code == 423
    assert resp.json["error"] == "account_locked"
    assert "retry_after" in resp.json


def test_login_lockout_exactly_5_attempts(client):
    """4 failed attempts must NOT lock the account."""
    client.post("/api/v1/auth/register", json={"username": "notlocked", "password": "ValidPass1234!"})
    for _ in range(4):
        client.post("/api/v1/auth/login", json={"username": "notlocked", "password": "WrongPass1234!"})
    # 5th attempt with correct password should succeed
    resp = client.post("/api/v1/auth/login", json={"username": "notlocked", "password": "ValidPass1234!"})
    assert resp.status_code == 200


def test_logout(client):
    client.post("/api/v1/auth/register", json={"username": "logout_u", "password": "ValidPass1234!"})
    login_resp = client.post("/api/v1/auth/login", json={"username": "logout_u", "password": "ValidPass1234!"})
    token = login_resp.json["token"]
    resp = client.post("/api/v1/auth/logout", headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"})
    assert resp.status_code == 204


def test_token_invalid_after_logout(client):
    """Token must be rejected by protected endpoints after logout."""
    reg = client.post("/api/v1/auth/register", json={"username": "postlogout", "password": "ValidPass1234!"})
    user_id = reg.json["user_id"]
    token = client.post("/api/v1/auth/login", json={
        "username": "postlogout", "password": "ValidPass1234!",
    }).json["token"]

    client.post("/api/v1/auth/logout", headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"})

    # Token must now be rejected
    resp = client.get(f"/api/v1/users/{user_id}", headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"})
    assert resp.status_code == 401


def test_require_auth_missing_header(client):
    resp = client.post("/api/v1/auth/logout")
    assert resp.status_code == 401


def test_require_auth_malformed_header(client):
    resp = client.post("/api/v1/auth/logout", headers={"Authorization": "Token abc123"})
    assert resp.status_code == 401
"}

{"file_path": "C:\TASK-105\repo\tests\test_users.py", "content": """"
User management endpoint tests.
Covers RBAC enforcement, row-level scoping (Member/Group Leader), and
sensitive-action guards (password change, role assignment, soft delete).
"""
import uuid


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helpers
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _register_login(client, role="Member", password="ValidPass1234!"):
    """Register a user with a unique username and return (user_id, token)."""
    username = f"u_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:10]}"
    reg = client.post("/api/v1/auth/register", json={
        "username": username, "password": password, "role": role,
    })
    assert reg.status_code == 201, reg.json
    token = client.post("/api/v1/auth/login", json={
        "username": username, "password": password,
    }).json["token"]
    return reg.json["user_id"], token


def _headers(token):
    return {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# GET /users  (list) — Admin / Operations Manager only
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def test_list_users_unauthenticated(client):
    resp = client.get("/api/v1/users")
    assert resp.status_code == 401


def test_list_users_member_forbidden(client):
    _, token = _register_login(client, role="Member")
    resp = client.get("/api/v1/users", headers=_headers(token))
    assert resp.status_code == 403


def test_list_users_group_leader_forbidden(client):
    _, token = _register_login(client, role="Group Leader")
    resp = client.get("/api/v1/users", headers=_headers(token))
    assert resp.status_code == 403


def test_list_users_admin_ok(client):
    _, token = _register_login(client, role="Administrator")
    resp = client.get("/api/v1/users", headers=_headers(token))
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    data = resp.json
    assert "items" in data
    assert "total" in data
    assert "page" in data


def test_list_users_ops_manager_ok(client):
    _, token = _register_login(client, role="Operations Manager")
    resp = client.get("/api/v1/users", headers=_headers(token))
    assert resp.status_code == 200


def test_list_users_role_filter(client):
    _, token = _register_login(client, role="Administrator")
    resp = client.get("/api/v1/users?role=Member", headers=_headers(token))
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    for item in resp.json["items"]:
        assert item["role"] == "Member"


def test_list_users_pagination(client):
    _, token = _register_login(client, role="Administrator")
    resp = client.get("/api/v1/users?page=1&page_size=2", headers=_headers(token))
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert len(resp.json["items"]) <= 2


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# GET /users/{user_id} — self or Admin/OpsMgr
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def test_get_user_self(client):
    user_id, token = _register_login(client)
    resp = client.get(f"/api/v1/users/{user_id}", headers=_headers(token))
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert resp.json["user_id"] == user_id


def test_get_user_member_cannot_view_other(client):
    """Row-level scoping: Member cannot view another user's profile."""
    other_id, _ = _register_login(client)
    _, token = _register_login(client)
    resp = client.get(f"/api/v1/users/{other_id}", headers=_headers(token))
    assert resp.status_code == 403


def test_get_user_group_leader_cannot_view_other(client):
    """Group Leader cannot view other users (they are not Admin/OpsMgr)."""
    other_id, _ = _register_login(client)
    _, token = _register_login(client, role="Group Leader")
    resp = client.get(f"/api/v1/users/{other_id}", headers=_headers(token))
    assert resp.status_code == 403


def test_get_user_admin_can_view_any(client):
    other_id, _ = _register_login(client)
    _, admin_token = _register_login(client, role="Administrator")
    resp = client.get(f"/api/v1/users/{other_id}", headers=_headers(admin_token))
    assert resp.status_code == 200


def test_get_user_not_found(client):
    _, token = _register_login(client, role="Administrator")
    resp = client.get(f"/api/v1/users/{uuid.uuid4()}", headers=_headers(token))
    assert resp.status_code == 404


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PATCH /users/{user_id} — role change: Admin only; username: self or Admin
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def test_change_role_requires_admin(client):
    user_id, token = _register_login(client, role="Moderator")
    resp = client.patch(f"/api/v1/users/{user_id}", json={"role": "Staff"}, headers=_headers(token))
    assert resp.status_code == 403


def test_ops_manager_cannot_change_role(client):
    user_id, _ = _register_login(client)
    _, ops_token = _register_login(client, role="Operations Manager")
    resp = client.patch(f"/api/v1/users/{user_id}", json={"role": "Staff"}, headers=_headers(ops_token))
    assert resp.status_code == 403


def test_admin_can_change_role(client):
    user_id, _ = _register_login(client)
    _, admin_token = _register_login(client, role="Administrator")
    resp = client.patch(f"/api/v1/users/{user_id}", json={"role": "Staff"}, headers=_headers(admin_token))
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert resp.json["role"] == "Staff"


def test_change_role_invalid_value(client):
    user_id, _ = _register_login(client)
    _, admin_token = _register_login(client, role="Administrator")
    resp = client.patch(f"/api/v1/users/{user_id}", json={"role": "Ghost"}, headers=_headers(admin_token))
    assert resp.status_code == 400
    assert resp.json["error"] == "invalid_role"


def test_user_can_change_own_username(client):
    user_id, token = _register_login(client)
    new_name = f"renamed_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:6]}"
    resp = client.patch(f"/api/v1/users/{user_id}", json={"username": new_name}, headers=_headers(token))
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert resp.json["username"] == new_name


def test_member_cannot_change_other_username(client):
    other_id, _ = _register_login(client)
    _, token = _register_login(client)
    resp = client.patch(f"/api/v1/users/{other_id}", json={"username": "hacked"}, headers=_headers(token))
    assert resp.status_code == 403


def test_update_username_uniqueness_enforced(client):
    """Changing to an already-taken username must return 409, not a DB error."""
    user_id1, _ = _register_login(client)
    # Get user1's username
    _, admin_token = _register_login(client, role="Administrator")
    u1 = client.get(f"/api/v1/users/{user_id1}", headers=_headers(admin_token)).json
    user_id2, token2 = _register_login(client)
    resp = client.patch(f"/api/v1/users/{user_id2}", json={"username": u1["username"]}, headers=_headers(admin_token))
    assert resp.status_code == 409
    assert resp.json["error"] == "username_taken"


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PATCH /users/{user_id}/password
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def test_change_password_self_success(client):
    user_id, token = _register_login(client, password="OldValidPass1234!")
    resp = client.patch(f"/api/v1/users/{user_id}/password", json={
        "current_password": "OldValidPass1234!",
        "new_password": "NewValidPass1234!",
    }, headers=_headers(token))
    assert resp.status_code == 204


def test_change_password_invalidates_old_token(client):
    """After password change, the previous token must be rejected."""
    user_id, token = _register_login(client, password="OldValidPass1234!")
    client.patch(f"/api/v1/users/{user_id}/password", json={
        "current_password": "OldValidPass1234!",
        "new_password": "NewValidPass5678!",
    }, headers=_headers(token))
    # Old token should now be invalid
    resp = client.get(f"/api/v1/users/{user_id}", headers=_headers(token))
    assert resp.status_code == 401


def test_change_password_wrong_current(client):
    user_id, token = _register_login(client)
    resp = client.patch(f"/api/v1/users/{user_id}/password", json={
        "current_password": "CompletelyWrong!!",
        "new_password": "NewValidPass1234!",
    }, headers=_headers(token))
    assert resp.status_code == 400
    assert resp.json["error"] == "invalid_current_password"


def test_change_password_missing_current(client):
    user_id, token = _register_login(client)
    resp = client.patch(f"/api/v1/users/{user_id}/password", json={
        "new_password": "NewValidPass1234!",
    }, headers=_headers(token))
    assert resp.status_code == 400
    assert resp.json["error"] == "current_password_required"


def test_change_password_too_short(client):
    user_id, token = _register_login(client)
    resp = client.patch(f"/api/v1/users/{user_id}/password", json={
        "current_password": "ValidPass1234!",
        "new_password": "short",
    }, headers=_headers(token))
    assert resp.status_code == 400
    assert resp.json["error"] == "password_too_short"


def test_change_password_other_member_forbidden(client):
    other_id, _ = _register_login(client)
    _, token = _register_login(client)
    resp = client.patch(f"/api/v1/users/{other_id}/password", json={
        "new_password": "NewValidPass1234!",
    }, headers=_headers(token))
    assert resp.status_code == 403


def test_admin_can_change_other_password_without_current(client):
    """Admin can reset any user's password without supplying current_password."""
    user_id, _ = _register_login(client)
    _, admin_token = _register_login(client, role="Administrator")
    resp = client.patch(f"/api/v1/users/{user_id}/password", json={
        "new_password": "AdminResetPass1234!",
    }, headers=_headers(admin_token))
    assert resp.status_code == 204


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# DELETE /users/{user_id} — Admin only, soft delete
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def test_delete_user_requires_admin(client):
    user_id, token = _register_login(client)
    resp = client.delete(f"/api/v1/users/{user_id}", headers=_headers(token))
    assert resp.status_code == 403


def test_ops_manager_cannot_delete(client):
    user_id, _ = _register_login(client)
    _, ops_token = _register_login(client, role="Operations Manager")
    resp = client.delete(f"/api/v1/users/{user_id}", headers=_headers(ops_token))
    assert resp.status_code == 403


def test_admin_soft_deletes_user(client):
    user_id, _ = _register_login(client)
    _, admin_token = _register_login(client, role="Administrator")
    resp = client.delete(f"/api/v1/users/{user_id}", headers=_headers(admin_token))
    assert resp.status_code == 204
    # User no longer visible
    resp2 = client.get(f"/api/v1/users/{user_id}", headers=_headers(admin_token))
    assert resp2.status_code == 404


def test_deleted_user_cannot_login(client):
    user_id, _ = _register_login(client, password="ValidPass1234!")
    # Need username — fetch it first
    _, admin_token = _register_login(client, role="Administrator")
    user_data = client.get(f"/api/v1/users/{user_id}", headers=_headers(admin_token)).json
    username = user_data["username"]

    client.delete(f"/api/v1/users/{user_id}", headers=_headers(admin_token))

    resp = client.post("/api/v1/auth/login", json={
        "username": username, "password": "ValidPass1234!",
    })
    assert resp.status_code == 401
"}

    client.delete(f"/api/v1/users/{user_id}", headers=_headers(admin_token))

    resp = client.post("/api/v1/auth/login", json={
        "username": username, "password": "ValidPass1234!",
    })
    assert resp.status_code == 401
"}
