1	"""Constraints, indexes, and immutability triggers
2	
3	Revision ID: 0002
4	Revises: 0001
5	Create Date: 2026-03-31
6	
7	What this migration adds on top of the baseline DDL (0001):
8	
9	CHECK CONSTRAINTS  (via batch_alter_table — SQLite requires full table recreation)
10	  users               ck_users_role
11	  commission_rules    ck_commission_rate_bounds, ck_commission_cycle
12	  settlement_runs     ck_settlement_status, ck_settlement_period
13	  settlement_disputes ck_dispute_status, ck_dispute_amount
14	  inventory_lots      ck_lot_costing_method, ck_lot_qty_nonneg
15	  inventory_transactions  ck_inv_txn_type, ck_inv_txn_adjustment_reason
16	  cost_layers         ck_cost_layer_qty, ck_cost_layer_cost
17	  avg_cost_snapshots  ck_avg_cost_nonneg, ck_avg_cost_qty
18	  cycle_count_lines   ck_cycle_count_variance_reason
19	  messages            ck_message_type, ck_message_single_target
20	  message_receipts    ck_receipt_status
21	  content_items       ck_content_type, ck_content_status
22	  content_versions    ck_cv_status, ck_cv_version_positive
23	  attachments         ck_attachment_mime, ck_attachment_size, ck_attachment_single_owner
24	  capture_templates   ck_template_status
25	  template_versions   ck_tv_status, ck_tv_version_positive
26	  template_migrations ck_tmig_from_positive, ck_tmig_forward_only
27	  admin_tickets       ck_ticket_type, ck_ticket_status
28	  audit_log           ck_audit_action_type
29	
30	UNIQUE CONSTRAINTS
31	  avg_cost_snapshots  uix_avg_cost_sku_wh   (sku_id, warehouse_id)
32	  message_receipts    uix_receipt_msg_recipient  (message_id, recipient_id)
33	
34	NEW INDEXES
35	  sessions            ix_sessions_user_id           (user_id)
36	  products            ix_products_price_usd         (price_usd)
37	  product_attributes  ix_product_attributes_product_id (product_id)
38	  product_tags        ix_product_tags_product_id    (product_id)
39	  cost_layers         ix_cost_layers_fifo           (sku_id, warehouse_id, received_at)
40	  cycle_counts        ix_cycle_counts_warehouse     (warehouse_id)
41	  cycle_count_lines   ix_ccl_cycle_count            (cycle_count_id)
42	  content_versions    ix_cv_content_id              (content_id)
43	  template_versions   ix_tv_template_id             (template_id)
44	  template_migrations ix_tmig_template_id           (template_id)
45	  settlement_runs     ix_settlement_community       (community_id)
46	  settlement_disputes ix_dispute_settlement         (settlement_id)
47	  commission_rules    ix_commission_community       (community_id)
48	  messages            ix_messages_expires_at        (expires_at)
49	
50	EXPRESSION INDEX (NULL-safe lot uniqueness)
51	  inventory_lots      uix_lot_location
52	      ON inventory_lots(sku_id, warehouse_id, COALESCE(bin_id,''), COALESCE(lot_number,''))
53	
54	SQLITE TRIGGERS
55	  trg_lot_costing_immutable     — prevents costing_method change after first transaction
56	  products_fts_ai / _au / _ad   — keeps products_fts FTS5 table in sync with products rows
57	
58	DOWNGRADE
59	  Drops all triggers, indexes, and expression index.
60	  Uses batch_alter_table to recreate affected tables without the check/unique constraints.
61	"""
62	from alembic import op
63	import sqlalchemy as sa
64	
65	revision = "0002"
66	down_revision = "0001"
67	branch_labels = None
68	depends_on = None
69	
70	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
71	# helpers
72	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
73	
74	_BATCH_NAMING = {"ix": "ix_%(column_0_label)s", "uq": "uq_%(table_name)s_%(column_0_name)s"}
75	
76	
77	def _batch(table: str):
78	    """Return a context manager for batch_alter_table (SQLite constraint recreation)."""
79	    return op.batch_alter_table(table, schema=None)
80	
81	
82	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
83	# upgrade
84	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
85	
86	def upgrade():
87	    # ---- new plain indexes (always safe; no data movement needed) ----------
88	
89	    op.create_index("ix_sessions_user_id", "sessions", ["user_id"])
90	    op.create_index("ix_products_price_usd", "products", ["price_usd"])
91	    op.create_index("ix_product_attributes_product_id", "product_attributes", ["product_id"])
92	    op.create_index("ix_product_tags_product_id", "product_tags", ["product_id"])
93	    op.create_index("ix_cost_layers_fifo", "cost_layers", ["sku_id", "warehouse_id", "received_at"])
94	    op.create_index("ix_cycle_counts_warehouse", "cycle_counts", ["warehouse_id"])
95	    op.create_index("ix_ccl_cycle_count", "cycle_count_lines", ["cycle_count_id"])
96	    op.create_index("ix_cv_content_id", "content_versions", ["content_id"])
97	    op.create_index("ix_tv_template_id", "template_versions", ["template_id"])
98	    op.create_index("ix_tmig_template_id", "template_migrations", ["template_id"])
99	    op.create_index("ix_settlement_community", "settlement_runs", ["community_id"])
100	    op.create_index("ix_dispute_settlement", "settlement_disputes", ["settlement_id"])
101	    op.create_index("ix_commission_community", "commission_rules", ["community_id"])
102	    op.create_index("ix_messages_expires_at", "messages", ["expires_at"])
103	
104	    # ---- expression index: NULL-safe lot uniqueness -----------------------
105	    # COALESCE turns NULL bin_id / lot_number into '' so that two rows with the
106	    # same sku+warehouse+no-bin+no-lot-number are correctly treated as duplicates.
107	    op.execute(
108	        "CREATE UNIQUE INDEX uix_lot_location "
109	        "ON inventory_lots(sku_id, warehouse_id, COALESCE(bin_id,''), COALESCE(lot_number,''))"
110	    )
111	
112	    # ---- CHECK constraints via batch_alter_table --------------------------
113	    # SQLite does not support ADD CONSTRAINT via ALTER TABLE; Alembic's
114	    # batch_alter_table handles this by recreating the table transparently.
115	
116	    with _batch("users") as b:
117	        b.create_check_constraint(
118	            "ck_users_role",
119	            "role IN ('Administrator','Operations Manager','Moderator',"
120	            "'Group Leader','Staff','Member')",
121	        )
122	
123	    with _batch("commission_rules") as b:
124	        b.create_check_constraint(
125	            "ck_commission_rate_bounds",
126	            "floor >= 0 AND ceiling <= 15 AND floor <= rate AND rate <= ceiling",
127	        )
128	        b.create_check_constraint(
129	            "ck_commission_cycle",
130	            "settlement_cycle IN ('weekly','biweekly')",
131	        )
132	
133	    with _batch("settlement_runs") as b:
134	        b.create_check_constraint(
135	            "ck_settlement_status",
136	            "status IN ('pending','processing','completed','disputed','cancelled')",
137	        )
138	        b.create_check_constraint(
139	            "ck_settlement_period",
140	            "period_end >= period_start",
141	        )
142	
143	    with _batch("settlement_disputes") as b:
144	        b.create_check_constraint(
145	            "ck_dispute_status",
146	            "status IN ('open','resolved','rejected')",
147	        )
148	        b.create_check_constraint("ck_dispute_amount", "disputed_amount >= 0")
149	
150	    with _batch("inventory_lots") as b:
151	        b.create_check_constraint(
152	            "ck_lot_costing_method",
153	            "costing_method IN ('fifo','moving_average')",
154	        )
155	        b.create_check_constraint("ck_lot_qty_nonneg", "on_hand_qty >= 0")
156	
157	    with _batch("inventory_transactions") as b:
158	        b.create_check_constraint(
159	            "ck_inv_txn_type",
160	            "type IN ('receipt','issue','transfer','adjustment')",
161	        )
162	        b.create_check_constraint(
163	            "ck_inv_txn_adjustment_reason",
164	            "type != 'adjustment' OR (reason IS NOT NULL AND reason != '')",
165	        )
166	
167	    with _batch("cost_layers") as b:
168	        b.create_check_constraint("ck_cost_layer_qty", "quantity_remaining >= 0")
169	        b.create_check_constraint("ck_cost_layer_cost", "unit_cost_usd >= 0")
170	
171	    with _batch("avg_cost_snapshots") as b:
172	        b.create_unique_constraint("uix_avg_cost_sku_wh", ["sku_id", "warehouse_id"])
173	        b.create_check_constraint("ck_avg_cost_nonneg", "avg_cost_usd >= 0")
174	        b.create_check_constraint("ck_avg_cost_qty", "on_hand_qty >= 0")
175	
176	    with _batch("cycle_count_lines") as b:
177	        b.create_check_constraint(
178	            "ck_cycle_count_variance_reason",
179	            "variance = 0 OR (variance_reason IS NOT NULL AND variance_reason != '')",
180	        )
181	
182	    with _batch("messages") as b:
183	        b.create_check_constraint(
184	            "ck_message_type",
185	            "type IN ('text','image_meta','file_meta','emoji','system')",
186	        )
187	        b.create_check_constraint(
188	            "ck_message_single_target",
189	            "NOT (recipient_id IS NOT NULL AND group_id IS NOT NULL)",
190	        )
191	
192	    with _batch("message_receipts") as b:
193	        b.create_unique_constraint(
194	            "uix_receipt_msg_recipient", ["message_id", "recipient_id"]
195	        )
196	        b.create_check_constraint(
197	            "ck_receipt_status",
198	            "status IN ('sent','delivered','read')",
199	        )
200	
201	    with _batch("content_items") as b:
202	        b.create_check_constraint(
203	            "ck_content_type",
204	            "type IN ('article','book','chapter')",
205	        )
206	        b.create_check_constraint(
207	            "ck_content_status",
208	            "status IN ('draft','published')",
209	        )
210	
211	    with _batch("content_versions") as b:
212	        b.create_check_constraint(
213	            "ck_cv_status",
214	            "status IN ('draft','published')",
215	        )
216	        b.create_check_constraint("ck_cv_version_positive", "version >= 1")
217	
218	    with _batch("attachments") as b:
219	        b.create_check_constraint(
220	            "ck_attachment_mime",
221	            "mime_type IN ('image/png','image/jpeg','application/pdf','text/plain','text/markdown')",
222	        )
223	        b.create_check_constraint(
224	            "ck_attachment_size",
225	            "size_bytes > 0 AND size_bytes <= 26214400",
226	        )
227	        b.create_check_constraint(
228	            "ck_attachment_single_owner",
229	            "(content_id IS NOT NULL) != (template_id IS NOT NULL)",
230	        )
231	
232	    with _batch("capture_templates") as b:
233	        b.create_check_constraint(
234	            "ck_template_status",
235	            "status IN ('draft','published')",
236	        )
237	
238	    with _batch("template_versions") as b:
239	        b.create_check_constraint(
240	            "ck_tv_status",
241	            "status IN ('draft','published')",
242	        )
243	        b.create_check_constraint("ck_tv_version_positive", "version >= 1")
244	
245	    with _batch("template_migrations") as b:
246	        b.create_check_constraint("ck_tmig_from_positive", "from_version >= 1")
247	        b.create_check_constraint("ck_tmig_forward_only", "to_version > from_version")
248	
249	    with _batch("admin_tickets") as b:
250	        b.create_check_constraint(
251	            "ck_ticket_type",
252	            "type IN ('moderation','report','other')",
253	        )
254	        b.create_check_constraint(
255	            "ck_ticket_status",
256	            "status IN ('open','in_progress','closed')",
257	        )
258	
259	    with _batch("audit_log") as b:
260	        b.create_check_constraint(
261	            "ck_audit_action_type",
262	            "action_type IN ('settlement','moderation','inventory','auth','content','template')",
263	        )
264	
265	    # ---- SQLite triggers ---------------------------------------------------
266	
267	    # Costing method immutability (design.md §3.5, questions.md Q8):
268	    # Fires BEFORE UPDATE OF costing_method and aborts if any transaction already
269	    # references this lot.
270	    op.execute("""
271	        CREATE TRIGGER trg_lot_costing_immutable
272	        BEFORE UPDATE OF costing_method ON inventory_lots
273	        WHEN EXISTS (
274	            SELECT 1 FROM inventory_transactions WHERE lot_id = OLD.lot_id LIMIT 1
275	        )
276	        BEGIN
277	            SELECT RAISE(ABORT,
278	                'costing_method_locked: cannot change after transactions exist');
279	        END
280	    """)
281	
282	    # FTS5 content-table sync triggers (design.md §3.4):
283	    # The products_fts virtual table uses content=products, so SQLite does NOT
284	    # automatically keep it in sync — these triggers are required.
285	    op.execute("""
286	        CREATE TRIGGER products_fts_ai
287	        AFTER INSERT ON products BEGIN
288	            INSERT INTO products_fts(rowid, name, brand, description)
289	            VALUES (new.rowid, new.name, new.brand, new.description);
290	        END
291	    """)
292	
293	    op.execute("""
294	        CREATE TRIGGER products_fts_au
295	        AFTER UPDATE ON products BEGIN
296	            INSERT INTO products_fts(products_fts, rowid, name, brand, description)
297	            VALUES ('delete', old.rowid, old.name, old.brand, old.description);
298	            INSERT INTO products_fts(rowid, name, brand, description)
299	            VALUES (new.rowid, new.name, new.brand, new.description);
300	        END
301	    """)
302	
303	    op.execute("""
304	        CREATE TRIGGER products_fts_ad
305	        AFTER DELETE ON products BEGIN
306	            INSERT INTO products_fts(products_fts, rowid, name, brand, description)
307	            VALUES ('delete', old.rowid, old.name, old.brand, old.description);
308	        END
309	    """)
310	
311	
312	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
313	# downgrade — reverses everything added above
314	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
315	
316	def downgrade():
317	    # Drop triggers first (no dependencies)
318	    op.execute("DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS trg_lot_costing_immutable")
319	    op.execute("DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS products_fts_ai")
320	    op.execute("DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS products_fts_au")
321	    op.execute("DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS products_fts_ad")
322	
323	    # Drop expression index
324	    op.execute("DROP INDEX IF EXISTS uix_lot_location")
325	
326	    # Drop plain indexes
327	    for ix in (
328	        "ix_sessions_user_id",
329	        "ix_products_price_usd",
330	        "ix_product_attributes_product_id",
331	        "ix_product_tags_product_id",
332	        "ix_cost_layers_fifo",
333	        "ix_cycle_counts_warehouse",
334	        "ix_ccl_cycle_count",
335	        "ix_cv_content_id",
336	        "ix_tv_template_id",
337	        "ix_tmig_template_id",
338	        "ix_settlement_community",
339	        "ix_dispute_settlement",
340	        "ix_commission_community",
341	        "ix_messages_expires_at",
342	    ):
343	        op.execute(f"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS {ix}")
344	
345	    # Remove check constraints and unique constraints via batch (table recreation)
346	
347	    with _batch("audit_log") as b:
348	        b.drop_constraint("ck_audit_action_type", type_="check")
349	
350	    with _batch("admin_tickets") as b:
351	        b.drop_constraint("ck_ticket_type", type_="check")
352	        b.drop_constraint("ck_ticket_status", type_="check")
353	
354	    with _batch("template_migrations") as b:
355	        b.drop_constraint("ck_tmig_from_positive", type_="check")
356	        b.drop_constraint("ck_tmig_forward_only", type_="check")
357	
358	    with _batch("template_versions") as b:
359	        b.drop_constraint("ck_tv_status", type_="check")
360	        b.drop_constraint("ck_tv_version_positive", type_="check")
361	
362	    with _batch("capture_templates") as b:
363	        b.drop_constraint("ck_template_status", type_="check")
364	
365	    with _batch("attachments") as b:
366	        b.drop_constraint("ck_attachment_mime", type_="check")
367	        b.drop_constraint("ck_attachment_size", type_="check")
368	        b.drop_constraint("ck_attachment_single_owner", type_="check")
369	
370	    with _batch("content_versions") as b:
371	        b.drop_constraint("ck_cv_status", type_="check")
372	        b.drop_constraint("ck_cv_version_positive", type_="check")
373	
374	    with _batch("content_items") as b:
375	        b.drop_constraint("ck_content_type", type_="check")
376	        b.drop_constraint("ck_content_status", type_="check")
377	
378	    with _batch("message_receipts") as b:
379	        b.drop_constraint("uix_receipt_msg_recipient", type_="unique")
380	        b.drop_constraint("ck_receipt_status", type_="check")
381

383	        b.drop_constraint("ck_message_type", type_="check")

1	import os
2	from flask import Flask
3	from sqlalchemy import event
4	from sqlalchemy.engine import Engine
5	import sqlite3
6	
7	from .config import config_map, Config
8	from .extensions import db, migrate, socketio, scheduler
9	from .errors import register_error_handlers
10	from .middleware import register_middleware
11	from .crypto import init_fernet
12	
13	
14	def create_app(config_name: str | None = None) -> Flask:
15	    if config_name is None:
16	        config_name = os.environ.get("FLASK_ENV", "development")
17	
18	    app = Flask(__name__, instance_relative_config=False)
19	    app.config.from_object(config_map.get(config_name, Config))
20	
21	    # --- Fernet encryption key ---
22	    init_fernet(app.config["FERNET_KEY_PATH"])
23	
24	    # --- Extensions ---
25	    db.init_app(app)
26	    migrate.init_app(app, db)
27	    async_mode = app.config.get("SOCKETIO_ASYNC_MODE", "threading")
28	    socketio.init_app(app, async_mode=async_mode)
29	
30	    # Enable WAL mode for SQLite
31	    @event.listens_for(Engine, "connect")
32	    def set_sqlite_pragma(dbapi_conn, connection_record):
33	        if isinstance(dbapi_conn, sqlite3.Connection):
34	            cursor = dbapi_conn.cursor()
35	            cursor.execute("PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL")
36	            cursor.execute("PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON")
37	            cursor.close()
38	
39	    # --- Middleware ---
40	    register_middleware(app)
41	
42	    # --- Blueprints ---
43	    _register_blueprints(app)
44	
45	    # --- Error handlers ---
46	    register_error_handlers(app)
47	
48	    # --- Background jobs ---
49	    if app.config.get("JOBS_ENABLED"):
50	        with app.app_context():
51	            _start_scheduler(app)
52	
53	    return app
54	
55	
56	def _register_blueprints(app: Flask) -> None:
57	    from .routes.health import health_bp
58	    from .routes.auth import auth_bp
59	    from .routes.users import users_bp
60	    from .routes.communities import communities_bp
61	    from .routes.commission import commission_bp
62	    from .routes.catalog import catalog_bp
63	    from .routes.search import search_bp
64	    from .routes.inventory import inventory_bp
65	    from .routes.messaging import messaging_bp
66	    from .routes.content import content_bp
67	    from .routes.templates import templates_bp
68	    from .routes.admin import admin_bp
69	
70	    # Health probes (no version prefix — standard convention)
71	    app.register_blueprint(health_bp)
72	
73	    v1 = "/api/v1"

75	    app.register_blueprint(users_bp, url_prefix=v1)
76	    app.register_blueprint(communities_bp, url_prefix=v1)
77	    app.register_blueprint(commission_bp, url_prefix=v1)

1	"""
2	Auth decorator — validates Bearer token and loads current_user into g.
3	Usage: @require_auth on any route handler.
4	"""
5	import hashlib
6	from functools import wraps
7	from datetime import datetime, timezone
8	from flask import request, g
9	from app.extensions import db
10	from app.models.user import User
11	from app.models.user import Session
12	from app.errors import UnauthorizedError
13	
14	
15	def _hash_token(raw: str) -> str:
16	    return hashlib.sha256(raw.encode()).hexdigest()
17	
18	
19	def load_user_from_token() -> User | None:
20	    auth = request.headers.get("Authorization", "")
21	    if not auth.startswith("Bearer "):
22	        return None
23	    raw_token = auth[7:]
24	    token_hash = _hash_token(raw_token)
25	    session = db.session.get(Session, token_hash)
26	    if session is None:
27	        return None
28	    if session.expires_at.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc) < datetime.now(timezone.utc):
29	        return None
30	    user = db.session.get(User, session.user_id)
31	    if user is None or user.deleted_at is not None:
32	        return None
33	    return user
34	
35	
36	def require_auth(f):
37	    @wraps(f)
38	    def decorated(*args, **kwargs):
39	        user = load_user_from_token()
40	        if user is None:
41	            raise UnauthorizedError("unauthorized", "Authentication required")
42	        g.current_user = user
43	        return f(*args, **kwargs)
44	    return decorated
45

{"file_path": "C:\TASK-105\repo\app\services\messaging_service.py", "content": """"Messaging service (REST fallback; WebSocket handled in app/websocket.py)."""
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta

from app.extensions import db
from app.models.messaging import Message, MessageReceipt, MESSAGE_TYPES, RECEIPT_STATUS_ORDER
from app.models.user import User
from app.errors import NotFoundError, AppError, ConflictError

_OFFLINE_TTL_DAYS = 7


class MessagingService:

    @staticmethod
    def send_message(data: dict, sender: User) -> Message:
        """Create a message and initial receipt (called from WebSocket and REST)."""
        msg_type = data.get("type", "text")
        if msg_type not in MESSAGE_TYPES:
            raise AppError("invalid_message_type",
                           f"type must be one of: {', '.join(MESSAGE_TYPES)}",
                           field="type", status_code=400)

        recipient_id = data.get("recipient_id")
        group_id = data.get("group_id")
        if recipient_id and group_id:
            raise AppError("ambiguous_target",
                           "Specify recipient_id or group_id, not both",
                           status_code=400)

        expires_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc) + timedelta(days=_OFFLINE_TTL_DAYS)
        msg = Message(
            type=msg_type,
            sender_id=sender.user_id,
            recipient_id=recipient_id,
            group_id=group_id,
            body=data.get("body"),
            file_metadata=data.get("file_metadata"),
            expires_at=expires_at,
            correlation_id=data.get("correlation_id", ""),
        )
        db.session.add(msg)
        db.session.flush()

        # Create a delivery receipt for direct messages
        if recipient_id:
            receipt = MessageReceipt(
                message_id=msg.message_id,
                recipient_id=recipient_id,
                status="sent",
            )
            db.session.add(receipt)

        db.session.commit()
        return msg

    @staticmethod
    def get_queued(user: User) -> list:
        """Return undelivered messages for this user (status='sent', not expired)."""
        receipts = (
            MessageReceipt.query
            .filter_by(recipient_id=user.user_id, status="sent")
            .join(Message)
            .filter(Message.expires_at > datetime.now(timezone.utc))
            .all()
        )
        return [r.message.to_dict() for r in receipts]

    @staticmethod
    def update_receipt(message_id: str, status: str, user: User) -> dict:
        """Advance a receipt status (sent → delivered → read, never backward)."""
        if status not in ("delivered", "read"):
            raise AppError("invalid_status", "status must be 'delivered' or 'read'",
                           field="status", status_code=400)
        receipt = MessageReceipt.query.filter_by(
            message_id=message_id, recipient_id=user.user_id
        ).first()
        if receipt is None:
            raise NotFoundError("message_receipt")

        current_order = RECEIPT_STATUS_ORDER.get(receipt.status, 0)
        new_order = RECEIPT_STATUS_ORDER.get(status, 0)
        if new_order <= current_order:
            raise ConflictError(
                "status_regression",
                f"Cannot transition from '{receipt.status}' to '{status}'",
            )

        receipt.status = status
        receipt.updated_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
        db.session.commit()
        msg = receipt.message.to_dict()
        msg["delivery_status"] = status
        return msg
"}

{"file_path": "C:\TASK-105\repo\app\jobs\message_redelivery.py", "content": """"
Retries undelivered messages with exponential backoff.
Purges messages past their 7-day expiry.

Backoff schedule (capped at 2 hours):
  attempt 0 → retry immediately
  attempt 1 → 60 s
  attempt 2 → 120 s
  attempt 3 → 240 s
  …
  attempt N → min(60 * 2^N, 7200) s
"""
import logging
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta

from app.extensions import db
from app.models.messaging import Message, MessageReceipt

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

_MAX_BACKOFF_SECONDS = 7200  # 2 hours


def _next_retry_delay(retry_count: int) -> int:
    """Return backoff in seconds for the given attempt number."""
    return min(60 * (2 ** retry_count), _MAX_BACKOFF_SECONDS)


def redeliver_messages() -> None:
    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)

    # 1. Purge expired messages (cascades to receipts via DB constraint)
    expired = Message.query.filter(Message.expires_at < now).all()
    for msg in expired:
        db.session.delete(msg)
    if expired:
        db.session.commit()
        logger.info({"event": "message_purge", "count": len(expired)})

    # 2. Redeliver pending receipts that are due (next_retry_at IS NULL or <= now)
    pending = (
        MessageReceipt.query
        .filter(
            MessageReceipt.status == "sent",
            db.or_(
                MessageReceipt.next_retry_at.is_(None),
                MessageReceipt.next_retry_at <= now,
            ),
        )
        .join(Message)
        .filter(Message.expires_at >= now)
        .all()
    )

    delivered = 0
    for receipt in pending:
        try:
            from app.extensions import socketio
            socketio.emit(
                "message",
                receipt.message.to_dict(),
                room=f"user_{receipt.recipient_id}",
                namespace="/ws/messaging",
            )
            delivered += 1
            logger.info({"event": "message_redelivery",
                         "message_id": str(receipt.message_id),
                         "attempt": receipt.retry_count})
        except Exception as exc:
            logger.warning({"event": "redelivery_error",
                            "message_id": str(receipt.message_id),
                            "error": str(exc)})

        # Advance backoff regardless of success/failure
        receipt.retry_count += 1
        delay = _next_retry_delay(receipt.retry_count)
        receipt.next_retry_at = now + timedelta(seconds=delay)

    if pending:
        db.session.commit()

    logger.info({"event": "redelivery_run", "attempted": len(pending), "emitted": delivered})

function.name: Write
function.arguments:
{"file_path": "C:\TASK-105\repo\app\jobs\message_redelivery.py", "content": """"
Retries undelivered messages with exponential backoff.
Purges messages past their 7-day expiry.

Backoff schedule (capped at 2 hours):
  attempt 0 → retry immediately
  attempt 1 → 60 s
  attempt 2 → 120 s
  attempt 3 → 240 s
  …
  attempt N → min(60 * 2^N, 7200) s
"""
import logging
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta

from app.extensions import db
from app.models.messaging import Message, MessageReceipt

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

_MAX_BACKOFF_SECONDS = 7200  # 2 hours


def _next_retry_delay(retry_count: int) -> int:
    """Return backoff in seconds for the given attempt number."""
    return min(60 * (2 ** retry_count), _MAX_BACKOFF_SECONDS)


def redeliver_messages() -> None:
    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)

    # 1. Purge expired messages (cascades to receipts via DB constraint)
    expired = Message.query.filter(Message.expires_at < now).all()
    for msg in expired:
        db.session.delete(msg)
    if expired:
        db.session.commit()
        logger.info({"event": "message_purge", "count": len(expired)})

    # 2. Redeliver pending receipts that are due (next_retry_at IS NULL or <= now)
    pending = (
        MessageReceipt.query
        .filter(
            MessageReceipt.status == "sent",
            db.or_(
                MessageReceipt.next_retry_at.is_(None),
                MessageReceipt.next_retry_at <= now,
            ),
        )
        .join(Message)
        .filter(Message.expires_at >= now)
        .all()
    )

    delivered = 0
    for receipt in pending:
        try:
            from app.extensions import socketio
            socketio.emit(
                "message",
                receipt.message.to_dict(),
                room=f"user_{receipt.recipient_id}",
                namespace="/ws/messaging",
            )
            delivered += 1
            logger.info({"event": "message_redelivery",
                         "message_id": str(receipt.message_id),
                         "attempt": receipt.retry_count})
        except Exception as exc:
            logger.warning({"event": "redelivery_error",
                            "message_id": str(receipt.message_id),
                            "error": str(exc)})

        # Advance backoff regardless of success/failure
        receipt.retry_count += 1
        delay = _next_retry_delay(receipt.retry_count)
        receipt.next_retry_at = now + timedelta(seconds=delay)

    if pending:
        db.session.commit()

    logger.info({"event": "redelivery_run", "attempted": len(pending), "emitted": delivered})
"}

Messaging REST endpoint tests.
Covers: send (direct/group), queue polling, delivery/read receipts,
        status progression enforcement, TTL, invalid inputs, auth.
"""
import uuid


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helpers
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _register_and_login(client, role="Member"):
    username = f"u_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]}"
    password = "ValidPass1234!"
    client.post("/api/v1/auth/register", json={
        "username": username, "password": password, "role": role,
    })
    token = client.post("/api/v1/auth/login", json={
        "username": username, "password": password,
    }).json["token"]
    return username, {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Send message
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def test_send_direct_message(client, auth_headers):
    """POST /messages with recipient_id → 201 + message body."""
    _, recip_headers = _register_and_login(client)
    recip_id = client.get("/api/v1/users/me", headers=recip_headers).json["user_id"]

    resp = client.post("/api/v1/messages", json={
        "type": "text",
        "recipient_id": recip_id,
        "body": "Hello!",
    }, headers=auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    d = resp.json
    assert d["type"] == "text"
    assert d["body"] == "Hello!"
    assert d["recipient_id"] == recip_id
    assert "message_id" in d


def test_send_group_message(client, auth_headers):
    """POST /messages with group_id → 201."""
    # Create a community first
    comm_resp = client.post("/api/v1/communities", json={
        "name": f"Grp-{uuid.uuid4().hex[:6]}",
        "address_line1": "1 A St",
        "city": "Austin",
        "state": "TX",
        "zip": "78701",
    }, headers=auth_headers)
    assert comm_resp.status_code == 201
    group_id = comm_resp.json["community_id"]

    resp = client.post("/api/v1/messages", json={
        "type": "text",
        "group_id": group_id,
        "body": "Group hello",
    }, headers=auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    assert resp.json["group_id"] == group_id


def test_send_both_targets_rejected(client, auth_headers):
    """recipient_id AND group_id together → 400."""
    _, recip_headers = _register_and_login(client)
    recip_id = client.get("/api/v1/users/me", headers=recip_headers).json["user_id"]
    comm_resp = client.post("/api/v1/communities", json={
        "name": f"C-{uuid.uuid4().hex[:6]}",
        "address_line1": "1 B St", "city": "Austin", "state": "TX", "zip": "78701",
    }, headers=auth_headers)
    group_id = comm_resp.json["community_id"]

    resp = client.post("/api/v1/messages", json={
        "type": "text",
        "recipient_id": recip_id,
        "group_id": group_id,
        "body": "Oops",
    }, headers=auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 400
    assert resp.json["error"] == "ambiguous_target"


def test_send_invalid_message_type(client, auth_headers):
    """Unknown type → 400."""
    _, recip_headers = _register_and_login(client)
    recip_id = client.get("/api/v1/users/me", headers=recip_headers).json["user_id"]

    resp = client.post("/api/v1/messages", json={
        "type": "video",
        "recipient_id": recip_id,
        "body": "hi",
    }, headers=auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 400
    assert resp.json["error"] == "invalid_message_type"


def test_send_requires_auth(client):
    """No auth → 401."""
    resp = client.post("/api/v1/messages", json={
        "type": "text",
        "body": "hi",
    })
    assert resp.status_code == 401


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Queue polling
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def test_get_queued_messages(client, auth_headers):
    """Recipient can poll undelivered messages."""
    _, recip_headers = _register_and_login(client)
    recip_id = client.get("/api/v1/users/me", headers=recip_headers).json["user_id"]

    # Send a message
    client.post("/api/v1/messages", json={
        "type": "text", "recipient_id": recip_id, "body": "Queue test",
    }, headers=auth_headers)

    # Recipient polls
    resp = client.get("/api/v1/messages", headers=recip_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    msgs = resp.json
    assert isinstance(msgs, list)
    assert any(m["body"] == "Queue test" for m in msgs)


def test_sender_has_empty_queue(client, auth_headers):
    """Sender doesn't see messages they sent (no receipt for themselves)."""
    _, recip_headers = _register_and_login(client)
    recip_id = client.get("/api/v1/users/me", headers=recip_headers).json["user_id"]

    client.post("/api/v1/messages", json={
        "type": "text", "recipient_id": recip_id, "body": "Sender check",
    }, headers=auth_headers)

    # Sender's own queue should be empty (they have no receipt)
    resp = client.get("/api/v1/messages", headers=auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    # The sent message should NOT appear in sender's queue
    assert not any(m["body"] == "Sender check" for m in resp.json)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Delivery / read receipts
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _send_to_recipient(client, sender_headers, recip_id):
    resp = client.post("/api/v1/messages", json={
        "type": "text", "recipient_id": recip_id, "body": "Receipt test",
    }, headers=sender_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    return resp.json["message_id"]


def test_receipt_delivered(client, auth_headers):
    """Recipient can mark a message as delivered."""
    _, recip_headers = _register_and_login(client)
    recip_id = client.get("/api/v1/users/me", headers=recip_headers).json["user_id"]
    mid = _send_to_recipient(client, auth_headers, recip_id)

    resp = client.post(f"/api/v1/messages/{mid}/receipt",
                       json={"status": "delivered"}, headers=recip_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert resp.json["delivery_status"] == "delivered"


def test_receipt_read(client, auth_headers):
    """Recipient can advance status to read."""
    _, recip_headers = _register_and_login(client)
    recip_id = client.get("/api/v1/users/me", headers=recip_headers).json["user_id"]
    mid = _send_to_recipient(client, auth_headers, recip_id)

    # delivered first
    client.post(f"/api/v1/messages/{mid}/receipt",
                json={"status": "delivered"}, headers=recip_headers)
    # then read
    resp = client.post(f"/api/v1/messages/{mid}/receipt",
                       json={"status": "read"}, headers=recip_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert resp.json["delivery_status"] == "read"


def test_receipt_backward_transition_blocked(client, auth_headers):
    """read → delivered must be rejected (409)."""
    _, recip_headers = _register_and_login(client)
    recip_id = client.get("/api/v1/users/me", headers=recip_headers).json["user_id"]
    mid = _send_to_recipient(client, auth_headers, recip_id)

    # advance to read
    client.post(f"/api/v1/messages/{mid}/receipt",
                json={"status": "read"}, headers=recip_headers)
    # try to go back
    resp = client.post(f"/api/v1/messages/{mid}/receipt",
                       json={"status": "delivered"}, headers=recip_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 409
    assert resp.json["error"] == "status_regression"


def test_receipt_sent_to_delivered_skip_ok(client, auth_headers):
    """sent → read (skip delivered) is allowed — just a forward jump."""
    _, recip_headers = _register_and_login(client)
    recip_id = client.get("/api/v1/users/me", headers=recip_headers).json["user_id"]
    mid = _send_to_recipient(client, auth_headers, recip_id)

    resp = client.post(f"/api/v1/messages/{mid}/receipt",
                       json={"status": "read"}, headers=recip_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert resp.json["delivery_status"] == "read"


def test_receipt_invalid_status(client, auth_headers):
    """status='sent' is not a valid target transition value."""
    _, recip_headers = _register_and_login(client)
    recip_id = client.get("/api/v1/users/me", headers=recip_headers).json["user_id"]
    mid = _send_to_recipient(client, auth_headers, recip_id)

    resp = client.post(f"/api/v1/messages/{mid}/receipt",
                       json={"status": "sent"}, headers=recip_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 400
    assert resp.json["error"] == "invalid_status"


def test_receipt_not_found(client, auth_headers):
    """Receipt for a message the user didn't receive → 404."""
    resp = client.post(f"/api/v1/messages/{uuid.uuid4()}/receipt",
                       json={"status": "delivered"}, headers=auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 404


def test_queue_cleared_after_delivered(client, auth_headers):
    """Messages marked 'delivered' no longer appear in the 'sent' queue."""
    _, recip_headers = _register_and_login(client)
    recip_id = client.get("/api/v1/users/me", headers=recip_headers).json["user_id"]
    mid = _send_to_recipient(client, auth_headers, recip_id)

    # Confirm message is in queue
    queue_before = client.get("/api/v1/messages", headers=recip_headers).json
    assert any(m["message_id"] == mid for m in queue_before)

    # Mark delivered
    client.post(f"/api/v1/messages/{mid}/receipt",
                json={"status": "delivered"}, headers=recip_headers)

    # Should not appear in 'sent' queue anymore
    queue_after = client.get("/api/v1/messages", headers=recip_headers).json
    assert not any(m["message_id"] == mid for m in queue_after)
"}

{"file_path": "C:\TASK-105\repo\tests\test_jobs.py", "content": """"
Background job tests.
Covers: safety stock alerts, slow-moving flags, attachment cleanup,
        message redelivery (purge + backoff), trending precompute.
"""
import os
import uuid
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Safety stock
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def test_safety_stock_creates_ticket(client, auth_headers):
    """Lot below threshold → AdminTicket created."""
    from app.jobs.safety_stock import check_safety_stock
    from app.models.admin import AdminTicket

    # Create warehouse + product + lot below threshold
    wh_id = client.post("/api/v1/warehouses",
                        json={"name": "WH-SS", "location": "A"},
                        headers=auth_headers).json["warehouse_id"]
    prod_id = client.post("/api/v1/products", json={
        "sku": f"SS-{uuid.uuid4().hex[:6]}", "name": "SafetyProd",
        "brand": "B", "category": "C", "price_usd": 1.0,
    }, headers=auth_headers).json["product_id"]

    # Receipt 5 units, set safety_stock threshold to 10
    client.post("/api/v1/inventory/receipts", json={
        "sku_id": prod_id, "warehouse_id": wh_id, "quantity": 5,
    }, headers=auth_headers)
    client.post(f"/api/v1/inventory/lots/{prod_id}/{wh_id}/safety-stock",
                json={"threshold": 10}, headers=auth_headers)

    with client.application.app_context():
        before = AdminTicket.query.filter(
            AdminTicket.target_type == "inventory_lot",
            AdminTicket.status == "open",
        ).count()
        check_safety_stock()
        after = AdminTicket.query.filter(
            AdminTicket.target_type == "inventory_lot",
            AdminTicket.status == "open",
        ).count()

    assert after > before


def test_safety_stock_no_duplicate_ticket(client, auth_headers):
    """Running the job twice doesn't create duplicate open tickets."""
    from app.jobs.safety_stock import check_safety_stock
    from app.models.admin import AdminTicket

    wh_id = client.post("/api/v1/warehouses",
                        json={"name": "WH-SS2", "location": "B"},
                        headers=auth_headers).json["warehouse_id"]

def test_attachment_cleanup_removes_soft_deleted(client, auth_headers, tmp_path):
    """Soft-deleted attachment DB row is hard-deleted; file removed from disk."""
    from app.jobs.attachment_cleanup import cleanup_attachments
    from app.models.content import Attachment
    from app.extensions import db
    import io

    # Create a content item
    content_id = client.post("/api/v1/content", json={
        "type": "article", "title": "Cleanup Test", "body": "body",
    }, headers=auth_headers).json["content_id"]

    # Upload a real file (use test client multipart)
    data = b"hello world"
    resp = client.post(
        f"/api/v1/content/{content_id}/attachments",
        data={"file": (io.BytesIO(data), "test.txt", "text/plain")},
        headers=auth_headers,
        content_type="multipart/form-data",
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    att_id = resp.json["attachment_id"]

    with client.application.app_context():
        att = db.session.get(Attachment, att_id)
        local_path = att.local_path
        # Write the file to disk so cleanup can remove it
        os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(local_path), exist_ok=True)
        with open(local_path, "wb") as f:
            f.write(data)

        # Soft-delete via route
        del_resp = client.delete(
            f"/api/v1/content/{content_id}/attachments/{att_id}",
            headers=auth_headers,
        )

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _make_user(client):
    username = f"u_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]}"
    client.post("/api/v1/auth/register", json={
        "username": username, "password": "ValidPass1234!", "role": "Member",
    })
    token = client.post("/api/v1/auth/login", json={
        "username": username, "password": "ValidPass1234!",
    }).json["token"]
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}
    user_id = client.get("/api/v1/users/me", headers=headers).json["user_id"]
    return user_id, headers


def test_redelivery_purges_expired(client, auth_headers):
    """Messages past 7-day TTL are hard-deleted by the redelivery job."""
    from app.jobs.message_redelivery import redeliver_messages
    from app.models.messaging import Message
    from app.extensions import db

    recip_id, _ = _make_user(client)
    client.post("/api/v1/messages", json={
        "type": "text", "recipient_id": recip_id, "body": "Expire me",
    }, headers=auth_headers)

    with client.application.app_context():
        # Backdate expires_at to past
        msg = Message.query.order_by(Message.sent_at.desc()).first()
        msg.expires_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(seconds=1)
        db.session.commit()
        mid = msg.message_id

        redeliver_messages()

        assert db.session.get(Message, mid) is None


def test_redelivery_backoff_respected(client, auth_headers):
    """Receipt with future next_retry_at is skipped during redelivery."""
    from app.jobs.message_redelivery import redeliver_messages
    from app.models.messaging import Message, MessageReceipt
    from app.extensions import db

    recip_id, _ = _make_user(client)
    client.post("/api/v1/messages", json={
        "type": "text", "recipient_id": recip_id, "body": "Backoff",
    }, headers=auth_headers)

    with client.application.app_context():
        msg = Message.query.order_by(Message.sent_at.desc()).first()
        receipt = MessageReceipt.query.filter_by(
            message_id=msg.message_id, status="sent"
        ).first()
        # Set next_retry_at far in the future
        receipt.next_retry_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc) + timedelta(hours=24)
        receipt.retry_count = 5
        db.session.commit()

        # Run redelivery — this receipt should be skipped
        redeliver_messages()

        db.session.expire(receipt)
        # retry_count should remain 5 (not incremented — was skipped)
        assert receipt.retry_count == 5


def test_redelivery_increments_retry_count(client, auth_headers):
    """Attempted delivery increments retry_count and sets next_retry_at."""
    from app.jobs.message_redelivery import redeliver_messages
    from app.models.messaging import Message, MessageReceipt
    from app.extensions import db

    recip_id, _ = _make_user(client)
    client.post("/api/v1/messages", json={
        "type": "text", "recipient_id": recip_id, "body": "Retry count",
    }, headers=auth_headers)

    with client.application.app_context():
        msg = Message.query.order_by(Message.sent_at.desc()).first()
        receipt = MessageReceipt.query.filter_by(
            message_id=msg.message_id, status="sent"
        ).first()
        assert receipt.retry_count == 0
        assert receipt.next_retry_at is None

        redeliver_messages()

        db.session.expire(receipt)
        assert receipt.retry_count == 1
        assert receipt.next_retry_at is not None
        # next_retry_at should be in the future (at least 60s from now)
        assert receipt.next_retry_at.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc) > datetime.now(timezone.utc)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Trending precompute
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def test_trending_precompute(client, auth_headers):
    """Searching a term multiple times makes it appear in trending cache."""
    from app.jobs.trending_precompute import precompute_trending
    from app.models.catalog import TrendingCache
    from app.extensions import db

    # Register a member to use search
    _, user_headers = _make_user(client)

    # Create a product so search works
    client.post("/api/v1/products", json={
        "sku": f"TR-{uuid.uuid4().hex[:6]}", "name": "TrendProd",
        "brand": "TrendBrand", "category": "Electronics", "price_usd": 9.99,
    }, headers=auth_headers)

    # Search for the term several times
    for _ in range(3):
        client.get("/api/v1/search/autocomplete?q=TrendProd", headers=user_headers)

    with client.application.app_context():
        precompute_trending()
        cached = db.session.query(TrendingCache).filter(
            TrendingCache.term.ilike("%trendprod%")
        ).first()
        assert cached is not None
        assert cached.score > 0
"}

def test_safety_stock_creates_ticket(client, auth_headers):
    """Lot below threshold → AdminTicket created."""
    from app.jobs.safety_stock import check_safety_stock
    from app.models.admin import AdminTicket

    # Create warehouse + product + lot below threshold
    wh_id = client.post("/api/v1/warehouses",
                        json={"name": "WH-SS", "location": "A"},
                        headers=auth_headers).json["warehouse_id"]
    prod_id = client.post("/api/v1/products", json={
        "sku": f"SS-{uuid.uuid4().hex[:6]}", "name": "SafetyProd",
        "brand": "B", "category": "C", "price_usd": 1.0,
    }, headers=auth_headers).json["product_id"]

    # Receipt 5 units, set safety_stock threshold to 10
    client.post("/api/v1/inventory/receipts", json={
        "sku_id": prod_id, "warehouse_id": wh_id, "quantity": 5,
    }, headers=auth_headers)
    client.post(f"/api/v1/inventory/lots/{prod_id}/{wh_id}/safety-stock",
                json={"threshold": 10}, headers=auth_headers)

    with client.application.app_context():
        before = AdminTicket.query.filter(
            AdminTicket.target_type == "inventory_lot",
            AdminTicket.status == "open",
        ).count()
        check_safety_stock()
        after = AdminTicket.query.filter(
            AdminTicket.target_type == "inventory_lot",
            AdminTicket.status == "open",
        ).count()

    assert after > before

Covers: versioning, publish/rollback, sanitized body storage, attachments (MIME, size limit, sha256), template migration requirement, RBAC.     """Lot below threshold → AdminTicket created."""
    from app.jobs.safety_stock import check_safety_stock
    from app.models.admin import AdminTicket
    from app.models.inventory import InventoryLot
    from app.extensions import db

    wh_id = client.post("/api/v1/warehouses",
                        json={"name": "WH-SS", "location": "A"},
                        headers=auth_headers).json["warehouse_id"]
    prod_id = client.post("/api/v1/products", json={
        "sku": f"SS-{uuid.uuid4().hex[:6]}", "name": "SafetyProd",
        "brand": "B", "category": "C", "price_usd": 1.0,
    }, headers=auth_headers).json["product_id"]

    # Receipt 5 units
    client.post("/api/v1/inventory/receipts", json={
        "sku_id": prod_id, "warehouse_id": wh_id, "quantity": 5,
    }, headers=auth_headers)

    with client.application.app_context():
        # Set safety_stock_threshold on the lot directly (no REST endpoint for this)
        lot = InventoryLot.query.filter_by(sku_id=prod_id, warehouse_id=wh_id).first()
        lot.safety_stock_threshold = 10  # on_hand=5 < threshold=10
        db.session.commit()

        before = AdminTicket.query.filter(
            AdminTicket.target_type == "inventory_lot",
            AdminTicket.status == "open",
        ).count()
        check_safety_stock()
        after = AdminTicket.query.filter(
            AdminTicket.target_type == "inventory_lot",
            AdminTicket.status == "open",
        ).count()

    assert after > before"}

    """Running the job twice doesn't create duplicate open tickets."""

    from app.models.admin import AdminTicket

    wh_id = client.post("/api/v1/warehouses",
                        json={"name": "WH-SS2", "location": "B"},
                        headers=auth_headers).json["warehouse_id"]
    sku = f"SS2-{uuid.uuid4().hex[:6]}"
    prod_id = client.post("/api/v1/products", json={
        "sku": sku, "name": "SafetyProd2", "brand": "B", "category": "C", "price_usd": 1.0,
    }, headers=auth_headers).json["product_id"]
    client.post("/api/v1/inventory/receipts", json={
        "sku_id": prod_id, "warehouse_id": wh_id, "quantity": 1,
    }, headers=auth_headers)
    client.post(f"/api/v1/inventory/lots/{prod_id}/{wh_id}/safety-stock",
                json={"threshold": 100}, headers=auth_headers)

    subject = f"Safety stock alert: SKU {prod_id}"
    with client.application.app_context():
        check_safety_stock()
        count_after_first = AdminTicket.query.filter_by(subject=subject, status="open").count()
        check_safety_stock()
        count_after_second = AdminTicket.query.filter_by(subject=subject, status="open").count()

    assert count_after_first == count_after_second == 1", "new_string": "def test_safety_stock_no_duplicate_ticket(client, auth_headers):
    """Running the job twice doesn't create duplicate open tickets."""
    from app.jobs.safety_stock import check_safety_stock
    from app.models.admin import AdminTicket
    from app.models.inventory import InventoryLot
    from app.extensions import db

    wh_id = client.post("/api/v1/warehouses",
                        json={"name": "WH-SS2", "location": "B"},
                        headers=auth_headers).json["warehouse_id"]
    prod_id = client.post("/api/v1/products", json={
        "sku": f"SS2-{uuid.uuid4().hex[:6]}", "name": "SafetyProd2",
        "brand": "B", "category": "C", "price_usd": 1.0,
    }, headers=auth_headers).json["product_id"]
    client.post("/api/v1/inventory/receipts", json={
        "sku_id": prod_id, "warehouse_id": wh_id, "quantity": 1,
    }, headers=auth_headers)

    with client.application.app_context():
        lot = InventoryLot.query.filter_by(sku_id=prod_id, warehouse_id=wh_id).first()
        lot.safety_stock_threshold = 100  # on_hand=1 < threshold=100
        db.session.commit()

        subject = f"Safety stock alert: SKU {prod_id}"
        check_safety_stock()
        count_after_first = AdminTicket.query.filter_by(subject=subject, status="open").count()
        check_safety_stock()
        count_after_second = AdminTicket.query.filter_by(subject=subject, status="open").count()

    assert count_after_first == count_after_second == 1"}

143/143 pass. Here's the delivery summary:

---

## 1. Implemented Services / Jobs

### Messaging
| Component | What was fixed / added |
|-----------|----------------------|
| `models/messaging.py` | Added `retry_count` (INT, default 0) + `next_retry_at` (DateTime) to `MessageReceipt`; added `RECEIPT_STATUS_ORDER` dict for progression enforcement |
| `services/messaging_service.py` | Added `type` + target validation to `send_message`; enforced monotonic status ordering in `update_receipt` (`sent→delivered→read`, never backward → 409 `status_regression`) |
| `routes/messaging.py` | Added `POST /messages` REST send endpoint |
| `websocket.py` | Fixed `_sid_user_map` population on connect (was never written); replaced `_auth.request` hack with direct SHA-256 token lookup; added `on_disconnect` handler to clean up `_sid_user_map` |
| `jobs/message_redelivery.py` | Added exponential backoff: each attempt increments `retry_count`, sets `next_retry_at = now + min(60×2^N, 7200)s`; job skips receipts where `next_retry_at > now` |

### Content / Templates
All were already correctly implemented. Tests revealed they work correctly.

### Background Jobs
All 5 jobs were already implemented. Added tests exposing/confirming behavior:

| Job | Schedule | Behavior verified |
|-----|----------|------------------|
| `message_redelivery` | every 1 min | purge expired → hard-delete; backoff respected; `retry_count` incremented |
| `trending_precompute` | every 15 min | `search_products` logs → TrendingCache populated |
| `safety_stock` | every 10 min | lot below threshold → AdminTicket created; no duplicates |
| `slow_moving` | daily 02:00 UTC | 60+ day no-issue → `slow_moving=True`; recent issue → not flagged |
| `attachment_cleanup` | daily 03:00 UTC | soft-deleted rows hard-deleted + file removed; orphan files on disk removed |

---

## 2. Scheduler Wiring

Already in `app/jobs/__init__.py` — `register_jobs(scheduler, app)` wraps each job in `_with_context(app, fn)` for correct app context, then registers with APScheduler triggers. Invoked from `app/__init__.py` when `JOBS_ENABLED=True`.

---

## 3. Data Integrity Safeguards

| Rule | Enforcement |
|------|-------------|
| Status monotonicity | `RECEIPT_STATUS_ORDER` dict + `ConflictError` on regression |
| WebSocket auth | `_load_user()` hashes token + validates expiry without touching HTTP request |
| SID cleanup | `on_disconnect` removes stale `_sid_user_map` entries (prevents stale user references) |
| Redelivery backoff | `next_retry_at` prevents hammering offline recipients; 2-hour cap |
| 7-day TTL | `expires_at` set at send time; redelivery job hard-deletes via cascade |
| Template non-additive change | `_requires_migration()` checks field removal + type changes; `UnprocessableError(migration_required)` blocks publish |
| Attachment constraints | MIME allowlist (5 types), 25 MB limit, sha256 computed server-side |
| Body sanitization | `bleach.clean()` with tag/attribute whitelist strips XSS-dangerous content |

---

## 4. Test Coverage Added

| File | Tests | Key scenarios |
|------|-------|--------------|
| `tests/test_messaging.py` | 14 | send direct/group, both-targets rejected, invalid type, queue polling, delivered/read receipt, backward transition blocked, skip-forward allowed, queue clears after delivery |
| `tests/test_jobs.py` | 9 | safety stock alert + no-duplicate, slow-moving flag + recent-issue skip, cleanup removes soft-deleted + orphaned files, redelivery purges expired + backoff skips + retry_count increments, trending precompute |
| `tests/test_content.py` | 22 (was 4) | body sanitization, rollback, list/get specific versions, attachment upload + sha256 + mime rejected + size limit + soft-delete, template additive publish (no migration needed), migration required on field removal + type change, create migration then publish |

    FERNET_KEY_PATH = os.environ.get("FERNET_KEY_PATH", "data/keys/secret.key")
    APP_VERSION = os.environ.get("APP_VERSION", "0.1.0")

    LOG_LEVEL = os.environ.get("LOG_LEVEL", "INFO")
    LOG_FILE = os.environ.get("LOG_FILE", "data/logs/app.jsonl")

    ATTACHMENT_DIR = os.environ.get("ATTACHMENT_DIR", "data/attachments")
    ATTACHMENT_MAX_BYTES = int(os.environ.get("ATTACHMENT_MAX_BYTES", 26_214_400))
    ATTACHMENT_ALLOWED_MIME = {"image/png", "image/jpeg", "application/pdf", "text/plain", "text/markdown"}

    JOBS_ENABLED = os.environ.get("JOBS_ENABLED", "true").lower() == "true"

    # Search
    SEARCH_HISTORY_CAP = 50
    SEARCH_PAGE_SIZE_MAX = 100


class DevelopmentConfig(Config):
    DEBUG = True
    SQLALCHEMY_ECHO = False


class TestingConfig(Config):
    TESTING = True
    SQLALCHEMY_DATABASE_URI = "sqlite:///:memory:"
    JOBS_ENABLED = False
    WTF_CSRF_ENABLED = False


class ProductionConfig(Config):
    DEBUG = False


config_map = {
    "development": DevelopmentConfig,
    "testing": TestingConfig,
    "production": ProductionConfig,
}

content.text:
"""
Structured JSON request/response logger.
Strips sensitive keys from any logged data: passwords, payout fields, message body.
Message.body is excluded at this layer so it never appears in log output even if
a future code path accidentally includes it in a loggable structure.
"""
import json
import os
import time
import uuid
import logging
from flask import Flask, g, request

# Keys redacted from any dict that passes through _redact().
# "body" covers Messages.body (chat content — must not be stored in logs).
REDACTED_KEYS = frozenset({
    "password",
    "password_hash",
    "current_password",
    "new_password",
    "body",          # Messages.body — chat content must not appear in logs
})


def _redact(obj, depth: int = 0):
    if depth > 5:
        return obj
    if isinstance(obj, dict):
        return {
            k: "[REDACTED]" if k in REDACTED_KEYS or k.startswith("payout_") else _redact(v, depth + 1)
            for k, v in obj.items()
        }
    if isinstance(obj, list):
        return [_redact(i, depth + 1) for i in obj]
    return obj


def init_logging_middleware(app: Flask) -> None:
    log_file = app.config.get("LOG_FILE", "data/logs/app.jsonl")
    log_level = app.config.get("LOG_LEVEL", "INFO")

    os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(log_file) or ".", exist_ok=True)
    handler = logging.FileHandler(log_file)
    handler.setLevel(log_level)
    app.logger.addHandler(handler)
    app.logger.setLevel(log_level)

    @app.before_request
    def start_timer():
        g.start_time = time.time()
        g.span_id = str(uuid.uuid4())

    @app.after_request
    def log_request(response):
        duration_ms = round((time.time() - getattr(g, "start_time", time.time())) * 1000, 2)
        user = getattr(g, "current_user", None)

        entry = {
            "timestamp": __import__("datetime").datetime.now(__import__("datetime").timezone.utc).isoformat(),
            "level": "WARN" if duration_ms > 100 else "INFO",
            "correlation_id": getattr(g, "correlation_id", ""),
            "span_id": getattr(g, "span_id", ""),
            "user_id": str(user.user_id) if user else None,
            "method": request.method,
            "path": request.path,
            "status_code": response.status_code,
            "duration_ms": duration_ms,
        }
        app.logger.info(json.dumps(entry))
        return response

3	The value is stored in Flask's g context so service/job code can access it.
4	"""
5	import uuid
6	from flask import Flask, g, request, Response
7	
8	
9	def init_correlation_middleware(app: Flask) -> None:
10	    @app.before_request
11	    def set_correlation_id():
12	        g.correlation_id = request.headers.get("X-Correlation-ID") or str(uuid.uuid4())
13	
14	    @app.after_request
15	    def add_correlation_header(response: Response) -> Response:
16	        response.headers["X-Correlation-ID"] = getattr(g, "correlation_id", "")
17	        return response

{"file_path": "C:\TASK-105\repo\scripts\seed.py", "content": """" Seed script — populates a fresh database with representative fixtures. Usage: cd repo/ python scripts/seed.py Idempotent: running twice is safe (duplicate registrations are silently skipped). """ import sys import os sys.path.insert(0, os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__)))) from app import create_app from app.extensions import db app = create_app("development") def _post(client, path, json=None, headers=None): resp = client.post(path, json=json or {}, headers=headers or {}) return resp def seed(): with app.app_context(): db.create_all() with app.test_client() as client: # ---------------------------------------------------------------- # 1. Users # ---------------------------------------------------------------- users = [ {"username": "admin", "password": "AdminPass1234!", "role": "Administrator"}, {"username": "opsmanager", "password": "OpsPass1234!", "role": "Operations Manager"}, {"username": "moderator", "password": "ModPass1234!", "role": "Moderator"}, {"username": "gl_alice", "password": "AlicePass1234!", "role": "Group Leader"}, {"username": "member_bob", "password": "BobPass1234!", "role": "Member"}, ] tokens = {} for u in users: _post(client, "/api/v1/auth/register", json=u) # 409 on re-run is safe resp = _post(client, "/api/v1/auth/login", json={"username": u["username"], "password": u["password"]}) tokens[u["username"]] = resp.json.get("token", "") print(f" user {u['username']:20s} token={tokens[u['username']][:16]}…") admin_h = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {tokens['admin']}"} # ---------------------------------------------------------------- # 2. Community # ---------------------------------------------------------------- comm_resp = _post(client, "/api/v1/communities", json={ "name": "Austin Community", "address_line1": "100 Main St", "city": "Austin", "state": "TX", "zip": "78701", }, headers=admin_h) if comm_resp.status_code == 201: community_id = comm_resp.json["community_id"] print(f" community {community_id}") # Service area _post(client, f"/api/v1/communities/{community_id}/service-areas", json={ "name": "Downtown", "address_line1": "200 Congress Ave", "city": "Austin", "state": "TX", "zip": "78701", }, headers=admin_h) # Bind group leader gl_resp = _post(client, "/api/v1/auth/login", json={"username": "gl_alice", "password": "AlicePass1234!"}) gl_token = gl_resp.json.get("token", "") # Get gl user_id by registering again to extract it reg = _post(client, "/api/v1/auth/register", json={"username": "gl_alice", "password": "AlicePass1234!", "role": "Group Leader"}) gl_uid = reg.json.get("user_id") if reg.status_code == 201 else None if gl_uid: _post(client, f"/api/v1/communities/{community_id}/leader-binding", json={"user_id": gl_uid}, headers=admin_h) # Commission rule _post(client, f"/api/v1/communities/{community_id}/commission-rules", json={ "rate": 8.0, "floor": 2.0, "ceiling": 12.0, "settlement_cycle": "weekly", }, headers=admin_h) # ---------------------------------------------------------------- # 3. Products # ---------------------------------------------------------------- products = [ {"sku": "LAPTOP-001", "name": "ProBook 15", "brand": "TechCo", "category": "Electronics", "price_usd": 999.99, "tags": ["laptop", "pro"], "attributes": [{"key": "ram", "value": "16GB"}]}, {"sku": "MOUSE-001", "name": "ErgoMouse", "brand": "TechCo", "category": "Electronics", "price_usd": 49.99, "tags": ["mouse", "ergonomic"]}, {"sku": "BOOK-001", "name": "Python Mastery", "brand": "PressHouse", "category": "Books", "price_usd": 34.99, "tags": ["python", "programming"]}, ] product_ids = {} for p in products: resp = _post(client, "/api/v1/products", json=p, headers=admin_h) if resp.status_code == 201: product_ids[p["sku"]] = resp.json["product_id"] print(f" product {p['sku']:20s} id={product_ids[p['sku']][:8]}…") # ---------------------------------------------------------------- # 4. content.name: Write
content.arguments:
{"file_path": "C:\TASK-105\repo\RUNBOOK.md", "content": "# Local Run Guide

## Requirements
- Python 3.12+
- `pip install -r requirements.txt` (or `pip install flask flask-sqlalchemy flask-migrate flask-socketio apscheduler bcrypt cryptography bleach marshmallow flake8 pytest`)

## First-time setup

```bash
cd repo/

# Generate Fernet encryption key
python - <<'EOF'
from cryptography.fernet import Fernet
import os
os.makedirs("data/keys", exist_ok=True)
with open("data/keys/secret.key", "wb") as f:
    f.write(Fernet.generate_key())
print("Key written to data/keys/secret.key")
EOF

# Create required directories
mkdir -p data/logs data/attachments
```

## Run dev server

```bash
FLASK_ENV=development python -m flask --app "app:create_app('development')" run --port 5000
```

## Seed database

```bash
python scripts/seed.py
```

## Run tests

```bash
python -m pytest tests/ -v
```

## Run lint

```bash
python -m flake8 app/ --max-line-length=120
```

## Key endpoints

| Method | Path | Auth | Description |
|--------|------|------|-------------|
| POST | `/api/v1/auth/register` | — | Register user |
| POST | `/api/v1/auth/login` | — | Login → Bearer token |
| GET  | `/health` | — | Liveness probe |
| GET  | `/health/ready` | — | Readiness probe (DB + scheduler) |
| POST | `/api/v1/messages` | Bearer | Send direct/group message |
| GET  | `/api/v1/messages` | Bearer | Poll offline message queue |
| GET  | `/api/v1/search/products?q=...` | Bearer | Full-text search |

## Observability

Every response includes `X-Correlation-ID` header (echo of request header or auto-generated UUID).

Structured JSON logs written to `data/logs/app.jsonl`:
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-31T18:00:00+00:00",
  "level": "INFO",
  "correlation_id": "uuid",
  "span_id": "uuid",
  "user_id": "uuid-or-null",
  "method": "POST",
  "path": "/api/v1/auth/login",
  "status_code": 200,
  "duration_ms": 42.1
}
```
Sensitive fields (`password`, `password_hash`, `body`, `current_password`, `new_password`, `payout_*`) are redacted to `[REDACTED]`.

## WebSocket messaging

Connect to `ws://localhost:5000/ws/messaging` with SocketIO:
```js
const socket = io("http://localhost:5000/ws/messaging", {
  auth: { token: "<bearer-token>" }
});
socket.emit("direct", { type: "text", recipient_id: "<uuid>", body: "Hello" });
socket.on("message", (msg) => console.log(msg));
```

## Background jobs (dev/prod only — disabled in tests)

| Job | Schedule | Purpose |
|-----|----------|---------|
| `message_redelivery` | every 1 min | Push pending receipts; exponential backoff; purge expired (7d TTL) |
| `trending_precompute` | every 15 min | Rebuild TrendingCache from last 7 days of searches |
| `safety_stock` | every 10 min | Flag lots below threshold → open AdminTicket |
| `slow_moving` | daily 02:00 UTC | Flag lots with no issue for 60+ days |
| `attachment_cleanup` | daily 03:00 UTC | Hard-delete soft-deleted attachments + orphaned files |
"}

{"file_path": "C:\TASK-105\repo\tests\test_observability.py", "content": """" Observability and API contract tests. Covers: X-Correlation-ID propagation, health endpoints, log field presence, response shape validation (required fields in all critical responses). """ import uuid # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Correlation ID propagation # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- def test_correlation_id_echoed(client): """Supplied X-Correlation-ID is echoed in the response header.""" cid = str(uuid.uuid4()) resp = client.get("/health", headers={"X-Correlation-ID": cid}) assert resp.headers.get("X-Correlation-ID") == cid def test_correlation_id_generated_when_absent(client): """Missing X-Correlation-ID gets a server-generated UUID in the response.""" resp = client.get("/health") cid = resp.headers.get("X-Correlation-ID", "") assert cid, "X-Correlation-ID header must be present" # Must be a valid UUID uuid.UUID(cid) # raises ValueError if invalid def test_correlation_id_on_error_response(client): """Error responses (404) also carry the correlation ID.""" cid = str(uuid.uuid4()) resp = client.get("/api/v1/nonexistent-path-xyz", headers={"X-Correlation-ID": cid}) assert resp.status_code == 404 assert resp.headers.get("X-Correlation-ID") == cid # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Health endpoints # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- def test_liveness(client): resp = client.get("/health") assert resp.status_code == 200 d = resp.json assert d["status"] == "ok" assert "version" in d assert d["db"] == "ok" def test_readiness(client): """Readiness probe: scheduler not running in test config → 503.""" resp = client.get("/health/ready") # In test mode JOBS_ENABLED=False so scheduler.running is False assert resp.status_code in (200, 503) if resp.status_code == 503: assert "scheduler" in str(resp.json.get("errors", [])) # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Auth response shapes # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- def _register_and_login(client): username = f"obs_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]}" password = "ObsPass1234!" reg = client.post("/api/v1/auth/register", json={"username": username, "password": password, "role": "Member"}) token = client.post("/api/v1/auth/login", json={"username": username, "password": password}).json["token"] return reg.json["user_id"], token, {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"} def test_register_response_shape(client): """POST /auth/register → {user_id, username, role, created_at}.""" resp = client.post("/api/v1/auth/register", json={ "username": f"s_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]}", "password": "ShapePass1234!", "role": "Member", }) assert resp.status_code == 201 d = resp.json for key in ("user_id", "username", "role", "created_at"): assert key in d, f"Missing field: {key}" def test_login_response_shape(client): """POST /auth/login → {token}.""" username = f"s2_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]}" client.post("/api/v1/auth/register", json={"username": username, "password": "ShapePass2_1234!", "role": "Member"}) resp = client.post("/api/v1/auth/login", json={"username": username, "password": "ShapePass2_1234!"}) assert resp.status_code == 200 assert "token" in resp.json def test_error_response_shape(client, auth_headers): """Error responses include {error, message} fields.""" resp = client.post("/api/v1/auth/login", json={ "username": "nobody_ever", "password": "WrongPass1234!", }) assert resp.status_code == 401 d = resp.json assert "error" in d assert "message" in d #       "test_observability":  {"passed": 18, "failed": 0},
      "test_users":          {"passed": 29, "failed": 0}
    }
  },

  "lint": {
    "tool": "flake8",
    "max_line_length": 120,
    "violations_before": 29,
    "violations_after": 0,
    "fixed": [
      "F401 unused imports in base.py, routes/auth.py, catalog.py, communities.py, search_service.py, template_service.py",
      "E741 ambiguous 'l' variable in inventory_service.py (3x) and search_service.py (1x) → renamed to 'layer', 'line', 'entry'",
      "E712 '== True' comparison in inventory_service.py → changed to .is_(True)",
      "E127/E261 continuation indent and inline comment spacing in audit_service.py, content.py",
      "E501 long lines in auth_service.py, community_service.py, search_service.py, template_service.py, user_service.py, rbac.py"
    ]
  },

  "implementation_decisions": {
    "auth": {
      "password_hashing": "bcrypt rounds=12; hash stored Fernet-encrypted at rest via EncryptedText TypeDecorator",
      "session_tokens": "secrets.token_hex(32), stored as SHA-256 hash in sessions table",
      "lockout": "5 failed attempts → locked_until = now + 15min; tracked in users.failed_attempts + users.locked_until",
      "role_hierarchy": "Administrator(6) > Operations Manager(5) > Moderator(4) > Group Leader(3) > Staff(2) > Member(1)"
    },
    "commission": {
      "precedence": "category rule > community default rule > system default (6.0%)",
      "idempotency": "create_settlement returns (settlement, created: bool); route maps to 201 or 409 both with full body",
      "rate_validation": "pre-apply all PATCH fields to local vars, validate, then write to model (prevents dirty-session autoflush hitting DB check constraint)"
    },
    "inventory": {
      "fifo": "_consume_fifo_layers() consumes CostLayer rows ordered by received_at ASC",
      "moving_average": "_update_avg_cost() writes/updates AvgCostSnapshot in place",
      "costing_method_lock": "DB trigger trg_lot_costing_immutable; also enforced in Python before DB write",
      "slow_moving_reset": "record_issue() sets slow_moving=False and last_issue_at=now"
    },
    "messaging": {
      "websocket": "Flask-SocketIO namespace /ws/messaging; auth via CONNECT frame 'token' field",
      "sid_map": "_sid_user_map populated on connect, cleaned on disconnect — prevents stale user references",
      "receipt_progression": "RECEIPT_STATUS_ORDER dict; ConflictError(status_regression) on backward transition",
      "redelivery_backoff": "retry_count + next_retry_at columns; delay = min(60 * 2^retry_count, 7200) seconds",
      "ttl": "7 days; expires_at set at send time; redelivery job hard-deletes via cascade"
    },
    "content": {
      "sanitization": "bleach.clean() with allowed_tags whitelist; strips script/style/onclick etc.",
      "versioning": "immutable ContentVersion rows; rollback changes current_version pointer only",
      "attachments": "SHA-256 computed server-side; MIME allowlist (5 types); 25 MB limit; soft-delete + daily cleanup job"
    },
    "templates": {
      "schema_evolution": "_requires_migration() detects field removal or type change; UnprocessableError if no TemplateMigration record before publish",
      "additive_allowed": "New optional fields do NOT require a migration record"
    },
    "observability": {
      "correlation_id": "X-Correlation-ID header echoed or auto-generated UUID; stored in flask.g; propagated to all log entries",
      "log_format": "Structured JSON to data/logs/app.jsonl; fields: timestamp, level, correlation_id, span_id, user_id, method, path, status_code, duration_ms",
      "redacted_keys": "password, password_hash, current_password, new_password, body, payout_*",
      "health": "GET /health (liveness: db check), GET /health/ready (readiness: db + scheduler)"
    },
    "database": {
      "engine": "SQLite WAL mode + foreign_keys=ON pragma",
      "encryption": "Fernet symmetric encryption for sensitive columns via EncryptedText TypeDecorator",
      "audit_log": "Append-only; DB triggers prevent UPDATE/DELETE; action_types: settlement, moderation, inventory, auth, content, template",
      "migrations": "3 Alembic migrations: 0001 baseline DDL, 0002 constraints/indexes/triggers, 0003 messaging retry columns"
    }
  },

  "files_created_or_modified": {
    "new": [
      "app/jobs/attachment_cleanup.py",
      "app/jobs/message_redelivery.py",
      "app/jobs/safety_stock.py",
      "app/jobs/slow_moving.py",
      "app/jobs/trending_precompute.py",
      "app/jobs/__init__.py",
      "migrations/versions/0003_messaging_retry.py",
      "scripts/seed.py",
      "RUNBOOK.md",
      "sessions/develop-1.json",
      "sessions/bugfix-1.json",
      "tests/test_messaging.py",
      "tests/test_jobs.py",
      "tests/test_observability.py"
    ],
    "modified": [
      "app/models/base.py (removed unused db import)",
      "app/models/messaging.py (added retry_count, next_retry_at, RECEIPT_STATUS_ORDER)",
      "app/models/content.py (comment spacing fix)",
      "app/routes/auth.py (removed unused g import)",
      "app/routes/catalog.py (removed unused g import)",
      "app/routes/communities.py (removed unused g, require_min_role imports)",
      "app/routes/messaging.py (added POST /messages send endpoint)",
      "app/services/auth_service.py (line length fix)",
      "app/services/audit_service.py (indent fix)",
      "app/services/commission_service.py (update_rule pre-validates before model mutation)",
      "app/services/community_service.py (line length fixes)",
      "app/services/inventory_service.py (rename l→layer/line, == True → .is_(True))",
      "app/services/messaging_service.py (type+target validation, receipt progression enforcement)",
      "app/services/search_service.py (removed unused datetime imports, renamed l→entry, line breaks)",
      "app/services/template_service.py (removed unused AppError import, line length fixes)",
      "app/services/user_service.py (line length fixes)",
      "app/middleware/rbac.py (line length fix for assert_self_or_elevated)",
      "app/websocket.py (fixed _sid_user_map, added disconnect, fixed token auth)",
      "tests/test_content.py (expanded from 4 to 22 tests)"
    ]
  }
}
"}
