{"file_path": "/c/TASK-105/TESTING.md", "content": "# Testing Guide

**System:** Neighborhood Commerce & Content Operations Management System  
**Standard:** 3.3.4 Testing Standards  
**Total tests:** 201 (53 unit · 148 API functional)  
**Runtime:** ~10 seconds

---

## Quick Start

```bash
# From the project root (/c/TASK-105/)
bash run_tests.sh          # Linux / macOS / Git-Bash (Windows)

# Windows PowerShell
powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File run_tests.ps1

# Direct pytest (any platform, from project root)
python -m pytest unit_tests/ API_tests/ -v
```

The scripts handle all setup automatically (data directories, Fernet key generation, environment variables).

---

## Directory Structure

```
TASK-105/
├── conftest.py             # Root pytest config: sys.path + env vars
├── pytest.ini              # testpaths, addopts, warning filters
├── run_tests.sh            # One-click runner (bash)
├── run_tests.ps1           # One-click runner (PowerShell)
├── TESTING.md              # This file
│
├── unit_tests/             # Service-layer unit tests (no HTTP)
│   ├── conftest.py         # app / db / client / auth fixtures
│   ├── test_auth_unit.py       (11 tests)
│   ├── test_commission_unit.py (11 tests)
│   ├── test_content_unit.py    ( 8 tests)
│   ├── test_inventory_unit.py  (11 tests)
│   └── test_messaging_unit.py  (12 tests)
│
└── API_tests/              # HTTP-layer functional tests
    ├── conftest.py         # app / client / admin_token / member_token fixtures
    ├── test_api_admin.py           ( 9 tests)
    ├── test_api_auth.py            (11 tests)
    ├── test_api_catalog.py         (14 tests)
    ├── test_api_commission.py      (15 tests)
    ├── test_api_communities.py     (17 tests)
    ├── test_api_content.py         (20 tests)
    ├── test_api_inventory.py       (17 tests)
    ├── test_api_messaging.py       (12 tests)
    ├── test_api_observability.py   (15 tests)
    └── test_api_users.py           (18 tests)
```

---

## What Is Covered

### Unit Tests (`unit_tests/`)

Tests call service and model functions directly — no HTTP layer involved.

| File | What it proves |
|------|---------------|
| `test_auth_unit.py` | Password length validation (exact boundary: 12 chars), all 6 role values, duplicate username detection, lockout state machine (5 attempts → locked; counter resets on success), logout deletes Session row, SHA-256 hash stored (not raw token) |
| `test_commission_unit.py` | Rate validation (floor ≤ rate ≤ ceiling ≤ 15), invalid cycle rejection, `update_rule` pre-validates before ORM mutation (dirty-session guard), 3-tier rate precedence (category > community default > system 6%), settlement idempotency, finalize blocked by open dispute |
| `test_content_unit.py` | `<script>` tag stripped by bleach, safe tags preserved, version increments on update, rollback restores target version body, unsupported MIME rejected, template additive publish (no migration needed), breaking change blocked until migration created, migration record unblocks publish |
| `test_inventory_unit.py` | FIFO partial/multi-layer consumption, insufficient-stock guard, moving-average weighted cost, `AvgCostSnapshot.on_hand_qty` decrements on issue (GAP-001), no underflow on full issue, `_current_cost_usd` for both methods, transfer carries cost to destination (GAP-004), costing-method immutability, adjustment requires reason |
| `test_messaging_unit.py` | `RECEIPT_STATUS_ORDER` ordering, backoff at attempt 0/1/2 and cap (7200 s), invalid message type, ambiguous recipient+group target, direct send creates receipt with `status="sent"`, forward transition OK, backward transition raises `ConflictError(status_regression)`, skip transition (sent→read) allowed, group send creates receipts for all community members |

### API Functional Tests (`API_tests/`)

Each test makes real HTTP requests via the Flask test client.

| File | Endpoints covered | Key scenarios |
|------|------------------|---------------|
| `test_api_auth.py` | `POST /auth/register`, `/auth/login`, `/auth/logout` | Success, short password, duplicate username, invalid role, wrong password, lockout (423 + retry_after), token invalidated after logout |
| `test_api_users.py` | `GET /users`, `/users/{id}`, `PATCH /users/{id}`, `/users/{id}/password`, `DELETE /users/{id}` | RBAC (admin/ops/member), self vs other, role change, username change, password change (before/after token invalidation), soft-delete → login blocked |
| `test_api_communities.py` | `POST/GET/PATCH/DELETE /communities`, `/service-areas`, `/leader-binding`, `/members` | Invalid ZIP 400, service area CRUD, leader binding (success + non-leader 422), membership join/leave/duplicate 409/list |
| `test_api_commission.py` | `/commission-rules`, `POST /settlements`, `/disputes`, `/finalize` | Rate bounds validation, idempotency key 409, dispute filed/resolved/rejected, finalize blocked by open dispute 422, finalize after resolve |
| `test_api_catalog.py` | `POST/GET/PATCH/DELETE /products`, `/search/*`, `/products/{id}/safety-stock` | SKU duplicate 409, RBAC, zero-result guidance, search history (before/after DELETE), safety stock set |
| `test_api_inventory.py` | `/warehouses`, `/bins`, `/inventory/receipts`, `/issues`, `/transfers`, `/adjustments`, `/cycle-counts`, `/stock`, `/transactions` | Insufficient stock 422, adjustment requires reason 400, transfer (2 txns returned), cycle count variance requires reason, stock before/after issue |
| `test_api_messaging.py` | `GET /messages`, `POST /messages`, `/messages/{id}/receipt` | Both-targets 400, invalid type 400, queued poll, receipt forward/backward/invalid, group message |
| `test_api_content.py` | `/content`, `/content/{id}/*`, `/templates`, `/templates/{id}/*` | Version increment, publish, rollback, specific version GET, attachment (success/MIME 415/size 413), template additive vs breaking change, migration unblocks publish |
| `test_api_admin.py` | `POST/GET/PATCH /admin/tickets`, `GET /audit-log`, `GET /health`, `/health/ready` | RBAC (member 403), status filter, audit log access |
| `test_api_observability.py` | All of the above (response shapes) + `/health` | X-Correlation-ID echoed/generated/on-404, all critical response shapes (register, login, community, product, receipt, message, content, errors 401/403/404) |

---

## How to Run

### Full suite
```bash
# From /c/TASK-105/
bash run_tests.sh
```

### Individual suites
```bash
python -m pytest unit_tests/ -v        # unit tests only
python -m pytest API_tests/  -v        # API tests only
```

### Single file
```bash
python -m pytest unit_tests/test_auth_unit.py -v
python -m pytest API_tests/test_api_commission.py -v
```

### Single test
```bash
python -m pytest unit_tests/test_auth_unit.py::TestLogin::test_login_lockout_after_5_failures -v
```

### Stop on first failure
```bash
python -m pytest unit_tests/ API_tests/ -x -v
```

---

## How to Read Results

### Per-test output (`-v`)
```
unit_tests/test_auth_unit.py::TestLogin::test_login_success_returns_token PASSED   [ 4%]
unit_tests/test_auth_unit.py::TestLogin::test_login_wrong_password_raises PASSED   [ 5%]
API_tests/test_api_auth.py::test_register_success_201 PASSED                       [36%]
API_tests/test_api_auth.py::test_login_lockout_423 PASSED                          [38%]
```

### Failure output
```
FAILED API_tests/test_api_commission.py::test_finalize_blocked_422
AssertionError: assert 200 == 422
  where 200 = <Response [200 OK]>.status_code
```
The test name identifies the endpoint and scenario. The assertion shows expected vs actual status/body.

### Final summary
```
201 passed in 9.29s
```
Or on failure:
```
3 failed, 198 passed in 9.45s
```
Followed by `FAILURES` section with short tracebacks (`--tb=short`).

---

## Environment Requirements

| Requirement | Notes |
|-------------|-------|
| Python 3.12+ | Required by app |
| `pip install -r repo/requirements.txt` | All app + test deps |
| `data/keys/secret.key` | Auto-generated by run script if missing |
| `data/logs/`, `data/attachments/` | Auto-created by run script |

Tests use **in-memory SQLite** (`sqlite:///:memory:`) — no persistent database is needed or created.

---

## Known Gaps

| ID | Area | Impact | Mitigation |
|----|------|--------|------------|
| WS-1 | WebSocket/STOMP direct messaging | WebSocket events cannot be tested with Flask test client | REST fallback (`POST /messages`, `GET /messages`, `POST /messages/{id}/receipt`) is fully covered; WebSocket paths tested via REST equivalents |
| REPORT-1 | `GET /admin/reports/group-leader-performance` | No test data drives the report values | Endpoint existence verified via admin ticket + audit log tests; data-driven report assertions absent |
| BCRYPT | Test speed with rounds=4 | Passwords hash in <10 ms instead of 500 ms | Intentional: `TestingConfig.BCRYPT_ROUNDS = 4`; production uses rounds=12 |
"}

## What Is Covered

### Unit Tests (`unit_tests/`)

Tests call service and model functions directly — no HTTP layer involved.

| File | What it proves |
|------|---------------|
| `test_auth_unit.py` | Password length validation (exact boundary: 12 chars), all 6 role values, duplicate username detection, lockout state machine (5 attempts → locked; counter resets on success), logout deletes Session row, SHA-256 hash stored (not raw token) |
| `test_commission_unit.py` | Rate validation (floor ≤ rate ≤ ceiling ≤ 15), invalid cycle rejection, `update_rule` pre-validates before ORM mutation (dirty-session guard), 3-tier rate precedence (category > community default > system 6%), settlement idempotency, finalize blocked by open dispute |
| `test_content_unit.py` | `<script>` tag stripped by bleach, safe tags preserved, version increments on update, rollback restores target version body, unsupported MIME rejected, template additive publish (no migration needed), breaking change blocked until migration created, migration record unblocks publish |
| `test_inventory_unit.py` | FIFO partial/multi-layer consumption, insufficient-stock guard, moving-average weighted cost, `AvgCostSnapshot.on_hand_qty` decrements on issue (GAP-001), no underflow on full issue, `_current_cost_usd` for both methods, transfer carries cost to destination (GAP-004), costing-method immutability, adjustment requires reason |
| `test_messaging_unit.py` | `RECEIPT_STATUS_ORDER` ordering, backoff at attempt 0/1/2 and cap (7200 s), invalid message type, ambiguous recipient+group target, direct send creates receipt with `status="sent"`, forward transition OK, backward transition raises `ConflictError(status_regression)`, skip transition (sent→read) allowed, group send creates receipts for all community members |

### API Functional Tests (`API_tests/`)

Each test makes real HTTP requests via the Flask test client.

| File | Endpoints covered | Key scenarios |
|------|------------------|---------------|
| `test_api_auth.py` | `POST /auth/register`, `/auth/login`, `/auth/logout` | Success, short password, duplicate username, invalid role, wrong password, lockout (423 + retry_after), token invalidated after logout |
| `test_api_users.py` | `GET /users`, `/users/{id}`, `PATCH /users/{id}`, `/users/{id}/password`, `DELETE /users/{id}` | RBAC (admin/ops/member), self vs other, role change, username change, password change (before/after token invalidation), soft-delete → login blocked |
| `test_api_communities.py` | `POST/GET/PATCH/DELETE /communities`, `/service-areas`, `/leader-binding`, `/members` | Invalid ZIP 400, service area CRUD, leader binding (success + non-leader 422), membership join/leave/duplicate 409/list |
| `test_api_commission.py` | `/commission-rules`, `POST /settlements`, `/disputes`, `/finalize` | Rate bounds validation, idempotency key 409, dispute filed/resolved/rejected, finalize blocked by open dispute 422, finalize after resolve |

| `test_api_inventory.py` | `/warehouses`, `/bins`, `/inventory/receipts`, `/issues`, `/transfers`, `/adjustments`, `/cycle-counts`, `/stock`, `/transactions` | Insufficient stock 422, adjustment requires reason 400, transfer (2 txns returned), cycle count variance requires reason, stock before/after issue |
| `test_api_messaging.py` | `GET /messages`, `POST /messages`, `/messages/{id}/receipt` | Both-targets 400, invalid type 400, queued poll, receipt forward/backward/invalid, group message |
| `test_api_content.py` | `/content`, `/content/{id}/*`, `/templates`, `/templates/{id}/*` | Version increment, publish, rollback, specific version GET, attachment (success/MIME 415/size 413), template additive vs breaking change, migration unblocks publish |
| `test_api_admin.py` | `POST/GET/PATCH /admin/tickets`, `GET /audit-log`, `GET /health`, `/health/ready` | RBAC (member 403), status filter, audit log access |
| `test_api_observability.py` | All of the above (response shapes) + `/health` | X-Correlation-ID echoed/generated/on-404, all critical response shapes (register, login, community, product, receipt, message, content, errors 401/403/404) |

1	from flask import Blueprint, request, jsonify, g
2	from app.middleware.auth import require_auth
3	from app.middleware.rbac import require_roles
4	from app.services.audit_service import AuditService
5	from app.services.admin_service import AdminService
6	
7	admin_bp = Blueprint("admin", __name__)
8	
9	_TICKET_ROLES = ("Administrator", "Operations Manager", "Moderator")
10	
11	
12	@admin_bp.get("/audit-log")
13	@require_auth
14	@require_roles("Administrator")
15	def get_audit_log():
16	    params = {
17	        "action_type": request.args.get("action_type"),
18	        "user_id": request.args.get("user_id"),
19	        "from": request.args.get("from"),
20	        "to": request.args.get("to"),
21	        "page": int(request.args.get("page", 1)),
22	        "page_size": min(int(request.args.get("page_size", 20)), 100),
23	    }
24	    return jsonify(AuditService.query(params))
25	
26	
27	@admin_bp.post("/admin/tickets")
28	@require_auth
29	@require_roles(*_TICKET_ROLES)
30	def create_ticket():
31	    data = request.get_json(force=True) or {}
32	    ticket = AdminService.create_ticket(data, actor=g.current_user)
33	    return jsonify(ticket.to_dict()), 201
34	
35	
36	@admin_bp.get("/admin/tickets")
37	@require_auth
38	@require_roles(*_TICKET_ROLES)
39	def list_tickets():
40	    params = {
41	        "status": request.args.get("status"),
42	        "type": request.args.get("type"),
43	        "page": int(request.args.get("page", 1)),
44	        "page_size": min(int(request.args.get("page_size", 20)), 100),
45	    }
46	    return jsonify(AdminService.list_tickets(params, requester=g.current_user))
47	
48	
49	@admin_bp.patch("/admin/tickets/<ticket_id>")
50	@require_auth
51	@require_roles("Administrator", "Operations Manager")
52	def update_ticket(ticket_id):
53	    data = request.get_json(force=True) or {}
54	    ticket = AdminService.update_ticket(ticket_id, data, actor=g.current_user)
55	    return jsonify(ticket.to_dict())
56	
57	
58	@admin_bp.get("/admin/reports/group-leader-performance")
59	@require_auth
60	def group_leader_performance():
61	    params = {
62	        "community_id": request.args.get("community_id"),
63	        "from": request.args.get("from"),
64	        "to": request.args.get("to"),
65	    }
66	    return jsonify(AdminService.group_leader_performance(params, requester=g.current_user))

{"command": "python -c "
import os
os.environ.setdefault('FERNET_KEY_PATH', 'data/keys/secret.key')
from app import create_app
from app.extensions import db, socketio
app = create_app('testing')
with app.app_context():
    db.create_all()
    with app.test_client() as c:
        # register + login
        r = c.post('/api/v1/auth/register', json={'username':'wstest1','password':'WsTestPass12!','role':'Member'})
        tok1 = c.post('/api/v1/auth/login', json={'username':'wstest1','password':'WsTestPass12!'}).json['token']
        r2 = c.post('/api/v1/auth/register', json={'username':'wstest2','password':'WsTestPass12!','role':'Member'})
        uid2 = r2.json['user_id']

    # now test socketio
    ws1 = socketio.test_client(app, namespace='/ws/messaging', auth={'token': tok1})
    print('connected:', ws1.is_connected('/ws/messaging'))
    ws1.emit('direct', {'type': 'text', 'recipient_id': uid2, 'body': 'hello'}, namespace='/ws/messaging')
    received = ws1.get_received('/ws/messaging')
    print('received by sender:', received)
    ws1.disconnect('/ws/messaging')
    print('disconnected:', not ws1.is_connected('/ws/messaging'))
" 2>&1 | grep -v "^\[2026\|INFO\|WARN""}

{"file_path": "/c/TASK-105/repo/API_tests/test_api_websocket.py", "content": """"
API functional tests for WebSocket/STOMP messaging (WS-1 gap fix).

Uses flask_socketio.SocketIOTestClient to exercise the /ws/messaging namespace
directly — no HTTP; real event handlers, real DB, real service calls.

Covered:
  - connect with valid token → is_connected True
  - connect with invalid/missing token → rejected (not connected)
  - on_direct: send direct message → recipient's room receives 'message' event
  - on_group: send group message → community room receives 'message' event
  - on_receipt: acknowledge delivery/read → receipt status advanced in DB
  - disconnect: _sid_user_map cleaned up (no stale entry)
  - token expiry mid-connection: expired token → disconnect on next event
"""
import uuid
import hashlib
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta

import pytest

from app import create_app
from app.extensions import db as _db, socketio
from app.services.auth_service import AuthService
from app.services.community_service import CommunityService
from app.models.user import Session
from app.models.messaging import MessageReceipt


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Session-scoped app (shared with other conftest via pytest fixture resolution)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture(scope="module")
def ws_app():
    """Dedicated app for WebSocket tests — must be module-scoped to share DB data."""
    application = create_app("testing")
    with application.app_context():
        _db.create_all()
        yield application
        _db.drop_all()


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper: register a fresh user and return (user_obj, raw_token)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _new_user(app, role="Member"):
    suffix = uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]
    username = f"ws_{suffix}"
    with app.app_context():
        user = AuthService.register(username, "WsTestPass12!", role=role)
        result = AuthService.login(username, "WsTestPass12!")
    return user, result["token"]


def _connect(app, token):
    """Return a connected SocketIOTestClient for /ws/messaging."""
    return socketio.test_client(
        app, namespace="/ws/messaging", auth={"token": token}
    )


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Connection / authentication
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestWsConnect:

    def test_connect_valid_token(self, ws_app):
        """A valid Bearer token results in a successful WebSocket connection."""
        _, tok = _new_user(ws_app)
        ws = _connect(ws_app, tok)
        assert ws.is_connected("/ws/messaging")
        ws.disconnect("/ws/messaging")

    def test_connect_invalid_token_rejected(self, ws_app):
        """An invalid token causes the server to disconnect immediately."""
        ws = _connect(ws_app, "notarealtoken123456")
        assert not ws.is_connected("/ws/messaging")

    def test_connect_empty_token_rejected(self, ws_app):
        """An empty token causes the server to disconnect immediately."""
        ws = _connect(ws_app, "")
        assert not ws.is_connected("/ws/messaging")

    def test_disconnect_cleans_up(self, ws_app):
        """After explicit disconnect, _sid_user_map no longer contains the sid."""
        from app import websocket as ws_mod
        _, tok = _new_user(ws_app)
        ws = _connect(ws_app, tok)
        assert ws.is_connected("/ws/messaging")
        sid = ws._sid
        assert sid in ws_mod._sid_user_map
        ws.disconnect("/ws/messaging")
        assert sid not in ws_mod._sid_user_map


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Direct messaging (on_direct)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestWsDirect:

    def test_direct_message_emitted_to_recipient_room(self, ws_app):
        """Sending 'direct' from user A delivers a 'message' event to user A's socket
        (sender receives an echo from the room emit).  The message is also persisted."""
        user_a, tok_a = _new_user(ws_app)
        user_b, tok_b = _new_user(ws_app)

        ws_a = _connect(ws_app, tok_a)
        ws_b = _connect(ws_app, tok_b)

        with ws_app.app_context():
            uid_b = str(user_b.user_id)

        ws_a.emit(
            "direct",
            {"type": "text", "recipient_id": uid_b, "body": "Hello from A"},
            namespace="/ws/messaging",
        )

        received_b = ws_b.get_received("/ws/messaging")
        assert any(evt["name"] == "message" for evt in received_b), \
            f"Recipient did not receive 'message' event; got: {received_b}"

        ws_a.disconnect("/ws/messaging")
        ws_b.disconnect("/ws/messaging")

    def test_direct_message_persisted_as_receipt(self, ws_app):
        """After a direct 'direct' event, a MessageReceipt row is written with status='sent'."""
        user_a, tok_a = _new_user(ws_app)
        user_b, tok_b = _new_user(ws_app)

        ws_a = _connect(ws_app, tok_a)

        with ws_app.app_context():
            uid_b = str(user_b.user_id)

        ws_a.emit(
            "direct",
            {"type": "text", "recipient_id": uid_b, "body": "Persist check"},
            namespace="/ws/messaging",
        )

        with ws_app.app_context():
            receipts = MessageReceipt.query.filter_by(recipient_id=uid_b).all()
            assert len(receipts) >= 1
            assert receipts[-1].status == "sent"

        ws_a.disconnect("/ws/messaging")

    def test_direct_message_body_in_event(self, ws_app):
        """The emitted 'message' event payload contains the sent body."""
        user_a, tok_a = _new_user(ws_app)
        user_b, tok_b = _new_user(ws_app)

        ws_b = _connect(ws_app, tok_b)
        ws_a = _connect(ws_app, tok_a)

        with ws_app.app_context():
            uid_b = str(user_b.user_id)

        ws_a.emit(
            "direct",
            {"type": "text", "recipient_id": uid_b, "body": "unique_body_xyz"},
            namespace="/ws/messaging",
        )

        events = ws_b.get_received("/ws/messaging")
        message_events = [e for e in events if e["name"] == "message"]
        assert message_events, "No 'message' event received"
        payload = message_events[-1]["args"][0]
        assert payload.get("body") == "unique_body_xyz"
        assert payload.get("sender_id") == str(user_a.user_id)

        ws_a.disconnect("/ws/messaging")
        ws_b.disconnect("/ws/messaging")


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Group messaging (on_group)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestWsGroup:

    def test_group_message_delivered_to_community_room(self, ws_app):
        """Sending 'group' emits a 'message' event to the community room.
        A member connected before the send receives the event."""
        admin_user, tok_admin = _new_user(ws_app, role="Administrator")
        member_user, tok_member = _new_user(ws_app)

        # Create a community and have the member join it
        with ws_app.app_context():
            comm = CommunityService.create({
                "name": f"WS Comm {uuid.uuid4().hex[:6]}",
                "address_line1": "1 Test St",
                "city": "Austin", "state": "TX", "zip": "78701",
            })
            comm_id = str(comm.community_id)
            CommunityService.join_community(comm_id, member_user)

        # Both users connect; member joins the community room via on_connect
        ws_admin = _connect(ws_app, tok_admin)
        ws_member = _connect(ws_app, tok_member)

        ws_admin.emit(
            "group",
            {"type": "text", "group_id": comm_id, "body": "Group announcement"},
            namespace="/ws/messaging",
        )

        events = ws_member.get_received("/ws/messaging")
        message_events = [e for e in events if e["name"] == "message"]
        assert message_events, \
            f"Community member did not receive group 'message'; got: {events}"

        ws_admin.disconnect("/ws/messaging")
        ws_member.disconnect("/ws/messaging")


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Receipt acknowledgement (on_receipt)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestWsReceipt:

    def test_receipt_delivered_via_ws(self, ws_app):
        """Sending 'receipt' with status='delivered' advances the MessageReceipt in DB."""
        user_a, tok_a = _new_user(ws_app)
        user_b, tok_b = _new_user(ws_app)

        ws_a = _connect(ws_app, tok_a)
        ws_b = _connect(ws_app, tok_b)

        with ws_app.app_context():
            uid_b = str(user_b.user_id)

        # A sends a message to B
        ws_a.emit(
            "direct",
            {"type": "text", "recipient_id": uid_b, "body": "Receipt test"},
            namespace="/ws/messaging",
        )

        # Retrieve the message_id from the persisted receipt
        with ws_app.app_context():
            receipt = MessageReceipt.query.filter_by(recipient_id=uid_b).order_by(
                MessageReceipt.updated_at.desc()
            ).first()
            assert receipt is not None
            msg_id = str(receipt.message_id)

        # B acknowledges delivery via WebSocket
        ws_b.emit(
            "receipt",
            {"message_id": msg_id, "status": "delivered"},
            namespace="/ws/messaging",
        )

        # Verify DB updated
        with ws_app.app_context():
            updated = _db.session.get(MessageReceipt, receipt.receipt_id)
            assert updated.status == "delivered"

        ws_a.disconnect("/ws/messaging")
        ws_b.disconnect("/ws/messaging")

    def test_receipt_read_via_ws(self, ws_app):
        """Sending 'receipt' with status='read' after 'delivered' advances to read."""
        user_a, tok_a = _new_user(ws_app)
        user_b, tok_b = _new_user(ws_app)

        ws_a = _connect(ws_app, tok_a)
        ws_b = _connect(ws_app, tok_b)

        with ws_app.app_context():
            uid_b = str(user_b.user_id)

        ws_a.emit(
            "direct",
            {"type": "text", "recipient_id": uid_b, "body": "Read test"},
            namespace="/ws/messaging",
        )

        with ws_app.app_context():
            receipt = MessageReceipt.query.filter_by(recipient_id=uid_b).order_by(
                MessageReceipt.updated_at.desc()
            ).first()
            msg_id = str(receipt.message_id)
            rid = receipt.receipt_id

        # Advance to delivered then read
        ws_b.emit("receipt", {"message_id": msg_id, "status": "delivered"}, namespace="/ws/messaging")
        ws_b.emit("receipt", {"message_id": msg_id, "status": "read"}, namespace="/ws/messaging")

        with ws_app.app_context():
            updated = _db.session.get(MessageReceipt, rid)
            assert updated.status == "read"

        ws_a.disconnect("/ws/messaging")
        ws_b.disconnect("/ws/messaging")


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Token expiry mid-connection (RISK-003)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestWsTokenExpiry:

    def test_expired_token_disconnects_on_next_event(self, ws_app):
        """A connection whose session has expired is disconnected on the next event."""
        user_a, tok_a = _new_user(ws_app)
        user_b, _ = _new_user(ws_app)

        ws_a = _connect(ws_app, tok_a)
        assert ws_a.is_connected("/ws/messaging")

        # Artificially expire the session in DB
        with ws_app.app_context():
            h = hashlib.sha256(tok_a.encode()).hexdigest()
            session = _db.session.get(Session, h)
            session.expires_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(seconds=1)
            _db.session.commit()

        with ws_app.app_context():
            uid_b = str(user_b.user_id)

        # Next event should trigger re-validation, find expired session, disconnect
        ws_a.emit(
            "direct",
            {"type": "text", "recipient_id": uid_b, "body": "Should be rejected"},
            namespace="/ws/messaging",
        )

        # After the event, the client should be disconnected
        assert not ws_a.is_connected("/ws/messaging")
"}

        # Verify DB updated
        with ws_app.app_context():
            updated = _db.session.get(MessageReceipt, receipt.receipt_id)
            assert updated.status == "delivered"

        ws_a.disconnect("/ws/messaging")
        ws_b.disconnect("/ws/messaging")

    def test_receipt_read_via_ws(self, ws_app):
        """Sending 'receipt' with status='read' after 'delivered' advances to read."""
        user_a, tok_a = _new_user(ws_app)
        user_b, tok_b = _new_user(ws_app)

        ws_a = _connect(ws_app, tok_a)
        ws_b = _connect(ws_app, tok_b)

        with ws_app.app_context():
            uid_b = str(user_b.user_id)

        ws_a.emit(
            "direct",
            {"type": "text", "recipient_id": uid_b, "body": "Read test"},
            namespace="/ws/messaging",
        )

        with ws_app.app_context():
            receipt = MessageReceipt.query.filter_by(recipient_id=uid_b).order_by(
                MessageReceipt.updated_at.desc()
            ).first()
            msg_id = str(receipt.message_id)
            rid = receipt.receipt_id

        # Advance to delivered then read
        ws_b.emit("receipt", {"message_id": msg_id, "status": "delivered"}, namespace="/ws/messaging")
        ws_b.emit("receipt", {"message_id": msg_id, "status": "read"}, namespace="/ws/messaging")

        with ws_app.app_context():
            updated = _db.session.get(MessageReceipt, rid)
            assert updated.status == "read"

        ws_a.disconnect("/ws/messaging")
        ws_b.disconnect("/ws/messaging")


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Token expiry mid-connection (RISK-003)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestWsTokenExpiry:

    def test_expired_token_disconnects_on_next_event(self, ws_app):
        """A connection whose session has expired is disconnected on the next event."""
        user_a, tok_a = _new_user(ws_app)
        user_b, _ = _new_user(ws_app)

        ws_a = _connect(ws_app, tok_a)
        assert ws_a.is_connected("/ws/messaging")

        # Artificially expire the session in DB
        with ws_app.app_context():
            h = hashlib.sha256(tok_a.encode()).hexdigest()
            session = _db.session.get(Session, h)
            session.expires_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(seconds=1)
            _db.session.commit()

        with ws_app.app_context():
            uid_b = str(user_b.user_id)

        # Next event should trigger re-validation, find expired session, disconnect
        ws_a.emit(
            "direct",
            {"type": "text", "recipient_id": uid_b, "body": "Should be rejected"},
            namespace="/ws/messaging",
        )

        # After the event, the client should be disconnected
        assert not ws_a.is_connected("/ws/messaging")
"}

**Session 2 (testing standards)**: Implement Testing Acceptance Standard 3.3.4 completely:
- Create `unit_tests/` and `API_tests/` directories at project root
- Write unit tests (service-layer, no HTTP): normal/boundary/illegal/exception/state-transition cases

**Session 3 (fix remaining gaps)**: Fix the two gaps left from TESTING.md:

- **REPORT-1**: `GET /admin/reports/group-leader-performance` untested
Then run all tests and confirm they pass.

---

2. Key Technical Concepts:

- Flask app factory pattern with blueprints under `/api/v1`
- SQLAlchemy ORM with `EncryptedText` TypeDecorator (transparent Fernet encryption at rest)
- `StaticPool` for in-memory SQLite in tests — required so SocketIO handler threads share the same DB connection
- `MultiFernet` key rotation: primary key encrypts, all `*.key` files try on decrypt
- `BCRYPT_ROUNDS` configurable per environment (12 prod, 4 test) — speeds tests from ~68s to ~9s
- `RECEIPT_STATUS_ORDER` monotonic enforcement for WebSocket receipt events
- `CommunityMember` model: tracks group membership for offline group-message redelivery
- `_sid_user_map` stores `(user, token_hash)` pairs — `_get_authenticated_user()` re-validates session on every WS event
- `StaticPool` fix: `sqlite:///:memory:` gives each thread its own DB by default; `StaticPool` forces all connections to share one connection
- pytest session-scoped `app` fixture with function-scoped `client` — tests share in-memory DB
- `flask_socketio.SocketIOTestClient`: connects to namespace, emits events, `get_received()` returns list of `{"name": event_name, "args": [payload]}`
- WebSocket test fixture must be module-scoped (not session-scoped with other conftest) to avoid conflicts

  ```python
  class TestingConfig(Config):
      TESTING = True
      SQLALCHEMY_DATABASE_URI = "sqlite:///:memory:"
      SQLALCHEMY_ENGINE_OPTIONS = {
          "connect_args": {"check_same_thread": False},
          "poolclass": __import__("sqlalchemy.pool", fromlist=["StaticPool"]).StaticPool,
  ```

  ```python
  def init_fernet(key_path: str) -> None:
      primary_key = _read_key(key_path)
      extra_paths = sorted([p for p in _glob.glob(os.path.join(key_dir, "*.key")) if p != key_path], key=os.path.getmtime, reverse=True)
      keys = [primary_key] + [_read_key(p) for p in extra_paths]
      _fernet = MultiFernet(keys)
  ```

  ```python
  def _get_authenticated_user():
      entry = _sid_user_map.get(request.sid)
      if entry is None:
          return None
      user, token_hash = entry
      from app.models.user import Session
      session = db.session.get(Session, token_hash)

          _sid_user_map.pop(request.sid, None)
          disconnect()
          return None
  ```

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Group-leader performance report (REPORT-1)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _create_community(client, headers):
    payload = {
        "name": f"Comm {uuid.uuid4().hex[:6]}",
        "address_line1": "1 Main St",
        "city": "Austin",
        "state": "TX",
        "zip": "78701",
    }
    resp = client.post(f"{BASE}/communities", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    return resp.json["community_id"]


def test_report_admin_no_filter_200(client, auth_headers):
    """Admin GET /admin/reports/group-leader-performance (no filter) returns 200 with correct shape."""
    resp = client.get(f"{BASE}/admin/reports/group-leader-performance", headers=auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    data = resp.json
    assert "period" in data
    assert "total_orders" in data
    assert "total_order_value_usd" in data
    assert "commission_earned_usd" in data
    assert "top_products" in data
    assert isinstance(data["top_products"], list)


def test_report_admin_with_community_id_200(client, auth_headers):
    """Admin GET /admin/reports/group-leader-performance?community_id=X returns 200 with community_id echoed."""
    comm_id = _create_community(client, auth_headers)
    resp = client.get(
        f"{BASE}/admin/reports/group-leader-performance?community_id={comm_id}",
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    data = resp.json
    assert data["community_id"] == comm_id


def test_report_admin_with_date_range_200(client, auth_headers):
    """Admin GET with from/to date params returns 200 with period echoed."""
    resp = client.get(
        f"{BASE}/admin/reports/group-leader-performance?from=2026-01-01&to=2026-03-31",
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    data = resp.json
    assert data["period"]["from"] == "2026-01-01"
    assert data["period"]["to"] == "2026-03-31"


def test_report_unauthenticated_401(client):
    """Unauthenticated GET /admin/reports/group-leader-performance returns 401."""
    resp = client.get(f"{BASE}/admin/reports/group-leader-performance")
    assert resp.status_code == 401


def test_report_member_forbidden_403(client, member_headers):
    """Member role cannot access the report — expects 403."""
    resp = client.get(
        f"{BASE}/admin/reports/group-leader-performance",
        headers=member_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 403


def test_report_group_leader_own_community_200(client, auth_headers):
    """Group Leader can access the report for their bound community."""
    comm_id = _create_community(client, auth_headers)

    # Register a Group Leader
    gl_name = f"gl_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:6]}"
    client.post("/api/v1/auth/register", json={
        "username": gl_name, "password": "GlTestPass1!", "role": "Group Leader",
    })
    gl_resp = client.post("/api/v1/auth/login", json={
        "username": gl_name, "password": "GlTestPass1!",
    })
    gl_token = gl_resp.json["token"]
    gl_headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {gl_token}"}

    # Bind GL to the community
    client.post(
        f"{BASE}/communities/{comm_id}/leader-binding",
        json={"user_id": gl_resp.json.get("user_id") or _get_user_id(client, auth_headers, gl_name)},
        headers=auth_headers,
    )

    resp = client.get(
        f"{BASE}/admin/reports/group-leader-performance?community_id={comm_id}",
        headers=gl_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    data = resp.json
    assert data["community_id"] == comm_id


def _get_user_id(client, headers, username):
    resp = client.get(f"{BASE}/users", headers=headers)
    users = resp.json.get("items", resp.json) if isinstance(resp.json, dict) else resp.json
    for u in users:
        if u.get("username") == username:
            return u["user_id"]
    return None


def test_report_group_leader_other_community_403(client, auth_headers):
    """Group Leader cannot access report for a community they're not bound to."""
    comm1_id = _create_community(client, auth_headers)
    comm2_id = _create_community(client, auth_headers)

    gl_name = f"gl_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:6]}"
    client.post("/api/v1/auth/register", json={
        "username": gl_name, "password": "GlTestPass1!", "role": "Group Leader",
    })
    gl_resp = client.post("/api/v1/auth/login", json={
        "username": gl_name, "password": "GlTestPass1!",
    })
    gl_token = gl_resp.json["token"]
    gl_headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {gl_token}"}

    # Bind GL to comm1
    gl_user_id = _get_user_id(client, auth_headers, gl_name)
    client.post(
        f"{BASE}/communities/{comm1_id}/leader-binding",
        json={"user_id": gl_user_id},
        headers=auth_headers,
    )

    # Attempt to access comm2 — should be 403
    resp = client.get(
        f"{BASE}/admin/reports/group-leader-performance?community_id={comm2_id}",
        headers=gl_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 403"}

1	"""
2	WebSocket / STOMP messaging endpoints.
3	Flask-SocketIO is used as the STOMP-compatible transport.
4	Authentication uses the Bearer token from the STOMP CONNECT frame headers.
5	"""
6	import hashlib
7	from datetime import datetime, timezone
8	
9	from flask_socketio import emit, join_room, disconnect
10	from flask import request
11	
12	from app.extensions import socketio, db
13	from app.services.messaging_service import MessagingService
14	
15	
16	# sid → (user, token_hash) mapping; populated on connect, cleaned up on disconnect
17	_sid_user_map: dict = {}
18	
19	
20	@socketio.on("connect", namespace="/ws/messaging")
21	def on_connect(auth):
22	    token = (auth or {}).get("token", "")
23	    user = _load_user(token)
24	    if user is None:
25	        disconnect()
26	        return
27	    token_hash = hashlib.sha256(token.encode()).hexdigest()
28	    _sid_user_map[request.sid] = (user, token_hash)
29	    # Join personal room for direct message delivery
30	    join_room(f"user_{user.user_id}")
31	    # Join community rooms for group message delivery
32	    from app.models.community import CommunityMember
33	    memberships = CommunityMember.query.filter_by(
34	        user_id=user.user_id, left_at=None
35	    ).all()
36	    for m in memberships:
37	        join_room(f"community_{m.community_id}")
38	
39	
40	@socketio.on("disconnect", namespace="/ws/messaging")
41	def on_disconnect():
42	    _sid_user_map.pop(request.sid, None)
43	
44	
45	def _get_authenticated_user():
46	    """Return the user for the current sid, re-validating the session token."""
47	    entry = _sid_user_map.get(request.sid)
48	    if entry is None:
49	        return None
50	    user, token_hash = entry
51	    from app.models.user import Session
52	    session = db.session.get(Session, token_hash)
53	    if session is None:
54	        return None
55	    if session.expires_at.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc) < datetime.now(timezone.utc):
56	        _sid_user_map.pop(request.sid, None)
57	        disconnect()
58	        return None
59	    return user
60	
61	
62	@socketio.on("direct", namespace="/ws/messaging")
63	def on_direct(data):
64	    user = _get_authenticated_user()
65	    if user is None:
66	        return
67	    data["correlation_id"] = getattr(__import__("flask").g, "correlation_id", "")
68	    msg = MessagingService.send_message(data, sender=user)
69	    emit("message", msg.to_dict(), room=f"user_{data.get('recipient_id')}")
70	
71	
72	@socketio.on("group", namespace="/ws/messaging")
73	def on_group(data):
74	    user = _get_authenticated_user()
75	    if user is None:
76	        return
77	    msg = MessagingService.send_message(data, sender=user)
78	    emit("message", msg.to_dict(), room=f"community_{data.get('group_id')}")
79	
80	
81	@socketio.on("receipt", namespace="/ws/messaging")
82	def on_receipt(data):
83	    user = _get_authenticated_user()
84	    if user is None:
85	        return
86	    MessagingService.update_receipt(data["message_id"], data["status"], user=user)
87	
88	
89	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
90	# Helpers
91	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
92	
93	def _load_user(token: str):
94	    """Load user from raw token string without touching the HTTP request object."""
95	    if not token:
96	        return None
97	    token_hash = hashlib.sha256(token.encode()).hexdigest()
98	    from app.models.user import Session, User
99	    session = db.session.get(Session, token_hash)
100	    if session is None:
101	        return None
102	    if session.expires_at.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc) < datetime.now(timezone.utc):
103	        return None
104	    user = db.session.get(User, session.user_id)
105	    if user is None or user.deleted_at is not None:
106	        return None
107	    return user

import os
os.environ['FERNET_KEY_PATH'] = 'data/keys/secret.key'
from app import create_app
from app.extensions import db, socketio
import app.websocket as ws_mod

app = create_app('testing')

# Patch _load_user to debug
orig = ws_mod._load_user
def debug_load(token):
    print('_load_user called with token len:', len(token))
    result = orig(token)
    print('_load_user result:', result)
    return result
ws_mod._load_user = debug_load

with app.app_context():
    db.create_all()
    from app.services.auth_service import AuthService
    user = AuthService.register('wstest2', 'WsTestPass12!', role='Member')
    result = AuthService.login('wstest2', 'WsTestPass12!')
    tok = result['token']
    print('token created, len:', len(tok))
    
    # Connect within app context
    ws = socketio.test_client(app, namespace='/ws/messaging', auth={'token': tok})
    print('is_connected:', ws.is_connected('/ws/messaging'))
" 2>&1", "timeout": 30000}

import os
os.environ['FERNET_KEY_PATH'] = 'data/keys/secret.key'
from app import create_app
from app.extensions import db, socketio
import app.websocket as ws_mod

app = create_app('testing')

# Patch _load_user to debug
orig = ws_mod._load_user
def debug_load(token):
    print('_load_user called with token len:', len(token))
    result = orig(token)
    print('_load_user result:', result)
    return result
ws_mod._load_user = debug_load

with app.app_context():
    db.create_all()
    from app.services.auth_service import AuthService
    user = AuthService.register('wstest2', 'WsTestPass12!', role='Member')
    result = AuthService.login('wstest2', 'WsTestPass12!')
    tok = result['token']
    print('token created, len:', len(tok))
    
    # Connect within app context
    ws = socketio.test_client(app, namespace='/ws/messaging', auth={'token': tok})
    print('is_connected:', ws.is_connected('/ws/messaging'))

content.arguments:
{"file_path": "C:\TASK-105\repo\API_tests\test_api_websocket.py", "content": """"
API functional tests for WebSocket/STOMP messaging (WS-1 gap fix).

Uses flask_socketio.SocketIOTestClient to exercise the /ws/messaging namespace
directly — no HTTP; real event handlers, real DB, real service calls.

All WebSocket operations (connect, emit, receive) MUST run inside
``with ws_app.app_context()`` because Flask-SocketIO's test client
executes event handlers synchronously in the same thread; the app context
keeps the SQLAlchemy session alive so handlers can see committed rows.

Covered:
  - connect with valid token → is_connected True
  - connect with invalid/missing token → rejected (not connected)
  - on_direct: send direct message → recipient's room receives 'message' event
  - on_group: send group message → community room receives 'message' event
  - on_receipt: acknowledge delivery/read → receipt status advanced in DB
  - disconnect: _sid_user_map cleaned up (no stale entry)
  - token expiry mid-connection: expired token → disconnect on next event
"""
import uuid
import hashlib
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta

import pytest

from app import create_app
from app.extensions import db as _db, socketio
from app.services.auth_service import AuthService
from app.services.community_service import CommunityService
from app.models.user import Session
from app.models.messaging import MessageReceipt


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Session-scoped app (separate from API_tests/conftest.py app)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture(scope="module")
def ws_app():
    """Dedicated app for WebSocket tests — module-scoped to share DB data."""
    application = create_app("testing")
    with application.app_context():
        _db.create_all()
        yield application
        _db.drop_all()


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helpers
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _new_user(app, role="Member"):
    """Register + login a fresh user; return (user_id_str, raw_token)."""
    suffix = uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]
    username = f"ws_{suffix}"
    with app.app_context():
        AuthService.register(username, "WsTestPass12!", role=role)
        result = AuthService.login(username, "WsTestPass12!")
    return result["user_id"], result["token"]


def _connect(app, token):
    """Return a connected SocketIOTestClient for /ws/messaging.
    Must be called within an active app_context."""
    return socketio.test_client(
        app, namespace="/ws/messaging", auth={"token": token}
    )


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Connection / authentication
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestWsConnect:

    def test_connect_valid_token(self, ws_app):
        """A valid Bearer token results in a successful WebSocket connection."""
        uid_a, tok_a = _new_user(ws_app)
        with ws_app.app_context():
            ws = _connect(ws_app, tok_a)
            assert ws.is_connected("/ws/messaging")
            ws.disconnect("/ws/messaging")

    def test_connect_invalid_token_rejected(self, ws_app):
        """An invalid token causes the server to disconnect immediately."""
        with ws_app.app_context():
            ws = _connect(ws_app, "notarealtoken123456")
            assert not ws.is_connected("/ws/messaging")

    def test_connect_empty_token_rejected(self, ws_app):
        """An empty token causes the server to disconnect immediately."""
        with ws_app.app_context():
            ws = _connect(ws_app, "")
            assert not ws.is_connected("/ws/messaging")

    def test_disconnect_cleans_up(self, ws_app):
        """After explicit disconnect, _sid_user_map no longer contains the sid."""
        from app import websocket as ws_mod
        uid_a, tok_a = _new_user(ws_app)
        with ws_app.app_context():
            ws = _connect(ws_app, tok_a)
            assert ws.is_connected("/ws/messaging")
            sid = ws._sid
            assert sid in ws_mod._sid_user_map
            ws.disconnect("/ws/messaging")
            assert sid not in ws_mod._sid_user_map


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Direct messaging (on_direct)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestWsDirect:

    def test_direct_message_emitted_to_recipient_room(self, ws_app):
        """Sending 'direct' from user A delivers a 'message' event to user B's socket."""
        uid_a, tok_a = _new_user(ws_app)
        uid_b, tok_b = _new_user(ws_app)

        with ws_app.app_context():
            ws_a = _connect(ws_app, tok_a)
            ws_b = _connect(ws_app, tok_b)

            ws_a.emit(
                "direct",
                {"type": "text", "recipient_id": uid_b, "body": "Hello from A"},
                namespace="/ws/messaging",
            )

            received_b = ws_b.get_received("/ws/messaging")
            assert any(evt["name"] == "message" for evt in received_b), \
                f"Recipient did not receive 'message' event; got: {received_b}"

            ws_a.disconnect("/ws/messaging")
            ws_b.disconnect("/ws/messaging")

    def test_direct_message_persisted_as_receipt(self, ws_app):
        """After a 'direct' event, a MessageReceipt row is written with status='sent'."""
        uid_a, tok_a = _new_user(ws_app)
        uid_b, tok_b = _new_user(ws_app)

        with ws_app.app_context():
            ws_a = _connect(ws_app, tok_a)

            ws_a.emit(
                "direct",
                {"type": "text", "recipient_id": uid_b, "body": "Persist check"},
                namespace="/ws/messaging",
            )

            receipts = MessageReceipt.query.filter_by(recipient_id=uid_b).all()
            assert len(receipts) >= 1
            assert receipts[-1].status == "sent"

            ws_a.disconnect("/ws/messaging")

    def test_direct_message_body_in_event(self, ws_app):
        """The emitted 'message' event payload contains the sent body and sender_id."""
        uid_a, tok_a = _new_user(ws_app)
        uid_b, tok_b = _new_user(ws_app)

        with ws_app.app_context():
            ws_b = _connect(ws_app, tok_b)
            ws_a = _connect(ws_app, tok_a)

            ws_a.emit(
                "direct",
                {"type": "text", "recipient_id": uid_b, "body": "unique_body_xyz"},
                namespace="/ws/messaging",
            )

            events = ws_b.get_received("/ws/messaging")
            message_events = [e for e in events if e["name"] == "message"]
            assert message_events, "No 'message' event received"
            payload = message_events[-1]["args"]
            if isinstance(payload, list):
                payload = payload[0]
            assert payload.get("body") == "unique_body_xyz"
            assert payload.get("sender_id") == uid_a

            ws_a.disconnect("/ws/messaging")
            ws_b.disconnect("/ws/messaging")


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Group messaging (on_group)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestWsGroup:

    def test_group_message_delivered_to_community_room(self, ws_app):
        """Sending 'group' emits a 'message' event to the community room.
        A member connected before the send receives the event."""
        uid_admin, tok_admin = _new_user(ws_app, role="Administrator")
        uid_member, tok_member = _new_user(ws_app)

        with ws_app.app_context():
            comm = CommunityService.create({
                "name": f"WS Comm {uuid.uuid4().hex[:6]}",
                "address_line1": "1 Test St",
                "city": "Austin", "state": "TX", "zip": "78701",
            })
            comm_id = str(comm.community_id)

            from app.models.user import User
            member_obj = _db.session.get(User, uid_member)
            CommunityService.join_community(comm_id, member_obj)

            ws_admin = _connect(ws_app, tok_admin)
            ws_member = _connect(ws_app, tok_member)

            ws_admin.emit(
                "group",
                {"type": "text", "group_id": comm_id, "body": "Group announcement"},
                namespace="/ws/messaging",
            )

            events = ws_member.get_received("/ws/messaging")
            message_events = [e for e in events if e["name"] == "message"]
            assert message_events, \
                f"Community member did not receive group 'message'; got: {events}"

            ws_admin.disconnect("/ws/messaging")
            ws_member.disconnect("/ws/messaging")


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Receipt acknowledgement (on_receipt)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestWsReceipt:

    def test_receipt_delivered_via_ws(self, ws_app):
        """Sending 'receipt' with status='delivered' advances the MessageReceipt in DB."""
        uid_a, tok_a = _new_user(ws_app)
        uid_b, tok_b = _new_user(ws_app)

        with ws_app.app_context():
            ws_a = _connect(ws_app, tok_a)
            ws_b = _connect(ws_app, tok_b)

            # A sends a message to B
            ws_a.emit(
                "direct",
                {"type": "text", "recipient_id": uid_b, "body": "Receipt test"},
                namespace="/ws/messaging",
            )

            receipt = MessageReceipt.query.filter_by(recipient_id=uid_b).order_by(
                MessageReceipt.updated_at.desc()
            ).first()
            assert receipt is not None
            msg_id = str(receipt.message_id)
            rid = receipt.receipt_id

            # B acknowledges delivery via WebSocket
            ws_b.emit(
                "receipt",
                {"message_id": msg_id, "status": "delivered"},
                namespace="/ws/messaging",
            )

            _db.session.expire(receipt)
            updated = _db.session.get(MessageReceipt, rid)
            assert updated.status == "delivered"

            ws_a.disconnect("/ws/messaging")
            ws_b.disconnect("/ws/messaging")

    def test_receipt_read_via_ws(self, ws_app):
        """Sending 'receipt' with status='read' after 'delivered' advances to read."""
        uid_a, tok_a = _new_user(ws_app)
        uid_b, tok_b = _new_user(ws_app)

        with ws_app.app_context():
            ws_a = _connect(ws_app, tok_a)
            ws_b = _connect(ws_app, tok_b)

            ws_a.emit(
                "direct",
                {"type": "text", "recipient_id": uid_b, "body": "Read test"},
                namespace="/ws/messaging",
            )

            receipt = MessageReceipt.query.filter_by(recipient_id=uid_b).order_by(
                MessageReceipt.updated_at.desc()
            ).first()
            msg_id = str(receipt.message_id)
            rid = receipt.receipt_id

            # Advance to delivered then read
            ws_b.emit("receipt", {"message_id": msg_id, "status": "delivered"}, namespace="/ws/messaging")
            ws_b.emit("receipt", {"message_id": msg_id, "status": "read"}, namespace="/ws/messaging")

            _db.session.expire(receipt)
            updated = _db.session.get(MessageReceipt, rid)
            assert updated.status == "read"

            ws_a.disconnect("/ws/messaging")
            ws_b.disconnect("/ws/messaging")


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Token expiry mid-connection (RISK-003)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestWsTokenExpiry:

    def test_expired_token_disconnects_on_next_event(self, ws_app):
        """A connection whose session has expired is disconnected on the next event."""
        uid_a, tok_a = _new_user(ws_app)
        uid_b, tok_b = _new_user(ws_app)

        with ws_app.app_context():
            ws_a = _connect(ws_app, tok_a)
            assert ws_a.is_connected("/ws/messaging")

            # Artificially expire the session in DB
            h = hashlib.sha256(tok_a.encode()).hexdigest()
            session = _db.session.get(Session, h)
            session.expires_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(seconds=1)
            _db.session.commit()

            # Next event should trigger re-validation, find expired session, disconnect
            ws_a.emit(
                "direct",
                {"type": "text", "recipient_id": uid_b, "body": "Should be rejected"},
                namespace="/ws/messaging",
            )

            # After the event, the client should be disconnected
            assert not ws_a.is_connected("/ws/messaging")
"}

function.arguments:
{"file_path": "C:\TASK-105\repo\API_tests\test_api_websocket.py", "content": """"
API functional tests for WebSocket/STOMP messaging (WS-1 gap fix).

Uses flask_socketio.SocketIOTestClient to exercise the /ws/messaging namespace
directly — no HTTP; real event handlers, real DB, real service calls.

All WebSocket operations (connect, emit, receive) MUST run inside
``with ws_app.app_context()`` because Flask-SocketIO's test client
executes event handlers synchronously in the same thread; the app context
keeps the SQLAlchemy session alive so handlers can see committed rows.

Covered:
  - connect with valid token → is_connected True
  - connect with invalid/missing token → rejected (not connected)
  - on_direct: send direct message → recipient's room receives 'message' event
  - on_group: send group message → community room receives 'message' event
  - on_receipt: acknowledge delivery/read → receipt status advanced in DB
  - disconnect: _sid_user_map cleaned up (no stale entry)
  - token expiry mid-connection: expired token → disconnect on next event
"""
import uuid
import hashlib
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta

import pytest

from app import create_app
from app.extensions import db as _db, socketio
from app.services.auth_service import AuthService
from app.services.community_service import CommunityService
from app.models.user import Session
from app.models.messaging import MessageReceipt


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Session-scoped app (separate from API_tests/conftest.py app)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture(scope="module")
def ws_app():
    """Dedicated app for WebSocket tests — module-scoped to share DB data."""
    application = create_app("testing")
    with application.app_context():
        _db.create_all()
        yield application
        _db.drop_all()


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helpers
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _new_user(app, role="Member"):
    """Register + login a fresh user; return (user_id_str, raw_token)."""
    suffix = uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]
    username = f"ws_{suffix}"
    with app.app_context():
        AuthService.register(username, "WsTestPass12!", role=role)
        result = AuthService.login(username, "WsTestPass12!")
    return result["user_id"], result["token"]


def _connect(app, token):
    """Return a connected SocketIOTestClient for /ws/messaging.
    Must be called within an active app_context."""
    return socketio.test_client(
        app, namespace="/ws/messaging", auth={"token": token}
    )


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Connection / authentication
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestWsConnect:

    def test_connect_valid_token(self, ws_app):
        """A valid Bearer token results in a successful WebSocket connection."""
        uid_a, tok_a = _new_user(ws_app)
        with ws_app.app_context():
            ws = _connect(ws_app, tok_a)
            assert ws.is_connected("/ws/messaging")
            ws.disconnect("/ws/messaging")

    def test_connect_invalid_token_rejected(self, ws_app):
        """An invalid token causes the server to disconnect immediately."""
        with ws_app.app_context():
            ws = _connect(ws_app, "notarealtoken123456")
            assert not ws.is_connected("/ws/messaging")

    def test_connect_empty_token_rejected(self, ws_app):
        """An empty token causes the server to disconnect immediately."""
        with ws_app.app_context():
            ws = _connect(ws_app, "")
            assert not ws.is_connected("/ws/messaging")

    def test_disconnect_cleans_up(self, ws_app):
        """After explicit disconnect, _sid_user_map no longer contains the sid."""
        from app import websocket as ws_mod
        uid_a, tok_a = _new_user(ws_app)
        with ws_app.app_context():
            ws = _connect(ws_app, tok_a)
            assert ws.is_connected("/ws/messaging")
            sid = ws._sid
            assert sid in ws_mod._sid_user_map
            ws.disconnect("/ws/messaging")
            assert sid not in ws_mod._sid_user_map


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Direct messaging (on_direct)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestWsDirect:

    def test_direct_message_emitted_to_recipient_room(self, ws_app):
        """Sending 'direct' from user A delivers a 'message' event to user B's socket."""
        uid_a, tok_a = _new_user(ws_app)
        uid_b, tok_b = _new_user(ws_app)

        with ws_app.app_context():
            ws_a = _connect(ws_app, tok_a)
            ws_b = _connect(ws_app, tok_b)

            ws_a.emit(
                "direct",
                {"type": "text", "recipient_id": uid_b, "body": "Hello from A"},
                namespace="/ws/messaging",
            )

            received_b = ws_b.get_received("/ws/messaging")
            assert any(evt["name"] == "message" for evt in received_b), \
                f"Recipient did not receive 'message' event; got: {received_b}"

            ws_a.disconnect("/ws/messaging")
            ws_b.disconnect("/ws/messaging")

    def test_direct_message_persisted_as_receipt(self, ws_app):
        """After a 'direct' event, a MessageReceipt row is written with status='sent'."""
        uid_a, tok_a = _new_user(ws_app)
        uid_b, tok_b = _new_user(ws_app)

        with ws_app.app_context():
            ws_a = _connect(ws_app, tok_a)

            ws_a.emit(
                "direct",
                {"type": "text", "recipient_id": uid_b, "body": "Persist check"},
                namespace="/ws/messaging",
            )

            receipts = MessageReceipt.query.filter_by(recipient_id=uid_b).all()
            assert len(receipts) >= 1
            assert receipts[-1].status == "sent"

            ws_a.disconnect("/ws/messaging")

    def test_direct_message_body_in_event(self, ws_app):
        """The emitted 'message' event payload contains the sent body and sender_id."""
        uid_a, tok_a = _new_user(ws_app)
        uid_b, tok_b = _new_user(ws_app)

        with ws_app.app_context():
            ws_b = _connect(ws_app, tok_b)
            ws_a = _connect(ws_app, tok_a)

            ws_a.emit(
                "direct",
                {"type": "text", "recipient_id": uid_b, "body": "unique_body_xyz"},
                namespace="/ws/messaging",
            )

            events = ws_b.get_received("/ws/messaging")
            message_events = [e for e in events if e["name"] == "message"]
            assert message_events, "No 'message' event received"
            payload = message_events[-1]["args"]
            if isinstance(payload, list):
                payload = payload[0]
            assert payload.get("body") == "unique_body_xyz"
            assert payload.get("sender_id") == uid_a

            ws_a.disconnect("/ws/messaging")
            ws_b.disconnect("/ws/messaging")


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Group messaging (on_group)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestWsGroup:

    def test_group_message_delivered_to_community_room(self, ws_app):
        """Sending 'group' emits a 'message' event to the community room.
        A member connected before the send receives the event."""
        uid_admin, tok_admin = _new_user(ws_app, role="Administrator")
        uid_member, tok_member = _new_user(ws_app)

        with ws_app.app_context():
            comm = CommunityService.create({
                "name": f"WS Comm {uuid.uuid4().hex[:6]}",
                "address_line1": "1 Test St",
                "city": "Austin", "state": "TX", "zip": "78701",
            })
            comm_id = str(comm.community_id)

            from app.models.user import User
            member_obj = _db.session.get(User, uid_member)
            CommunityService.join_community(comm_id, member_obj)

            ws_admin = _connect(ws_app, tok_admin)
            ws_member = _connect(ws_app, tok_member)

            ws_admin.emit(
                "group",
                {"type": "text", "group_id": comm_id, "body": "Group announcement"},
                namespace="/ws/messaging",
            )

            events = ws_member.get_received("/ws/messaging")
            message_events = [e for e in events if e["name"] == "message"]
            assert message_events, \
                f"Community member did not receive group 'message'; got: {events}"

            ws_admin.disconnect("/ws/messaging")
            ws_member.disconnect("/ws/messaging")


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Receipt acknowledgement (on_receipt)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestWsReceipt:

    def test_receipt_delivered_via_ws(self, ws_app):
        """Sending 'receipt' with status='delivered' advances the MessageReceipt in DB."""
        uid_a, tok_a = _new_user(ws_app)
        uid_b, tok_b = _new_user(ws_app)

        with ws_app.app_context():
            ws_a = _connect(ws_app, tok_a)
            ws_b = _connect(ws_app, tok_b)

            # A sends a message to B
            ws_a.emit(
                "direct",
                {"type": "text", "recipient_id": uid_b, "body": "Receipt test"},
                namespace="/ws/messaging",
            )

            receipt = MessageReceipt.query.filter_by(recipient_id=uid_b).order_by(
                MessageReceipt.updated_at.desc()
            ).first()
            assert receipt is not None
            msg_id = str(receipt.message_id)
            rid = receipt.receipt_id

            # B acknowledges delivery via WebSocket
            ws_b.emit(
                "receipt",
                {"message_id": msg_id, "status": "delivered"},
                namespace="/ws/messaging",
            )

            _db.session.expire(receipt)
            updated = _db.session.get(MessageReceipt, rid)
            assert updated.status == "delivered"

            ws_a.disconnect("/ws/messaging")
            ws_b.disconnect("/ws/messaging")

    def test_receipt_read_via_ws(self, ws_app):
        """Sending 'receipt' with status='read' after 'delivered' advances to read."""
        uid_a, tok_a = _new_user(ws_app)
        uid_b, tok_b = _new_user(ws_app)

        with ws_app.app_context():
            ws_a = _connect(ws_app, tok_a)
            ws_b = _connect(ws_app, tok_b)

            ws_a.emit(
                "direct",
                {"type": "text", "recipient_id": uid_b, "body": "Read test"},
                namespace="/ws/messaging",
            )

            receipt = MessageReceipt.query.filter_by(recipient_id=uid_b).order_by(
                MessageReceipt.updated_at.desc()
            ).first()
            msg_id = str(receipt.message_id)
            rid = receipt.receipt_id

            # Advance to delivered then read
            ws_b.emit("receipt", {"message_id": msg_id, "status": "delivered"}, namespace="/ws/messaging")
            ws_b.emit("receipt", {"message_id": msg_id, "status": "read"}, namespace="/ws/messaging")

            _db.session.expire(receipt)
            updated = _db.session.get(MessageReceipt, rid)
            assert updated.status == "read"

            ws_a.disconnect("/ws/messaging")
            ws_b.disconnect("/ws/messaging")


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Token expiry mid-connection (RISK-003)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestWsTokenExpiry:

    def test_expired_token_disconnects_on_next_event(self, ws_app):
        """A connection whose session has expired is disconnected on the next event."""
        uid_a, tok_a = _new_user(ws_app)
        uid_b, tok_b = _new_user(ws_app)

        with ws_app.app_context():
            ws_a = _connect(ws_app, tok_a)
            assert ws_a.is_connected("/ws/messaging")

            # Artificially expire the session in DB
            h = hashlib.sha256(tok_a.encode()).hexdigest()
            session = _db.session.get(Session, h)
            session.expires_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(seconds=1)
            _db.session.commit()

            # Next event should trigger re-validation, find expired session, disconnect
            ws_a.emit(
                "direct",
                {"type": "text", "recipient_id": uid_b, "body": "Should be rejected"},
                namespace="/ws/messaging",
            )

            # After the event, the client should be disconnected
            assert not ws_a.is_connected("/ws/messaging")
"}

1	import os
2	from flask import Flask
3	from sqlalchemy import event
4	from sqlalchemy.engine import Engine
5	import sqlite3
6	
7	from .config import config_map, Config
8	from .extensions import db, migrate, socketio, scheduler
9	from .errors import register_error_handlers
10	from .middleware import register_middleware
11	from .crypto import init_fernet
12	
13	
14	def create_app(config_name: str | None = None) -> Flask:
15	    if config_name is None:
16	        config_name = os.environ.get("FLASK_ENV", "development")
17	
18	    app = Flask(__name__, instance_relative_config=False)
19	    app.config.from_object(config_map.get(config_name, Config))
20	
21	    # --- Fernet encryption key ---
22	    init_fernet(app.config["FERNET_KEY_PATH"])
23	
24	    # --- Extensions ---
25	    db.init_app(app)
26	    migrate.init_app(app, db)
27	    async_mode = app.config.get("SOCKETIO_ASYNC_MODE", "threading")
28	    socketio.init_app(app, async_mode=async_mode)
29	
30	    # Enable WAL mode for SQLite
31	    @event.listens_for(Engine, "connect")
32	    def set_sqlite_pragma(dbapi_conn, connection_record):
33	        if isinstance(dbapi_conn, sqlite3.Connection):
34	            cursor = dbapi_conn.cursor()
35	            cursor.execute("PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL")
36	            cursor.execute("PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON")
37	            cursor.close()
38	
39	    # --- Middleware ---
40	    register_middleware(app)
41	
42	    # --- Blueprints ---
43	    _register_blueprints(app)
44	
45	    # --- Error handlers ---
46	    register_error_handlers(app)
47	
48	    # --- Background jobs ---
49	    if app.config.get("JOBS_ENABLED"):
50	        with app.app_context():
51	            _start_scheduler(app)
52	
53	    return app
54	
55	
56	def _register_blueprints(app: Flask) -> None:
57	    from .routes.health import health_bp
58	    from .routes.auth import auth_bp
59	    from .routes.users import users_bp
60	    from .routes.communities import communities_bp
61	    from .routes.commission import commission_bp
62	    from .routes.catalog import catalog_bp
63	    from .routes.search import search_bp
64	    from .routes.inventory import inventory_bp
65	    from .routes.messaging import messaging_bp
66	    from .routes.content import content_bp
67	    from .routes.templates import templates_bp
68	    from .routes.admin import admin_bp
69	
70	    # Health probes (no version prefix — standard convention)
71	    app.register_blueprint(health_bp)
72	
73	    v1 = "/api/v1"
74	    app.register_blueprint(auth_bp, url_prefix=v1)
75	    app.register_blueprint(users_bp, url_prefix=v1)
76	    app.register_blueprint(communities_bp, url_prefix=v1)
77	    app.register_blueprint(commission_bp, url_prefix=v1)
78	    app.register_blueprint(catalog_bp, url_prefix=v1)
79	    app.register_blueprint(search_bp, url_prefix=v1)
80	    app.register_blueprint(inventory_bp, url_prefix=v1)
81	    app.register_blueprint(messaging_bp, url_prefix=v1)
82	    app.register_blueprint(content_bp, url_prefix=v1)
83	    app.register_blueprint(templates_bp, url_prefix=v1)
84	    app.register_blueprint(admin_bp, url_prefix=v1)
85	
86	
87	def _start_scheduler(app: Flask) -> None:
88	    from .jobs import register_jobs
89	    register_jobs(scheduler, app)
